### Mensaje:
Let me now assess the situation more carefully.\n\nThis is a delivery app for \"repartidores\" (delivery drivers). Offline capability is critical for them because:\n1. They drive around and may lose connectivity\n2. They need to confirm deliveries even without internet\n3. The app explicitly has `offline-patterns.md` rules that dictate this behavior\n\nHowever, let me think about whether the FULL implementation is truly necessary given:\n- The app already has mock data fallback\n- The partial cache in `usePedidosDelDia` works for reads\n- The mutations (confirm/cancel) are the most critical part for offline\n\nLet me look at what screens consume these hooks to understand the real impact.\n\nLet me also check existing tests to understand what we need.\n\nActually, looking at the code more carefully:\n\n1. **TAREA 4.4** ÔÇö The offline integration in `usePedidos.ts`:\n   - `useNetworkStatus` is imported but not used\n   - `useOfflineStore` is imported but not used\n   - The cache-only fallback for `usePedidosDelDia` partially works (always tries API first)\n   - Mutations don't queue offline\n   \n   The missing piece is: use `isConnected` from `useNetworkStatus` to decide whether to hit the API or go straight to cache/queue.\n\n2. **TAREA 4.5** ÔÇö ConnectivityBanner:\n   - Component is fully done, just needs to be placed in screens\n\n3. **TAREA 4.6** ÔÇö useOfflineSync.ts + layout:\n   - The hook needs to be created, which involves:\n     - Processing the queue (FIFO)\n     - Retry logic (max 3 retries)\n     - Listening to NetInfo for connectivity restoration\n     - Persisting queue to AsyncStorage\n   - Layout needs to trigger sync on mount and listen for connectivity changes\n\n4. **TAREA 5** ÔÇö Tests:\n   - 4 new test files minimum\n\nNow, the user is asking: **\"Son necesarias? requieren de mucho esfuerzo?\"**\n\nLet me evaluate necessity:\n\n**For a delivery app where drivers are on the field:**\n- Ô£à Offline reads (seeing pedidos when offline) ÔÇö HIGH NECESSITY. A driver must see their delivery list.\n- Ô£à Offline mutations (confirming/cancelling deliveries offline) ÔÇö HIGH NECESSITY. A driver must be able to mark deliveries.\n- Ô£à Connectivity indicator (knowing you're offline) ÔÇö MEDIUM NECESSITY. Good UX.\n- Ô£à Sync when back online ÔÇö HIGH NECESSITY. Otherwise offline actions are lost.\n\nSo these tasks are actually quite necessary for the app to function as a real delivery tool.\n\nLet me think about effort:\n\n**Simplified approach vs Full approach:**\n\nThe current offline patterns doc is quite complex. We could simplify:\n\n**Simplified MVP approach (less effort):**\n1. **TAREA 4.4** ÔÇö Minimal: Use `isConnected` in `usePedidosDelDia` to skip API when offline. For mutations, use optimistic updates (TanStack Query already does this with `onMutate`). Queue mutations for later sync.\n2. **TAREA 4.5** ÔÇö Add `<ConnectivityBanner>` to the main layouts, not every screen.\n3. **TAREA 4.6** ÔÇö Create a simpler `useOfflineSync` that just processes the queue when connectivity comes back. Persist queue to AsyncStorage. Add to `_layout.tsx`.\n4. **TAREA 5** ÔÇö Tests: 4 new test files + update existing test.\n\nLet me think about a realistic effort estimate in terms of hours:\n\n**Simplified approach:**\n- TAREA 4.4: ~1.5-2 hours\n- TAREA 4.5: ~0.5 hours\n- TAREA 4.6: ~2-3 hours\n- TAREA 5: ~1.5-2 hours\nTotal: ~6-8 hours\n\n**Full approach (as per docs):**\n- TAREA 4.4: ~3 hours\n- TAREA 4.5: ~1 hour\n- TAREA 4.6: ~4 hours\n- TAREA 5: ~3 hours\nTotal: ~11 hours\n\nI'll present both options and let the user decide. Let me also think about whether the full offline queue persistence is really needed or if just optimistic updates + retry on reconnect would suffice.\n\nActually, looking at the codebase rules more carefully:\n\nFrom `offline-patterns.md`:\n- \"NO bloquear la UI cuando offline\"\n- \"NO perder datos: si falla la sincronizaci├│n, mantener la cola hasta reintentar\"\n- \"Sincronizar alµüóÕñìar conexi├│n autom├íticamente\"\n- \"La cola offline debe tener un l├¡mite m├íximo de reintentos (3) antes de marcar como fallido permanente\"\n- \"No cachear datos sensibles (contrase├▒as, tokens) en AsyncStorage\"\n\nThese are clear requirements. The full approach is what the rules dictate. But let me offer both a \"completo\" and \"m├¡nimo viable\" option.\n\nLet me also check what existing tests look like for reference.

### Mensaje:
Perfecto, ya tengo toda la informaci├│n. Aqu├¡ va mi an├ílisis completo:\n\n---\n\n## 1. ┬┐Son necesarias estas tareas?\n\n**S├¡, absolutamente necesarias.** Esta es una app para **repartidores en ruta** que estar├ín en movimiento constante. Sin soporte offline, la app es b├ísicamente inusable cuando pierden se├▒al (algo frecuente en calles, s├│tanos, zonas rurales).\n\n### Impacto por tarea:\n\n| Tarea | ┬┐Qu├® pasa si no se hace? | Prioridad |\n|---|---|---|\n| **4.4** ÔÇö Offline en `usePedidos.ts` | El repartidor **no puede ver su lista de pedidos** sin internet, ni confirmar entregas. La app se vuelve in├║til en campo. | ­ƒö┤ **Cr├¡tica** |\n| **4.5** ÔÇö ConnectivityBanner | El repartidor **no sabe que est├í offline**. Puede hacer acciones pensando que se guardaron y perderlas. | ­ƒƒí **Alta** (UX) |\n| **4.6** ÔÇö useOfflineSync + layout | Las confirmaciones/cancelaciones hechas offline **se pierden al cerrar la app**. Nunca se sincronizan. | ­ƒö┤ **Cr├¡tica** |\n| **5** ÔÇö Tests faltantes | Sin tests, cualquier cambio futuro puede romper el offline **sin que nadie se d├® cuenta**. | ­ƒƒí **Alta** (calidad) |\n\n---\n\n## 2. ┬┐Requieren mucho esfuerzo?\n\n**No, es esfuerzo moderado.** Analizando el c├│digo existente, la mayor parte de la infraestructura ya est├í creada:\n\n| Componente | Estado actual | Esfuerzo restante |\n|---|---|---|\n| `ConnectivityBanner` Ô£à | 100% listo, s├│lo hay que **importarlo** en screens | **10 min** |\n| `offlineStore` Ô£à | 90% listo, falta **persistencia en AsyncStorage** | **20 min** |\n| `useNetworkStatus` Ô£à | 100% listo, s├│lo hay que **invocarlo** | **5 min** |\n| `AsyncStorage` cache | Parcial en `usePedidosDelDia` | **15 min** extenderlo |\n| `useOfflineSync.ts` ÔØî | **No existe**, hay que crearlo | **~2-3h** |\n| `_layout.tsx` | 50% ÔÇö hay que **agregar inicializaci├│n** de offline sync | **30 min** |\n| Tests ÔØî | 4 archivos nuevos + actualizar 1 existente | **~2h** |\n\n### Estimaci├│n total: **~6-8 horas** (un d├¡a y medio aprox.)\n\n---\n\n## 3. Plan de implementaci├│n detallado\n\n### Opci├│n A: Completa (recomendada)\n\nSigue todas las reglas de `offline-patterns.md` al pie de la letra.\n\n### Opci├│n B: MVP simplificada (menos esfuerzo, 4-5h)\n\nSacrifica la persistencia de la cola en AsyncStorage y el `persistQueryClient`, pero cubre lo esencial.\n\nTe recomiendo la **Opci├│n A** porque:\n- Es una app para repartidores que estar├ín offline **largos periodos**\n- Sin persistencia, si la app se cierra se pierden confirmaciones de entrega\n- El costo adicional es solo ~1-2h m├ís que la opci├│n B\n\n---\n\n### Plan detallado (Opci├│n A ÔÇö Completa)\n\n#### TAREA 4.4 ÔÇö Integrar offline en `usePedidos.ts`\n\n**Archivos a modificar:**\n- `features/pedidos/hooks/usePedidos.ts`\n\n**Cambios concretos:**\n\n| # | Cambio | C├│digo a modificar |\n|---|---|---|\n| 1 | Invocar `useNetworkStatus()` al inicio y usarlo en `usePedidosDelDia` | Si `!isConnected` ÔåÆ ir directo a AsyncStorage cache, sin intentar API |\n| 2 | Extender cache a `usePedidoDetalle` | Guardar/leer en AsyncStorage con key `@supplycycle/pedido_${id}` |\n| 3 | En `useConfirmarEntrega` y `useCancelarPedido`: si `!isConnected`, en lugar de llamar API ÔåÆ `offlineStore.addToQueue()` con actualizaci├│n optimista del query cache | Agregar `onMutate` optimista + condici├│n offline |\n| 4 | Marcar visualmente pedidos con cambios pendientes de sync | Agregar flag `pendingSync` al query data |\n\n**Esfuerzo**: ~1.5h\n\n---\n\n#### TAREA 4.5 ÔÇö Agregar ConnectivityBanner a screens\n\n**Archivos a modificar:**\n\n| Archivo | Cambio |\n|---|---|\n| `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx` | Agregar `<ConnectivityBanner>` dentro del layout de tabs para que se vea en TODAS las pantallas principales |\n| Opcional: `app/(tabs)/pedidos/_layout.tsx` | Si se quiere visible tambi├®n en stacks anidados |\n\n**C├│digo a agregar en `(tabs)/_layout.tsx`:**\n```tsx\nimport { useNetworkStatus } from '@/hooks/useNetworkStatus';\nimport { useOfflineStore } from '@/stores/offlineStore';\nimport { ConnectivityBanner } from '@/components/ui';\n\n// Dentro del componente:\nconst { isConnected } = useNetworkStatus();\nconst queueLength = useOfflineStore((s) => s.queue.length);\n\n// En el JSX, antes del Tabs:\n<>\n  <ConnectivityBanner isConnected={isConnected} hasPendingSync={queueLength > 0} />\n  <Tabs>...</Tabs>\n</>\n```\n\n**Esfuerzo**: ~30 min\n\n---\n\n#### TAREA 4.6 ÔÇö Crear `useOfflineSync.ts` + layout ra├¡z\n\n**Archivos a crear:**\n- `hooks/useOfflineSync.ts` ÔÇö Nuevo\n\n**Archivos a modificar:**\n- `stores/offlineStore.ts` ÔÇö Agregar persistencia en AsyncStorage\n- `app/_layout.tsx` ÔÇö Inicializar offline sync\n- OPCIONAL: `services/api.ts` ÔÇö Para interceptor de reconexi├│n\n\n**Estructura de `useOfflineSync.ts`:**\n\n```ts\n// hooks/useOfflineSync.ts\nexport default function useOfflineSync() {\n  // 1. Escuchar cambios de conectividad con NetInfo.addEventListener\n  // 2. AlµüóÕñìar conexi├│n (isConnected pasa de false ÔåÆ true):\n  //    a. Procesar cola FIFO desde offlineStore\n  //    b. Para cada mutaci├│n: ejecutar mutationFn con payload\n  //    c. Si ├®xito ÔåÆ removeFromQueue\n  //    d. Si error ÔåÆ incrementRetry. Si retryCount >= 3 ÔåÆ marcar fallido permanente\n  // 3. Mantener flag isSyncing para UI\n}\n```\n\n**Cambios en `offlineStore.ts`:**\n\n```ts\n// Agregar:\n// - persistMiddleware de Zustand o manual load/save a AsyncStorage\n// - Evento on queue change ÔåÆ guardar en AsyncStorage\n// - Al inicializar store, cargar desde AsyncStorage\n```\n\n**Cambios en `app/_layout.tsx`:**\n\n```tsx\nimport useOfflineSync from '@/hooks/useOfflineSync';\n\n// Dentro de RootLayout:\nuseOfflineSync(); // Inicializa el sync autom├ítico\n```\n\n**Esfuerzo**: ~3h\n- Hook: 1.5h\n- Persistencia en store: 0.5h\n- Layout: 0.5h\n- Manejo de errores y l├¡mite de reintentos: 0.5h\n\n---\n\n#### TAREA 5 ÔÇö Tests faltantes\n\n**Archivos a crear (4 nuevos):**\n\n| Archivo | Qu├® probar | Esfuerzo |\n|---|---|---|\n| `hooks/__tests__/useNetworkStatus.test.ts` | Hook devuelve isConnected, cambios de estado, cleanup | ~30 min |\n| `components/ui/__tests__/connectivity-banner.test.tsx` | Render condicional: online sin pendientes ÔåÆ null; offline ÔåÆ muestra banner; pending sync ÔåÆ muestra mensaje | ~30 min |\n| `stores/__tests__/offlineStore.test.ts` | addToQueue, removeFromQueue, incrementRetry, clearQueue, max retry | ~30 min |\n| `stores/__tests__/uiStore.test.ts` | isOffline, setOffline | ~15 min |\n\n**Archivos a modificar (1):**\n\n| Archivo | Cambio | Esfuerzo |\n|---|---|---|\n| `features/pedidos/hooks/__tests__/usePedidos.test.ts` | Agregar tests para: comportamiento offline en usePedidosDelDia (leer de cache), mutaciones offline (addToQueue), cacheo de detalle | ~45 min |\n\n**Esfuerzo total**: ~2.5h\n\n---\n\n## 4. Resumen de esfuerzo\n\n| Tarea | Archivos nuevos | Archivos modificados | Esfuerzo |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| **4.4** ÔÇö Offline en usePedidos.ts | 0 | 1 | ~1.5h |\n| **4.5** ÔÇö ConnectivityBanner | 0 | 1 (o 2) | ~0.5h |\n| **4.6** ÔÇö useOfflineSync + layout | 1 | 2 | ~3h |\n| **5** ÔÇö Tests | 4 | 1 | ~2.5h |\n| **Total** | **5 archivos nuevos** | **~5 archivos modificados** | **~7-8h** |\n\n**Riesgos identificados:**\n1. La persistencia de la cola en AsyncStorage puede tener race conditions si el usuario hace m├║ltiples acciones offline r├ípidas ÔÇö mitigar con debounce\n2. Si el backend no soporta ciertas mutaciones al sincronizar (ej: confirmar un pedido ya cancelado), hay que manejar conflicto ÔÇö agregar validaci├│n\n3. El test de `usePedidos` requiere mockear `useNetworkStatus` y `useOfflineStore` adem├ís de lo actual\n\n---\n\n┬┐Quieres que:\n- **Opci├│n A (recomendada)**: Siga este plan completo de ~8h\n- **Opci├│n B (simplificada)**: Reduzcamos alcance (por ej: sin persistencia de cola, sin cache de detalle, menos tests)\n- **Priorizar por entregas**: Hacer 4.4 y 4.6 primero (lo cr├¡tico), luego 4.5 y tests despu├®s\n\n┬┐Qu├® prefieres?

### Mensaje:
The user wants to reduce scope. Let me think about what's truly essential vs nice-to-have, and propose a simplified plan.\n\nKey priorities for a delivery app:\n1. MUST: See pedidos when offline (read) ÔÇö otherwise app is useless\n2. MUST: Confirm/cancel deliveries when offline (write) ÔÇö otherwise app is useless\n3. MUST: Sync when back online ÔÇö otherwise data is lost\n4. SHOULD: Connectivity indicator ÔÇö good UX\n5. SHOULD: Tests ÔÇö code quality\n\nLet me think about what we can simplify:\n\n**TAREA 4.4 ÔÇö Integrar offline en usePedidos.ts**\n- Minimal: Just use `useNetworkStatus` to skip API when offline, fallback to AsyncStorage cache\n- Minimal: In mutations, just do optimistic update + addToQueue, no need for separate cache per pedido\n- Remove: Cache for `usePedidoDetalle` and `useBuscarPedidos` (nice to have but not critical)\n\n**TAREA 4.5 ÔÇö ConnectivityBanner**\n- Minimal: Just add to `(tabs)/_layout.tsx` only, not individual screens\n- This is already very simple (30 min), no need to reduce further\n\n**TAREA 4.6 ÔÇö useOfflineSync + layout**\n- Minimal: Create a simpler `useOfflineSync` that:\n  - Only processes queue when coming back online\n  - No AsyncStorage persistence for queue (if app closes, queue lost ÔÇö but this is acceptable for MVP)\n  - Simple retry (3 attempts) without complex error handling\n  - No `persistQueryClient`\n  - Just listen to NetInfo and process FIFO\n\n**TAREA 5 ÔÇö Tests**\n- Minimal: Only test the really new behaviors\n  - Test ConnectivityBanner (2 scenarios)\n  - Test offlineStore (basic operations)\n  - Skip useNetworkStatus test (it's simple enough)\n  - Update usePedidos test minimally for offline branching\n\nLet me calculate effort for the simplified version:\n\nTAREA 4.4 simplified: ~1h\n- Use `isConnected` to skip API in usePedidosDelDia (15 min)\n- Add offline branching to mutations with addToQueue (30 min)\n- Remove unused imports (5 min)\n\nTAREA 4.5 simplified: ~20 min (already minimal)\n- Add ConnectivityBanner to (tabs)/_layout.tsx\n\nTAREA 4.6 simplified: ~1.5h\n- Create useOfflineSync.ts with basic queue processing\n- Wire into _layout.tsx\n- Skip AsyncStorage persistence for queue\n\nTAREA 5 simplified: ~1h\n- ConnectivityBanner test (20 min)\n- offlineStore test (20 min)\n- Minimal updates to usePedidos.test (20 min)\n\nTotal simplified: ~3.5-4h\n\nThis is much more reasonable. ### Mensaje:
Bien, vamos a reducir dr├ísticamente. Lo divido en **3 entregas** priorizadas, eliminando lo no esencial.\n\n---\n\n## Plan reducido (MVP realista) ~3.5-4h total\n\n### Entrega 1 ÔÇö TAREA 4.4 (esencial) ÔÇö ~1h\n\n**Qu├® hacemos (m├¡nimo indispensable):**\n\n| Cambio | Archivo | Tiempo |\n|---|---|---|\n| Invocar `useNetworkStatus()` y usarlo en `usePedidosDelDia`: si `!isConnected` ÔåÆ leer directo de AsyncStorage, no intentar API | `features/pedidos/hooks/usePedidos.ts` | 15 min |\n| En `useConfirmarEntrega` y `useCancelarPedido`: si `!isConnected` ÔåÆ hacer `addToQueue()` + actualizaci├│n optimista local (sin llamar API) | `features/pedidos/hooks/usePedidos.ts` | 30 min |\n| Eliminar imports muertos (`useOfflineStore` ya se usa, `useNetworkStatus` ahora se usa) | `features/pedidos/hooks/usePedidos.ts` | 5 min |\n\n**Lo que NO hacemos (diferencias con el plan completo):**\n- ÔØî No cacheamos `usePedidoDetalle` ni `useBuscarPedidos` (solo `usePedidosDelDia`)\n- ÔØî No agregamos flag `pendingSync` a los pedidos (se puede hacer despu├®s)\n\n---\n\n### Entrega 2 ÔÇö TAREA 4.6 + 4.5 (integraci├│n) ÔÇö ~2h\n\n#### 4.6 ÔÇö useOfflineSync.ts (versi├│n simplificada)\n\n**Archivo nuevo:** `hooks/useOfflineSync.ts`\n\nL├│gica m├¡nima:\n```ts\nexport default function useOfflineSync() {\n  const { queue, removeFromQueue, incrementRetry, setSyncing } = useOfflineStore();\n  const { isConnected } = useNetworkStatus();\n  const wasConnected = useRef(isConnected);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    // Cuando vuelve la conexi├│n (false ÔåÆ true), procesar cola\n    if (isConnected && !wasConnected.current && queue.length > 0) {\n      processQueue();\n    }\n    wasConnected.current = isConnected;\n  }, [isConnected]);\n\n  async function processQueue() {\n    setSyncing(true);\n    for (const mutation of [...queue]) {\n      if (mutation.retryCount >= 3) {\n        removeFromQueue(mutation.id); // fall├│ permanentemente\n        continue;\n      }\n      try {\n        if (mutation.type === 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA') {\n          await confirmarEntregaRequest(mutation.payload.pedidoId as string);\n        } else if (mutation.type === 'CANCELAR_PEDIDO') {\n          await cancelarPedidoRequest(\n            mutation.payload.pedidoId as string,\n            mutation.payload.motivo as MotivoCancelacion,\n          );\n        }\n        removeFromQueue(mutation.id);\n      } catch {\n        incrementRetry(mutation.id);\n      }\n    }\n    setSyncing(false);\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Modificaciones en `app/_layout.tsx`:**\n```tsx\n// Agregar:\nimport useOfflineSync from '@/hooks/useOfflineSync';\n\n// Dentro de RootLayout():\nuseOfflineSync();\n```\n\n**Lo que NO hacemos:**\n- ÔØî No persistimos la cola en AsyncStorage (si la app se cierra, se pierde ÔÇö aceptable para MVP)\n- ÔØî No configuramos `persistQueryClient`\n- ÔØî No hay interceptor en `api.ts`\n\n#### 4.5 ÔÇö ConnectivityBanner\n\n**Archivo a modificar:** `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx`\n\n```tsx\nimport { ConnectivityBanner } from '@/components/ui';\nimport useNetworkStatus from '@/hooks/useNetworkStatus';\nimport { useOfflineStore } from '@/stores/offlineStore';\n\nexport default function TabLayout() {\n  const { isConnected } = useNetworkStatus();\n  const hasPendingSync = useOfflineStore((s) => s.queue.length > 0);\n\n  return (\n    <>\n      <ConnectivityBanner isConnected={isConnected} hasPendingSync={hasPendingSync} />\n      <Tabs>...</Tabs>\n    </>\n  );\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Entrega 3 ÔÇö TAREA 5 (tests m├¡nimos) ÔÇö ~1h\n\nSolo 2 archivos nuevos + 1 modificaci├│n:\n\n| Archivo | Qu├® prueba | Tiempo |\n|---|---|---|\n| **Nuevo**: `components/ui/__tests__/connectivity-banner.test.tsx` | 2 tests: renderiza offline, renderiza pending sync, no renderiza online sin pendientes | 20 min |\n| **Nuevo**: `stores/__tests__/offlineStore.test.ts` | 3 tests: addToQueue, removeFromQueue, incrementRetry > 3 | 20 min |\n| **Modificar**: `features/pedidos/hooks/__tests__/usePedidos.test.ts` | 2 tests nuevos: queryFn usa cache cuando offline; mutation llama addToQueue | 20 min |\n\n**Lo que NO hacemos:**\n- ÔØî No creamos test de `useNetworkStatus` (es demasiado simple, solo envuelve NetInfo)\n- ÔØî No creamos test de `useOfflineSync` (implica mockear NetInfo + offlineStore + servicios ÔÇö mucho setup para MVP)\n- ÔØî No creamos test de `uiStore` (es trivial)\n\n---\n\n## Resumen final del alcance reducido\n\n| Tarea | Archivos nuevos | Archivos modificados | Esfuerzo |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| **4.4** ÔÇö Offline en usePedidos | 0 | 1 | **~1h** |\n| **4.6** ÔÇö useOfflineSync + layout | 1 (hook) | 1 (_layout.tsx) | **~1.5h** |\n| **4.5** ÔÇö ConnectivityBanner | 0 | 1 (tabs _layout) | **~20 min** |\n| **5** ÔÇö Tests | 2 | 1 | **~1h** |\n| **TOTAL** | **3 archivos nuevos** | **~4 archivos modificados** | **~3.5-4h** |\n\n### ┬┐Qu├® queda fuera (para despu├®s)?\n- Cache offline de detalle de pedido y b├║squeda\n- Persistencia de la cola offline en AsyncStorage\n- Flag visual \"pendiente de sincronizar\" en cada pedido\n- Tests de `useNetworkStatus` y `useOfflineSync`\n- `persistQueryClient` de TanStack Query\n\n---\n\n┬┐Te parece bien este alcance reducido? Ah, quieres el detalle exacto de **qu├® archivos tocar y qu├® c├│digo poner** en cada entrega. Vamos:\n\n---\n\n## ­ƒÜÇ ENTREGA 1 ÔÇö TAREA 4.4 (offline en usePedidos.ts)\n\n**1 archivo a modificar:** `features/pedidos/hooks/usePedidos.ts`\n\n### Cambio 1: Invocar `useNetworkStatus()` al inicio del hook\n\n```ts\n// L├ìNEA 5 ÔÇö el import ya existe Ô£à\nimport useNetworkStatus from '@/hooks/useNetworkStatus';\n\n// La idea es usar un custom hook dentro de useQuery, PERO hay un problema t├®cnico:\n// useNetworkStatus() no se puede llamar dentro de queryFn (viola rules of hooks).\n// SOLUCI├ôN: Leer el estado directamente del store de uiStore que se actualiza desde useNetworkStatus\n\n// En su lugar, usamos un enfoque m├ís simple:\n// Creamos una variable global sincr├│nica desde uiStore\n\nimport { useUIStore } from '@/stores/uiStore';\n\n// Y en cada queryFn/mutationFn:\nconst isOffline = useUIStore.getState().isOffline;\n```\n\n**ESPERA** ÔÇö Revisando el c├│digo existente, `uiStore` tiene `isOffline` y `setOffline`. Pero `useNetworkStatus` no actualiza `uiStore`. La forma m├ís limpia y que **respeta las reglas de hooks** es:\n\n### Soluci├│n final (sin hooks dentro de queryFn):\n\n**En `usePedidos.ts`** ÔÇö reemplazar el `import useNetworkStatus` (l├¡nea 5) por:\n\n```ts\nimport { useUIStore } from '@/stores/uiStore';\nimport { useOfflineStore } from '@/stores/offlineStore'; // ya existe l├¡nea 4 Ô£à\n```\n\n**MODIFICAR `queryFn` de `usePedidosDelDia` (l├¡neas 33-53):**\n\n```ts\nqueryFn: async () => {\n  const isOffline = useUIStore.getState().isOffline; // ÔåÉ leer estado sincr├│nico\n\n  if (isOffline) {\n    // Modo offline: ir directo a cach├®, no tentar API\n    const cached = await AsyncStorage.getItem(PEDIDOS_CACHE_KEY);\n    if (cached) return JSON.parse(cached) as Pedido[];\n    return await mockGetPedidosDelDiaRequest();\n  }\n\n  // Modo online: normal\n  try {\n    const data = await getPedidosDelDiaRequest(getRepartidorId());\n    await AsyncStorage.setItem(PEDIDOS_CACHE_KEY, JSON.stringify(data));\n    return data;\n  } catch {\n    const cached = await AsyncStorage.getItem(PEDIDOS_CACHE_KEY);\n    if (cached) return JSON.parse(cached) as Pedido[];\n    return await mockGetPedidosDelDiaRequest();\n  }\n},\n```\n\n**REEMPLAZAR `useConfirmarEntrega` (l├¡neas 107-121) y `useCancelarPedido` (l├¡neas 123-145):**\n\n```ts\nexport function useConfirmarEntrega() {\n  const queryClient = useQueryClient();\n  const addToQueue = useOfflineStore((s) => s.addToQueue);\n\n  return useMutation({\n    mutationFn: async (pedidoId: string) => {\n      const isOffline = useUIStore.getState().isOffline;\n\n      if (isOffline) {\n        // Encolar para despu├®s\n        addToQueue({\n          type: 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA',\n          payload: { pedidoId },\n        });\n        // Actualizaci├│n optimista local\n        queryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', pedidoId], (old) => {\n          if (old) return { ...old, estado: 'ENTREGADO' as const };\n          return old;\n        });\n        queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedidos'] });\n        queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['reparto'] });\n        return { id: pedidoId, estado: 'ENTREGADO' as const, actualizadoEn: new Date().toISOString() };\n      }\n\n      return await confirmarEntregaRequest(pedidoId);\n    },\n    onSuccess: (data) => {\n      // Solo actualizar cache si no se hizo ya en modo offline\n      if (useUIStore.getState().isOffline) return;\n      queryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', data.id], (old) => {\n        if (old) return { ...old, estado: 'ENTREGADO' as const };\n        return old;\n      });\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedidos'] });\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['reparto'] });\n    },\n  });\n}\n\nexport function useCancelarPedido() {\n  const queryClient = useQueryClient();\n  const addToQueue = useOfflineStore((s) => s.addToQueue);\n\n  return useMutation({\n    mutationFn: async ({ pedidoId, motivo }: { pedidoId: string; motivo: MotivoCancelacion }) => {\n      const isOffline = useUIStore.getState().isOffline;\n\n      if (isOffline) {\n        addToQueue({\n          type: 'CANCELAR_PEDIDO',\n          payload: { pedidoId, motivo },\n        });\n        queryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', pedidoId], (old) => {\n          if (old) return { ...old, estado: 'NO_ENTREGADO' as const, motivoFalla: motivo };\n          return old;\n        });\n        queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedidos'] });\n        queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['reparto'] });\n        return { id: pedidoId, estado: 'NO_ENTREGADO' as const, motivoFalla: motivo, actualizadoEn: new Date().toISOString() };\n      }\n\n      return await cancelarPedidoRequest(pedidoId, motivo);\n    },\n    onSuccess: (data) => {\n      if (useUIStore.getState().isOffline) return;\n      queryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', data.id], (old) => {\n        if (old) return { ...old, estado: 'NO_ENTREGADO' as const, motivoFalla: data.motivoFalla };\n        return old;\n      });\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedidos'] });\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['reparto'] });\n    },\n  });\n}\n```\n\n### Cambio adicional: `hooks/useNetworkStatus.ts` debe actualizar `uiStore`\n\n**Modificar** `hooks/useNetworkStatus.ts` ÔÇö agregar `useUIStore`:\n\n```ts\nimport { useUIStore } from '@/stores/uiStore';\n\n// Dentro del useEffect, despu├®s de setStatus:\nconst setOffline = useUIStore.getState().setOffline;\nuseEffect(() => {\n  const unsubscribe = NetInfo.addEventListener((state: NetInfoState) => {\n    const isConnected = state.isConnected ?? true;\n    setStatus({ isConnected, connectionType: state.type });\n    setOffline(!isConnected); // ÔåÉ sincronizar uiStore\n  });\n  return () => unsubscribe();\n}, []);\n```\n\n---\n\n## ­ƒÜÇ ENTREGA 2 ÔÇö TAREA 4.6 + 4.5 (sync + banner)\n\n### Archivo NUEVO: `hooks/useOfflineSync.ts`\n\n```ts\nimport { useEffect, useRef } from 'react';\nimport NetInfo from '@react-native-community/netinfo';\nimport { useOfflineStore } from '@/stores/offlineStore';\nimport { confirmarEntregaRequest, cancelarPedidoRequest } from '@/features/pedidos/services/pedidoService';\nimport type { MotivoCancelacion } from '@/types';\n\nconst MAX_RETRIES = 3;\n\nexport default function useOfflineSync() {\n  const { queue, removeFromQueue, incrementRetry, setSyncing } = useOfflineStore();\n  const processingRef = useRef(false);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const unsubscribe = NetInfo.addEventListener((state) => {\n      const isConnected = state.isConnected ?? false;\n      if (isConnected && queue.length > 0 && !processingRef.current) {\n        processQueue();\n      }\n    });\n\n    // Tambi├®n procesar al montar si hay conexi├│n y cola pendiente\n    if (queue.length > 0) {\n      NetInfo.fetch().then((state) => {\n        if (state.isConnected) processQueue();\n      });\n    }\n\n    return () => unsubscribe();\n  }, [queue.length]);\n\n  async function processQueue() {\n    if (processingRef.current) return;\n    processingRef.current = true;\n    setSyncing(true);\n\n    const items = [...queue];\n\n    for (const mutation of items) {\n      if (mutation.retryCount >= MAX_RETRIES) {\n        removeFromQueue(mutation.id);\n        continue;\n      }\n\n      try {\n        if (mutation.type === 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA') {\n          await confirmarEntregaRequest(mutation.payload.pedidoId as string);\n        } else if (mutation.type === 'CANCELAR_PEDIDO') {\n          await cancelarPedidoRequest(\n            mutation.payload.pedidoId as string,\n            mutation.payload.motivo as MotivoCancelacion,\n          );\n        }\n        removeFromQueue(mutation.id);\n      } catch {\n        incrementRetry(mutation.id);\n        // Si ya lleg├│ a 3, remover en la pr├│xima iteraci├│n\n        if (mutation.retryCount + 1 >= MAX_RETRIES) {\n          removeFromQueue(mutation.id);\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    setSyncing(false);\n    processingRef.current = false;\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Modificar: `app/_layout.tsx`\n\nAgregar despu├®s de `const queryClient = ...` y antes de `export default function RootLayout()`:\n\n```tsx\n// Importar (con los imports existentes)\nimport useOfflineSync from '@/hooks/useOfflineSync';\n\n// Dentro de RootLayout(), antes del if(isLoading):\nuseOfflineSync();\n```\n\n### Modificar: `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx`\n\nAgregar `<ConnectivityBanner>`:\n\n```tsx\n// Imports nuevos:\nimport { ConnectivityBanner } from '@/components/ui';\nimport useNetworkStatus from '@/hooks/useNetworkStatus';\nimport { useOfflineStore } from '@/stores/offlineStore';\n\n// Dentro de TabLayout(), antes de todo:\nconst { isConnected } = useNetworkStatus();\nconst hasPendingSync = useOfflineStore((s) => s.queue.length > 0);\n\n// En el JSX, cambiar el return por:\nreturn (\n  <>\n    <ConnectivityBanner isConnected={isConnected} hasPendingSync={hasPendingSync} />\n    <Tabs screenOptions={{ ... }}>\n      ...\n    </Tabs>\n  </>\n);\n```\n\n---\n\n## ­ƒÜÇ ENTREGA 3 ÔÇö TAREA 5 (tests m├¡nimos)\n\n### Archivo NUEVO: `components/ui/__tests__/connectivity-banner.test.tsx`\n\n```tsx\nimport { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest';\nimport { render } from '@testing-library/react-native';\nimport ConnectivityBanner from '../connectivity-banner';\n\ndescribe('ConnectivityBanner', () => {\n  it('no renderiza nada cuando est├í online y sin pendientes', () => {\n    const { queryByText } = render(\n      <ConnectivityBanner isConnected={true} hasPendingSync={false} />\n    );\n    expect(queryByText(/conexi├│n/i)).toBeNull();\n  });\n\n  it('renderiza banner offline cuando no hay conexi├│n', () => {\n    const { getByText } = render(\n      <ConnectivityBanner isConnected={false} />\n    );\n    expect(getByText(/sin conexi├│n/i)).toBeTruthy();\n  });\n\n  it('renderiza banner de sync pendiente cuando online con cola', () => {\n    const { getByText } = render(\n      <ConnectivityBanner isConnected={true} hasPendingSync={true} />\n    );\n    expect(getByText(/pendientes de sincronizar/i)).toBeTruthy();\n  });\n});\n```\n\n### Archivo NUEVO: `stores/__tests__/offlineStore.test.ts`\n\n```ts\nimport { describe, it, expect, beforeEach } from 'vitest';\nimport { useOfflineStore } from '../offlineStore';\n\ndescribe('offlineStore', () => {\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    useOfflineStore.setState({ queue: [], isSyncing: false });\n  });\n\n  it('addToQueue agrega una mutaci├│n a la cola', () => {\n    useOfflineStore.getState().addToQueue({\n      type: 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA',\n      payload: { pedidoId: 'p1' },\n    });\n    const { queue } = useOfflineStore.getState();\n    expect(queue).toHaveLength(1);\n    expect(queue[0].type).toBe('CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA');\n    expect(queue[0].retryCount).toBe(0);\n  });\n\n  it('removeFromQueue elimina por id', () => {\n    useOfflineStore.getState().addToQueue({\n      type: 'CANCELAR_PEDIDO',\n      payload: { pedidoId: 'p1', motivo: 'CLIENTE_AUSENTE' },\n    });\n    const id = useOfflineStore.getState().queue[0].id;\n    useOfflineStore.getState().removeFromQueue(id);\n    expect(useOfflineStore.getState().queue).toHaveLength(0);\n  });\n\n  it('incrementRetry aumenta el contador', () => {\n    useOfflineStore.getState().addToQueue({\n      type: 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA',\n      payload: { pedidoId: 'p1' },\n    });\n    const id = useOfflineStore.getState().queue[0].id;\n    useOfflineStore.getState().incrementRetry(id);\n    expect(useOfflineStore.getState().queue[0].retryCount).toBe(1);\n  });\n});\n```\n\n### Modificar: `features/pedidos/hooks/__tests__/usePedidos.test.ts`\n\nAgregar 2 tests nuevos al final del archivo:\n\n```ts\n// Mock de uiStore y offlineStore\nconst mockIsOffline = vi.fn();\nvi.mock('@/stores/uiStore', () => ({\n  useUIStore: {\n    getState: () => ({ isOffline: mockIsOffline() }),\n  },\n}));\n\nconst mockAddToQueue = vi.fn();\nvi.mock('@/stores/offlineStore', () => ({\n  useOfflineStore: vi.fn((selector) => {\n    const state = { addToQueue: mockAddToQueue };\n    return selector ? selector(state) : state;\n  }),\n}));\n\n// Mock AsyncStorage\nconst mockAsyncStorageGet = vi.fn();\nconst mockAsyncStorageSet = vi.fn();\nvi.mock('@react-native-async-storage/async-storage', () => ({\n  default: {\n    getItem: (...args: unknown[]) => mockAsyncStorageGet(...args),\n    setItem: (...args: unknown[]) => mockAsyncStorageSet(...args),\n  },\n}));\n\n// TESTS NUEVOS:\n\ndescribe('usePedidosDelDia offline', () => {\n  it('usa cach├® cuando est├í offline en lugar de llamar API', async () => {\n    mockIsOffline.mockReturnValue(true);\n    mockAsyncStorageGet.mockResolvedValueOnce(JSON.stringify([{ id: 'cached-1' }]));\n\n    usePedidosDelDia();\n    const opts = getQueryOptions();\n    const result = await opts.queryFn();\n\n    expect(mockGetPedidosDelDia).not.toHaveBeenCalled();\n    expect(mockAsyncStorageGet).toHaveBeenCalledWith('@supplycycle/pedidos_hoy');\n    expect(result).toEqual([{ id: 'cached-1' }]);\n  });\n});\n\ndescribe('useConfirmarEntrega offline', () => {\n  it('encola mutaci├│n en offlineStore cuando no hay conexi├│n', async () => {\n    mockIsOffline.mockReturnValue(true);\n    vi.mocked(useMutation).mockImplementation((options: any) => ({\n      mutate: vi.fn(), mutateAsync: vi.fn(), isPending: false, isError: false,\n      error: null, isSuccess: false, reset: vi.fn(), data: undefined,\n    } as never));\n\n    useConfirmarEntrega();\n    const opts = getMutationOptions();\n    await opts.mutationFn('p1');\n\n    expect(mockAddToQueue).toHaveBeenCalledWith({\n      type: 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA',\n      payload: { pedidoId: 'p1' },\n    });\n  });\n});\n```\n\n> **ÔÜá´©Å Nota:** El mock de `useConfirmarEntrega` y `useCancelarPedido` es delicado porque ahora usan `useOfflineStore` dentro del hook. Si hay problemas de mocking, la alternativa es testear el comportamiento offline **desde el screen** con un test de integraci├│n, o simplificar el test unitario para solo verificar que `addToQueue` se llama en el `onMutate`.\n\n---\n\n## ­ƒôï Resumen visual: qu├® tocar exactamente\n\n```\nmobile/\nÔö£ÔöÇÔöÇ hooks/\nÔöé   Ôö£ÔöÇÔöÇ useNetworkStatus.ts          ÔåÉ MODIFICAR (agregar setOffline)\nÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ useOfflineSync.ts            ÔåÉ NUEVO archivo\nÔö£ÔöÇÔöÇ stores/\nÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ __tests__/\nÔöé       ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ offlineStore.test.ts     ÔåÉ NUEVO archivo\nÔö£ÔöÇÔöÇ features/pedidos/hooks/\nÔöé   Ôö£ÔöÇÔöÇ usePedidos.ts                ÔåÉ MODIFICAR (l├│gica offline)\nÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ __tests__/\nÔöé       ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ usePedidos.test.ts       ÔåÉ MODIFICAR (2 tests nuevos)\nÔö£ÔöÇÔöÇ components/ui/__tests__/\nÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ connectivity-banner.test.tsx  ÔåÉ NUEVO archivo\nÔööÔöÇÔöÇ app/\n    Ôö£ÔöÇÔöÇ _layout.tsx                   ÔåÉ MODIFICAR (agregar useOfflineSync)\n    ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ (tabs)/\n        ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ _layout.tsx               ÔåÉ MODIFICAR (agregar ConnectivityBanner)\n```\n\n**Total:**\n- **3 archivos nuevos** (useOfflineSync.ts, offlineStore.test.ts, connectivity-banner.test.tsx)\n- **5 archivos modificados** (useNetworkStatus.ts, usePedidos.ts, usePedidos.test.ts, _layout.tsx, tabs _layout.tsx)\n\n---\n\n┬┐Te parece bien este nivel de detalle? ┬┐Quieres que ajuste algo antes de que empiece a escribir el c├│digo?

Expo Web has it, but React Native doesn't. So we need something cross-platform.\n\nOptions for cross-platform connectivity detection without native modules:\n1. A simple periodic `fetch`/ping to the API server - if it fails, assume offline\n2. `navigator.onLine` for web + a fallback for native\n3. Use `expo-network` which works on both native and web\n\nActually, `expo-network` is part of Expo SDK and is cross-platform (iOS, Android, Web). It's not a \"native module\" in the problematic sense - it's an Expo package that works everywhere.\n\nBut wait, the user said \"sin las acciones nativas\" - maybe they mean:\n- No native-only updates/optimistic updates\n- Just simpler: cache reads when offline, queue writes, sync when back\n- Without complex mutation interception\n\nLet me re-read the user's message: \"mejor hagamoslo sin las acciones nativas, como habiamos dicho asi tambien funciona en el web y no requiere tanto esfuerzo\"\n\nI think \"acciones nativas\" = \"native modules/dependencies\" (like NetInfo). They want something that works on web too, and requires less effort.\n\nThe simplest cross-platform approach that requires NO native modules at all:\n\n**Use a simple fetch-based connectivity check:**\n```ts\n// Instead of NetInfo, we try to ping the API\nasync function checkConnectivity(): Promise<boolean> {\n  try {\n    const controller = new AbortController();\n    const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), 3000);\n    await fetch(API_URL + '/health', { method: 'HEAD', signal: controller.signal });\n    clearTimeout(timeoutId);\n    return true;\n  } catch {\n    return false;\n  }\n}\n```\n\nOr even simpler: just catch errors in the actual API calls and treat failures as offline.\n\nActually, the SIMPLEST approach that works everywhere:\n\n**Make `useNetworkStatus.ts` use a hybrid approach:**\n- For web: `navigator.onLine` + online/offline events\n- For native (fallback): assume online, rely on API errors to detect offline\n\nOr even simpler - we don't even need a separate connectivity check. It's the most lean and works on web too.\n\nLet me rewrite the plan.

Tienes raz├│n. Olvidemos `@react-native-community/netinfo` y toda la detecci├│n de conectividad nativa. Vamos con un enfoque **ultra-liviano que funciona en web y mobile**:\n\n---\n\n## Enfoque simplificado: sin NetInfo, sin conectividad nativa\n\n**Principio:** No detectamos conectividad proactivamente. Simplemente:\n1. Si una llamada API **falla**, asumimos que estamos offline\n2. Si falla un **GET** ÔåÆ servimos desde cach├® (ya existe Ô£à)\n3. Si falla un **POST/PATCH** (confirmar/cancelar) ÔåÆ encolamos para despu├®s\n4. Peri├│dicamente reintentamos la cola\n\nEsto elimina `useNetworkStatus.ts` por completo y todo el manejo de estado de conectividad.\n\n---\n\n### Archivos que ya NO tocamos (se eliminan del plan):\n\n| Archivo | Motivo |\n|---|---|\n| `hooks/useNetworkStatus.ts` | ÔØî **Eliminado** ÔÇö ya no lo necesitamos |\n| `stores/uiStore.ts` | ÔØî **No se toca** ÔÇö el flag `isOffline` ya no hace falta |\n\n---\n\n### Archivos que S├ì tocamos (versi├│n final reducida):\n\n```\nmobile/\nÔö£ÔöÇÔöÇ hooks/\nÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ useOfflineSync.ts            ÔåÉ NUEVO (reintenta cola cada 30s + al montar)\nÔö£ÔöÇÔöÇ stores/\nÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ __tests__/\nÔöé       ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ offlineStore.test.ts     ÔåÉ NUEVO\nÔö£ÔöÇÔöÇ features/pedidos/hooks/\nÔöé   Ôö£ÔöÇÔöÇ usePedidos.ts                ÔåÉ MODIFICAR (encolar en mutations al fallar)\nÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ __tests__/\nÔöé       ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ usePedidos.test.ts       ÔåÉ MODIFICAR (2 tests)\nÔö£ÔöÇÔöÇ components/ui/__tests__/\nÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ connectivity-banner.test.tsx  ÔåÉ NUEVO\nÔööÔöÇÔöÇ app/\n    Ôö£ÔöÇÔöÇ _layout.tsx                   ÔåÉ MODIFICAR (inicializar useOfflineSync)\n    ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ (tabs)/\n        ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ _layout.tsx               ÔåÉ MODIFICAR (ConnectivityBanner seg├║n cola)\n```\n\n**Total: 3 archivos nuevos + 4 modificados** (vs 3+5 antes)\n\n---\n\n### ENTREGA 1 ÔÇö usePedidos.ts (simplificado a├║n m├ís)\n\n**Archivo:** `features/pedidos/hooks/usePedidos.ts`\n\nCambios:\n\n1. **Eliminar imports** que ya no usamos:\n   ```ts\n   // ELIMINAR l├¡nea 5:\n   import useNetworkStatus from '@/hooks/useNetworkStatus';\n   ```\n\n2. **`useConfirmarEntrega`** ÔÇö intenta API, si falla ÔåÆ encola:\n   ```ts\n   export function useConfirmarEntrega() {\n     const queryClient = useQueryClient();\n     const addToQueue = useOfflineStore((s) => s.addToQueue);\n\n     return useMutation({\n       mutationFn: async (pedidoId: string) => {\n         try {\n           return await confirmarEntregaRequest(pedidoId);\n         } catch {\n           // Offline: actualizaci├│n optimista + encolar\n           addToQueue({ type: 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA', payload: { pedidoId } });\n           queryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', pedidoId], (old) => {\n             if (old) return { ...old, estado: 'ENTREGADO' as const };\n             return old;\n           });\n           queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedidos'] });\n           queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['reparto'] });\n           return { id: pedidoId, estado: 'ENTREGADO' as const, actualizadoEn: new Date().toISOString() };\n         }\n       },\n       onSuccess: (data) => {\n         // Solo actualizar si NO fue offline (en offline ya se actualiz├│ en mutationFn)\n         if (data.__isOffline) return;\n         queryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', data.id], (old) => {\n           if (old) return { ...old, estado: 'ENTREGADO' as const };\n           return old;\n         });\n         queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedidos'] });\n         queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['reparto'] });\n       },\n     });\n   }\n   ```\n\n   Lo mismo para `useCancelarPedido` (an├ílogo).\n\n3. **`usePedidosDelDia`** ÔÇö ya tiene try/catch con fallback a cach├® Ô£à (no tocar)\n\n**Esfuerzo:** ~30 min\n\n---\n\n### ENTREGA 2 ÔÇö useOfflineSync.ts + ConnectivityBanner\n\n#### Archivo NUEVO: `hooks/useOfflineSync.ts`\n\n```ts\nimport { useEffect, useRef } from 'react';\nimport { useOfflineStore } from '@/stores/offlineStore';\nimport { confirmarEntregaRequest, cancelarPedidoRequest } from '@/features/pedidos/services/pedidoService';\nimport type { MotivoCancelacion } from '@/types';\n\nconst RETRY_INTERVAL = 30000; // 30 segundos\nconst MAX_RETRIES = 3;\n\nexport default function useOfflineSync() {\n  const { queue, removeFromQueue, incrementRetry, setSyncing } = useOfflineStore();\n  const intervalRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null>(null);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (queue.length > 0) {\n      processQueue();\n      // Reintentar peri├│dicamente mientras haya cola\n      intervalRef.current = setInterval(processQueue, RETRY_INTERVAL);\n    }\n\n    return () => {\n      if (intervalRef.current) {\n        clearInterval(intervalRef.current);\n        intervalRef.current = null;\n      }\n    };\n  }, [queue.length]);\n\n  async function processQueue() {\n    setSyncing(true);\n    const items = [...queue];\n\n    for (const mutation of items) {\n      if (mutation.retryCount >= MAX_RETRIES) {\n        removeFromQueue(mutation.id);\n        continue;\n      }\n\n      try {\n        if (mutation.type === 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA') {\n          await confirmarEntregaRequest(mutation.payload.pedidoId as string);\n        } else if (mutation.type === 'CANCELAR_PEDIDO') {\n          await cancelarPedidoRequest(\n            mutation.payload.pedidoId as string,\n            mutation.payload.motivo as MotivoCancelacion,\n          );\n        }\n        removeFromQueue(mutation.id);\n      } catch {\n        incrementRetry(mutation.id);\n        if (mutation.retryCount + 1 >= MAX_RETRIES) {\n          removeFromQueue(mutation.id);\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    setSyncing(false);\n  }\n}\n```\n\n#### Modificar: `app/_layout.tsx`\n\nAgregar despu├®s de `useOfflineSync();` dentro de `RootLayout()`.\n\n#### Modificar: `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx`\n\n```tsx\nimport { ConnectivityBanner } from '@/components/ui';\nimport { useOfflineStore } from '@/stores/offlineStore';\n\n// Dentro de TabLayout:\nconst hasPendingSync = useOfflineStore((s) => s.queue.length > 0);\n\n// Envolver return:\nreturn (\n  <>\n    {hasPendingSync && (\n      <ConnectivityBanner isConnected={true} hasPendingSync={true} />\n    )}\n    <Tabs>...</Tabs>\n  </>\n);\n```\n\n> **Nota:** El banner solo muestra \"pendientes de sincronizar\" cuando hay cola. Como no detectamos conectividad activamente, nunca mostramos \"Sin conexi├│n\". Es m├ís simple y evita falsos positivos.\n\n**Esfuerzo:** ~1h\n\n---\n\n### ENTREGA 3 ÔÇö Tests\n\n#### Archivo NUEVO: `components/ui/__tests__/connectivity-banner.test.tsx`\n(igual que antes, 3 tests)\n\n#### Archivo NUEVO: `stores/__tests__/offlineStore.test.ts`\n(igual que antes, 3 tests)\n\n#### Modificar: `features/pedidos/hooks/__tests__/usePedidos.test.ts`\n\nAgregar mock de `useOfflineStore`:\n```ts\nconst mockAddToQueue = vi.fn();\nvi.mock('@/stores/offlineStore', () => ({\n  useOfflineStore: vi.fn((selector) => {\n    const state = { addToQueue: mockAddToQueue, queue: [] };\n    return selector ? selector(state) : state;\n  }),\n}));\n```\n\nY agregar test:\n```ts\nit('encola mutaci├│n cuando confirmarEntrega falla (offline)', async () => {\n  mockConfirmarEntrega.mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error('Network Error'));\n  vi.mocked(useMutation).mockImplementation((options: any) => ({\n    mutate: vi.fn(), mutateAsync: vi.fn(), isPending: false, isError: false,\n    error: null, isSuccess: false, reset: vi.fn(), data: undefined,\n  } as never));\n\n  useConfirmarEntrega();\n  const opts = getMutationOptions();\n  await opts.mutationFn('p1');\n\n  expect(mockAddToQueue).toHaveBeenCalledWith({\n    type: 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA',\n    payload: { pedidoId: 'p1' },\n  });\n});\n```\n\n**Esfuerzo:** ~1h\n\n---\n\n### Resumen final\n\n| Componente | Dependencias nativas | Funciona en web |\n|---|---|---|\n| `useOfflineSync` | ÔØî Ninguna | Ô£à |\n| `offlineStore` | ÔØî Ninguna (Zustand puro) | Ô£à |\n| `ConnectivityBanner` | ÔØî Ninguna | Ô£à |\n| `usePedidos` offline | ÔØî Ninguna | Ô£à |\n\n**Esfuerzo total: ~2.5h** (la mitad que el plan anterior)\n\n**Riesgos eliminados:**\n- Ô£à No depende de `@react-native-community/netinfo` (falla en web)\n- Ô£à No depende de `expo-secure-store`\n- Ô£à No hay estado de conectividad que se dessincronice\n- Ô£à L├│gica pura: si falla ÔåÆ encola; si hay cola ÔåÆ reintenta\n\n┬┐Te parece bien este enfoque? Si est├ís de acuerdo, paso a escribir el c├│digo de cada entrega.

The user wants me to implement the code. Let me start with the first entrega. Let me plan the order:\n\n1. First, modify `usePedidos.ts` (Entrega 1)\n2. Create `useOfflineSync.ts` (Entrega 2)\n3. Modify `app/_layout.tsx` (Entrega 2)\n4. Modify `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx` (Entrega 2)\n5. Create test files (Entrega 3)\n6. Modify existing test file (Entrega 3)\n\nLet me start by reading the files I need to modify to make sure I have the latest content, then make all the changes.\n\nActually, I already read all the files. I can proceed with the edits.\n\nLet me think about the order carefully:\n\n1. `features/pedidos/hooks/usePedidos.ts` ÔÇö modify\n   - Remove `import useNetworkStatus` (line 5)\n   - Modify `useConfirmarEntrega` \n   - Modify `useCancelarPedido`\n   - Keep `useOfflineStore` import (already there)\n\n2. Create `hooks/useOfflineSync.ts`\n\n3. Modify `app/_layout.tsx` ÔÇö add `useOfflineSync` import and call\n\n4. Modify `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx` ÔÇö add ConnectivityBanner\n\n5. Create `components/ui/__tests__/connectivity-banner.test.tsx`\n\n6. Create `stores/__tests__/offlineStore.test.ts`\n\n7. Modify `features/pedidos/hooks/__tests__/usePedidos.test.ts` ÔÇö add offline tests\n\nLet me start implementing. I'll make the changes file by file.\n\nFor `usePedidos.ts`:\n\nI need to:\n1. Remove `import useNetworkStatus from '@/hooks/useNetworkStatus';` (line 5)\n2. Modify `useConfirmarEntrega` to use try/catch and addToQueue\n3. Modify `useCancelarPedido` same way\n\nLet me think about the exact code:\n\nFor `useConfirmarEntrega`:\n```ts\nexport function useConfirmarEntrega() {\n  const queryClient = useQueryClient();\n  const addToQueue = useOfflineStore((s) => s.addToQueue);\n\n  return useMutation({\n    mutationFn: async (pedidoId: string) => {\n      try {\n        return await confirmarEntregaRequest(pedidoId);\n      } catch {\n        addToQueue({ type: 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA', payload: { pedidoId } });\n        queryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', pedidoId], (old) => {\n          if (old) return { ...old, estado: 'ENTREGADO' as const };\n          return old;\n        });\n        queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedidos'] });\n        queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['reparto'] });\n        return { id: pedidoId, estado: 'ENTREGADO' as const, actualizadoEn: new Date().toISOString() };\n      }\n    },\n    onSuccess: (data) => {\n      queryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', data.id], (old) => {\n        if (old) return { ...old, estado: 'ENTREGADO' as const };\n        return old;\n      });\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedidos'] });\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['reparto'] });\n    },\n  });\n}\n```\n\nWait, there's an issue. The `onSuccess` will run even when the mutation returns from the catch block (in offline mode). So we'd be updating the cache twice. Actually... in the catch block we already update the cache. But `onSuccess` runs after `mutationFn` resolves successfully. Since we return from catch, it will resolve successfully, and then `onSuccess` will run again.\n\nSo I need to differentiate between a real online success and an offline emulated success. Let me add a flag:\n\nActually, a simpler approach: In the catch block, just throw a special error to prevent onSuccess from running. But that would show an error to the user...\n\nBetter approach: Use `onMutate` for optimistic updates and `onSettled` for cleanup. Let me restructure:\n\n```ts\nexport function useConfirmarEntrega() {\n  const queryClient = useQueryClient();\n  const addToQueue = useOfflineStore((s) => s.addToQueue);\n\n  return useMutation({\n    mutationFn: async (pedidoId: string) => {\n      return await confirmarEntregaRequest(pedidoId);\n    },\n    onMutate: async (pedidoId: string) => {\n      // Optimistic update\n      queryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', pedidoId], (old) => {\n        if (old) return { ...old, estado: 'ENTREGADO' as const };\n        return old;\n      });\n    },\n    onError: (err, pedidoId) => {\n      // Si falla, revertir o encolar\n      addToQueue({ type: 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA', payload: { pedidoId } });\n      // Mantener el optimistic update (est├í bien, es offline)\n    },\n    onSuccess: () => {\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedidos'] });\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['reparto'] });\n    },\n  });\n}\n```\n\nHmm, but with `onMutate`, the optimistic update happens BEFORE the mutationFn. If mutationFn succeeds, great. If it fails (offline), we keep the optimistic update (which is what we want) and enqueue for later.\n\nActually wait, there's a subtlety. The current code calls `confirmarEntregaRequest` which does an HTTP request. If offline, it will throw. Then `onError` runs. In `onError`, we add to queue. The optimistic update from `onMutate` already updated the cache, so the user sees the change immediately. \n\nBut we also need to invalidate queries even in the error case (offline), because the cache was already optimistically updated. Let me check... Actually, `invalidateQueries` in `onSuccess` would NOT run when there's an error. So we need to invalidate in the error case too.\n\nLet me revise:\n\n```ts\nexport function useConfirmarEntrega() {\n  const queryClient = useQueryClient();\n  const addToQueue = useOfflineStore((s) => s.addToQueue);\n\n  return useMutation({\n    mutationFn: async (pedidoId: string) => {\n      return await confirmarEntregaRequest(pedidoId);\n    },\n    onMutate: async (pedidoId: string) => {\n      // Cancel outgoing refetches\n      await queryClient.cancelQueries({ queryKey: ['pedido', pedidoId] });\n      \n      // Snapshot previous value\n      const previousPedido = queryClient.getQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', pedidoId]);\n      \n      // Optimistic update\n      queryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', pedidoId], (old) => {\n        if (old) return { ...old, estado: 'ENTREGADO' as const };\n        return old;\n      });\n\n      return { previousPedido };\n    },\n    onError: (err, pedidoId, context) => {\n      // Encolar para sincronizar despu├®s\n      addToQueue({ type: 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA', payload: { pedidoId } });\n      // No revertimos el optimistic update porque queremos mostrar el cambio inmediato\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedidos'] });\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['reparto'] });\n    },\n    onSuccess: (data) => {\n      queryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', data.id], (old) => {\n        if (old) return { ...old, estado: 'ENTREGADO' as const };\n        return old;\n      });\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedidos'] });\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['reparto'] });\n    },\n    onSettled: () => {\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedido'] });\n    },\n  });\n}\n```\n\nActually this is getting complex. Let me look at the SIMPLEST possible approach that works. The user wants reduced scope. Let me keep it dead simple:\n\n```ts\nexport function useConfirmarEntrega() {\n  const queryClient = useQueryClient();\n  const addToQueue = useOfflineStore((s) => s.addToQueue);\n\n  return useMutation({\n    mutationFn: async (pedidoId: string) => {\n      try {\n        return await confirmarEntregaRequest(pedidoId);\n      } catch {\n        // Si falla la llamada (offline), encolamos\n        addToQueue({ type: 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA', payload: { pedidoId } });\n        // Devolvemos un objeto simulado para que el flujo contin├║e\n        return { id: pedidoId, estado: 'ENTREGADO' as const, actualizadoEn: new Date().toISOString() };\n      }\n    },\n    onSuccess: (data) => {\n      queryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', data.id], (old) => {\n        if (old) return { ...old, estado: 'ENTREGADO' as const };\n        return old;\n      });\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedidos'] });\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['reparto'] });\n    },\n  });\n}\n```\n\nThis is simple and straightforward. When offline:\n1. API call throws\n2. Catch block: enqueue + return simulated response\n3. `onSuccess` runs with simulated response, updates cache\n\nWhen online:\n1. API call succeeds\n2. `onSuccess` runs, updates cache\n\nSimple. No need for onMutate or onError. The `onSuccess` handles both cases.\n\nBut wait, there's a subtle issue: when the mutation is in the queue and later syncs successfully, the onSuccess will run again... Actually no, the queued mutations are processed by `useOfflineSync` which calls the API directly, not through the mutation hook. So `onSuccess` won't run for the retry. Good.\n\nActually wait, looking at `useOfflineSync`, I'm calling `confirmarEntregaRequest` directly, not through the mutation hook. So the `onSuccess` of the mutation hook won't fire. The sync just removes the item from the queue. The cache was already updated optimistically. So we're good.\n\nBut there's another issue: if the mutation succeeds (online), `onSuccess` updates the cache and invalidates queries. If it fails (offline), the catch enqueues, returns a simulated result, and then `onSuccess` updates the cache and invalidates queries. So the cache is updated in both cases. When the queue processes later, the API call will succeed, but the cache might already have the correct data from the optimistic update. We should invalidate after sync too.\n\nFor `useOfflineSync`, after successfully processing a mutation, we could invalidate queries. Let me add that.\n\nOK let me just write the code. I'll keep it simple.\n\nLet me also think about tests. For the test, since the mutationFn catches the error internally, the test just needs to verify that when the API throws, `addToQueue` is called and the mutation returns a value.\n\nNow let me also think about `useCancelarPedido`:\n\n```ts\nexport function useCancelarPedido() {\n  const queryClient = useQueryClient();\n  const addToQueue = useOfflineStore((s) => s.addToQueue);\n\n  return useMutation({\n    mutationFn: async ({ pedidoId, motivo }: { pedidoId: string; motivo: MotivoCancelacion }) => {\n      try {\n        return await cancelarPedidoRequest(pedidoId, motivo);\n      } catch {\n        addToQueue({ type: 'CANCELAR_PEDIDO', payload: { pedidoId, motivo } });\n        return { id: pedidoId, estado: 'NO_ENTREGADO' as const, motivoFalla: motivo, actualizadoEn: new Date().toISOString() };\n      }\n    },\n    onSuccess: (data) => {\n      queryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', data.id], (old) => {\n        if (old) return { ...old, estado: 'NO_ENTREGADO' as const, motivoFalla: data.motivoFalla };\n        return old;\n      });\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedidos'] });\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['reparto'] });\n    },\n  });\n}\n```\n\nWait, in the catch block, `data.motivoFalla` would be... hmm, in the catch block I'm returning `motivo` but the type of `data` in `onSuccess` expects `data.motivoFalla` to be a string. Let me check the return type.\n\nIn `cancelarPedidoRequest` the return type is:\n```ts\n{ id: string; estado: 'NO_ENTREGADO'; motivoFalla: string; actualizadoEn: string }\n```\n\nAnd `MotivoCancelacion` is presumably `'CLIENTE_AUSENTE' | 'RECHAZADO' | 'DIRECCION_INCORRECTA'` or similar. So `motivo` should be assignable to `string`. Let me use `motivo as string` or just `motivo`.\n\nActually, looking at the offline store mutation payload: `payload: Record<string, unknown>`. So the payload type doesn't enforce MotivoCancelacion. That's fine.\n\nLet me just write the code now. I'll also need to think about the `useOfflineSync` - after a successful sync, we should invalidate the relevant queries so the UI refreshes with fresh data.\n\nLet me finalize my approach and start writing:\n\nFor the tests, the issue is that `useConfirmarEntrega` now catches the error internally in `mutationFn`. The test currently mocks `useMutation` at the top level and then accesses `mutationFn` via `getMutationOptions()`. So the test would call `mutationFn` and verify that `mockAddToQueue` was called.\n\nBut wait, in the test, `useConfirmarEntrega` calls `useMutation()` which the test has mocked. The mock returns an object where `mutationFn` is set by the mock... Actually no, the mock captures the options passed to useMutation. Let me look at the test again.\n\nIn the test, `vi.mocked(useMutation).mockImplementation((options: any) => ({...}))` captures the options. So when `useConfirmarEntrega` calls `useMutation({ mutationFn: ..., onSuccess: ... })`, the test captures those options.\n\nThen `getMutationOptions()` gets those options, and `opts.mutationFn(payload)` calls the actual mutationFn from the production code.\n\nSo in the test:\n```ts\nit('encola mutaci├│n cuando confirmarEntrega falla (offline)', async () => {\n  mockConfirmarEntrega.mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error('Network Error'));\n  vi.mocked(useMutation).mockImplementation((options: any) => ({\n    mutate: vi.fn(), mutateAsync: vi.fn(), isPending: false, isError: false,\n    error: null, isSuccess: false, reset: vi.fn(), data: undefined,\n  } as never));\n\n  useConfirmarEntrega();\n  const opts = getMutationOptions();\n  const result = await opts.mutationFn('p1');\n\n  expect(mockAddToQueue).toHaveBeenCalledWith({\n    type: 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA',\n    payload: { pedidoId: 'p1' },\n  });\n  expect(result).toEqual({\n    id: 'p1',\n    estado: 'ENTREGADO',\n    actualizadoEn: expect.any(String),\n  });\n});\n```\n\nThis should work. The `mockConfirmarEntrega` will reject, the catch block in the production code will handle it, call `addToQueue`, and return the simulated object.\n\nNow for the `useOfflineSync` - I need to invalidate queries after successful sync. Let me update the hook:\n\n```ts\nimport { useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query';\n\n// In processQueue, after successful mutation:\nconst queryClient = useQueryClient();\n// ... after removeFromQueue:\nqueryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedidos'] });\nqueryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['reparto'] });\n```\n\nActually, using `useQueryClient` inside `processQueue` (which is not a hook) won't work. I need to get the query client outside the effect:\n\n```ts\nconst queryClient = useQueryClient();\n```\n\nAnd then use it inside `processQueue`. Yes, that works because `useQueryClient` is called at the top level of the hook component.\n\nOK wait, `useOfflineSync` is a hook (it has `useEffect` and `useRef`), so `useQueryClient` at the top level is fine.\n\nLet me now plan the exact changes I need to make:\n\n1. **`features/pedidos/hooks/usePedidos.ts`**:\n   - Remove line 5: `import useNetworkStatus from '@/hooks/useNetworkStatus';`\n   - Replace `useConfirmarEntrega()` function\n   - Replace `useCancelarPedido()` function\n\n2. **Create `hooks/useOfflineSync.ts`**\n\n3. **`app/_layout.tsx`**:\n   - Add import for `useOfflineSync`\n   - Add `useOfflineSync()` call in `RootLayout()`\n\n4. **`app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx`**:\n   - Add imports for `ConnectivityBanner` and `useOfflineStore`\n   - Get `hasPendingSync`\n   - Add `ConnectivityBanner` to JSX\n\n5. **Create `components/ui/__tests__/connectivity-banner.test.tsx`**\n\n6. **Create `stores/__tests__/offlineStore.test.ts`**\n\n7. **`features/pedidos/hooks/__tests__/usePedidos.test.ts`**:\n   - Add mock for `@/stores/offlineStore`\n   - Add tests for offline behavior\n\nLet me think about the test file more carefully. Let me verify:\n\n```ts\nvi.mock('@/stores/offlineStore', () => ({\n  useOfflineStore: vi.fn((selector) => {\n    const state = { addToQueue: mockAddToQueue, queue: [] };\n    return selector ? selector(state) : state;\n  }),\n}));\n```\n\nWhen `useOfflineStore((s) => s.addToQueue)` is called:\n- `selector` = `(s) => s.addToQueue`\n- `state` = `{ addToQueue: mockAddToQueue, queue: [] }`\n- Returns `selector(state)` = `mockAddToQueue` Ô£à\n\nWhen `useOfflineStore((s) => s.queue.length)` is called (though this probably doesn't happen in the hook since we use `addToQueue` directly):\n- `selector` = `(s) => s.queue.length`\n- `state` = `{ addToQueue: mockAddToQueue, queue: [] }`\n- Returns `selector(state)` = `0` Ô£à\n\nGood.\n\nBut wait, in the tests for hooks, we're mocking `useMutation` globally. The `useConfirmarEntrega` function calls `useMutation` with options, but the test's `vi.mocked(useMutation).mockImplementation(...)` captures those options. So when the test calls `opts.mutationFn('p1')`, it runs the production `mutationFn` which calls `confirmarEntregaRequest` (mocked to reject) and then calls `addToQueue` (mocked as `mockAddToQueue`). \n\nActually wait, I just realized there's a subtlety. The current test does:\n\n```ts\nvi.mocked(useMutation).mockImplementation((options: any) => ({\n  mutate: vi.fn(), mutateAsync: vi.fn(), isPending: false, isError: false,\n  error: null, isSuccess: false, reset: vi.fn(), data: undefined,\n} as never));\n```\n\nThis means it captures `options` but ignores it. But then `getMutationOptions()` gets the options from `vi.mocked(useMutation).mock.calls[callIndex][0]`. So it gets the options object that was passed to `useMutation`.\n\nBut there's a problem: the mock is set up BEFORE `useConfirmarEntrega()` is called in the test. When `useConfirmarEntrega()` runs, it calls `useMutation({...})` which is mocked. The mock implementation ignores the options and returns a fake object. Then `getMutationOptions()` gets the options from the mock calls.\n\nWait, looking more carefully at the existing test:\n\n```ts\nit('should pass mutationFn that calls confirmarEntregaRequest', async () => {\n  vi.mocked(useMutation).mockImplementation((options: any) => ({\n    mutate: vi.fn(), mutateAsync: vi.fn(), isPending: false, isError: false,\n    error: null, isSuccess: false, reset: vi.fn(), data: undefined,\n  } as never));\n\n  mockConfirmarEntrega.mockResolvedValueOnce({ id: 'p1' });\n\n  useConfirmarEntrega();\n  const opts = getMutationOptions();\n  const result = await opts.mutationFn('p1');\n\n  expect(mockConfirmarEntrega).toHaveBeenCalledWith('p1');\n  expect(result).toEqual({ id: 'p1' });\n});\n```\n\nHere, `getMutationOptions()` returns the first argument from `useMutation()` calls, which is the options object. `opts.mutationFn` is the actual production `mutationFn`. The test calls it directly and verifies the result.\n\nSo when `opts.mutationFn('p1')` is called, it runs the production code which calls `confirmarEntregaRequest('p1')`. The service is mocked (`mockConfirmarEntrega`) and resolves to `{ id: 'p1' }`. The catch block doesn't execute. Result is `{ id: 'p1' }`. Test passes.\n\nFor my new test (offline case):\n- `mockConfirmarEntrega.mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error('Network Error'))`\n- `opts.mutationFn('p1')` runs production code\n- Production code calls `confirmarEntregaRequest('p1')` ÔåÆ rejects\n- Catch block: `addToQueue({ type: 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA', payload: { pedidoId: 'p1' } })` ÔåÆ calls `mockAddToQueue`\n- Returns `{ id: 'p1', estado: 'ENTREGADO', actualizadoEn: ... }`\n- Test verifies `mockAddToQueue` was called with the right args\n- Test verifies result is as expected\n\nThis should work!\n\nBut I need to make sure `useOfflineStore` is mocked properly in the test. In Jest/Vitest, `jest.mock`/`vi.mock` calls are hoisted to the top of the file (before any imports). But if the factory function references variables outside the factory, those variables need to be available when the factory runs. With `vi.mock`, the factory runs lazily when the module is first imported.\n\nActually, with vitest's `vi.mock(path, factory)`, the factory function creates the mock module. It's hoisted, but variables declared with `const` at the top level using `vi.fn()` should be available because they're also hoisted (though as `undefined` until assigned).\n\nHmm, this can be tricky. Let me look at how the existing test does it:\n\n```ts\nconst mockInvalidateQueries = vi.fn();\nconst mockSetQueryData = vi.fn();\n\nvi.mock('@tanstack/react-query', async (importOriginal) => {\n  const actual = await importOriginal();\n  return {\n    ...(actual as object),\n    useQuery: vi.fn(),\n    useMutation: vi.fn(),\n    useQueryClient: vi.fn(() => ({\n      invalidateQueries: mockInvalidateQueries,\n      setQueryData: mockSetQueryData,\n    })),\n  };\n});\n```\n\nHere, `mockInvalidateQueries` and `mockSetQueryData` are defined BEFORE `vi.mock`, so they're available in the factory. This is the correct pattern. So I should follow the same pattern:\n\n```ts\nconst mockAddToQueue = vi.fn();\n\nvi.mock('@/stores/offlineStore', () => ({\n  useOfflineStore: vi.fn((selector) => {\n    const state = { addToQueue: mockAddToQueue, queue: [] };\n    return selector ? selector(state) : state;\n  }),\n}));\n```\n\nBut wait, `vi.mock` is hoisted to the top, so `mockAddToQueue` would need to be defined before any other code... Since `vi.mock` is hoisted, the factory function is created before any code runs... but `mockInvalidateQueries` might not be initialized yet...\n\nWait, this is confusing. Let me look at how it actually works in practice. The fact that the existing test works tells me that `vi.fn()` declarations before `vi.mock` ARE available in the factory. This is because vitest's `vi.mock` hoisting works differently - the factory function captures variables from the closure, and if `vi.fn()` is called at module level, it's initialized before the mock factory runs.\n\nActually, in vitest, the behavior is:\n- `vi.mock()` is hoisted to the top (above imports)\n- But any variables defined via `const`/`let`/`var` at the top level are evaluated at module load time\n- The factory function inside `vi.mock` captures these variables\n- When the mock is applied, the variables have already been initialized\n\nSo `const mockAddToQueue = vi.fn(); vi.mock('...', () => ({ useOfflineStore: ...mockAddToQueue... }))` should work.\n\nOK, I'm going to go ahead and write the code. ### Contenido:
import { useEffect, useRef } from 'react';\nimport { useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query';\nimport { useOfflineStore } from '@/stores/offlineStore';\nimport {\n  confirmarEntregaRequest,\n  cancelarPedidoRequest,\n} from '@/features/pedidos/services/pedidoService';\nimport type { MotivoCancelacion } from '@/types';\n\nconst RETRY_INTERVAL = 30000; // 30 segundos\nconst MAX_RETRIES = 3;\n\nexport default function useOfflineSync() {\n  const queryClient = useQueryClient();\n  const queue = useOfflineStore((s) => s.queue);\n  const removeFromQueue = useOfflineStore((s) => s.removeFromQueue);\n  const incrementRetry = useOfflineStore((s) => s.incrementRetry);\n  const setSyncing = useOfflineStore((s) => s.setSyncing);\n  const processingRef = useRef(false);\n  const intervalRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null>(null);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (queue.length > 0) {\n      processQueue();\n      // Reintentar peri├│dicamente mientras haya cola\n      intervalRef.current = setInterval(processQueue, RETRY_INTERVAL);\n    }\n\n    return () => {\n      if (intervalRef.current) {\n        clearInterval(intervalRef.current);\n        intervalRef.current = null;\n      }\n    };\n    // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps\n  }, [queue.length]);\n\n  async function processQueue() {\n    if (processingRef.current) return;\n    processingRef.current = true;\n    setSyncing(true);\n\n    const items = [...queue];\n\n    for (const mutation of items) {\n      if (mutation.retryCount >= MAX_RETRIES) {\n        removeFromQueue(mutation.id);\n        continue;\n      }\n\n      try {\n        if (mutation.type === 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA') {\n          await confirmarEntregaRequest(mutation.payload.pedidoId as string);\n        } else if (mutation.type === 'CANCELAR_PEDIDO') {\n          await cancelarPedidoRequest(\n            mutation.payload.pedidoId as string,\n            mutation.payload.motivo as MotivoCancelacion,\n          );\n        }\n        removeFromQueue(mutation.id);\n        // Refrescar datos despu├®s de sincronizar\n        queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedidos'] });\n        queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['reparto'] });\n      } catch {\n        incrementRetry(mutation.id);\n        if (mutation.retryCount + 1 >= MAX_RETRIES) {\n          removeFromQueue(mutation.id);\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    setSyncing(false);\n    processingRef.current = false;\n  }\n}\n

### Contenido:
import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest';\nimport { render } from '@testing-library/react-native';\nimport ConnectivityBanner from '../connectivity-banner';\n\ndescribe('ConnectivityBanner', () => {\n  it('no renderiza nada cuando est├í online y sin pendientes', () => {\n    const { queryByText } = render(\n      <ConnectivityBanner isConnected={true} hasPendingSync={false} />,\n    );\n    expect(queryByText(/conexi├│n/i)).toBeNull();\n  });\n\n  it('renderiza banner offline cuando no hay conexi├│n', () => {\n    const { getByText } = render(<ConnectivityBanner isConnected={false} />);\n    expect(getByText(/sin conexi├│n/i)).toBeTruthy();\n  });\n\n  it('renderiza banner de sync pendiente cuando online con cola', () => {\n    const { getByText } = render(\n      <ConnectivityBanner isConnected={true} hasPendingSync={true} />,\n    );\n    expect(getByText(/pendientes de sincronizar/i)).toBeTruthy();\n  });\n});\n

### Contenido:
import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach } from 'vitest';\nimport { useOfflineStore } from '../offlineStore';\n\ndescribe('offlineStore', () => {\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    useOfflineStore.setState({ queue: [], isSyncing: false });\n  });\n\n  it('addToQueue agrega una mutaci├│n a la cola con valores por defecto', () => {\n    useOfflineStore.getState().addToQueue({\n      type: 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA',\n      payload: { pedidoId: 'p1' },\n    });\n    const { queue } = useOfflineStore.getState();\n    expect(queue).toHaveLength(1);\n    expect(queue[0].type).toBe('CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA');\n    expect(queue[0].retryCount).toBe(0);\n    expect(queue[0].payload).toEqual({ pedidoId: 'p1' });\n  });\n\n  it('removeFromQueue elimina la mutaci├│n por id', () => {\n    useOfflineStore.getState().addToQueue({\n      type: 'CANCELAR_PEDIDO',\n      payload: { pedidoId: 'p1', motivo: 'CLIENTE_AUSENTE' },\n    });\n    const id = useOfflineStore.getState().queue[0].id;\n    useOfflineStore.getState().removeFromQueue(id);\n    expect(useOfflineStore.getState().queue).toHaveLength(0);\n  });\n\n  it('incrementRetry aumenta el contador de reintentos', () => {\n    useOfflineStore.getState().addToQueue({\n      type: 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA',\n      payload: { pedidoId: 'p1' },\n    });\n    const id = useOfflineStore.getState().queue[0].id;\n    useOfflineStore.getState().incrementRetry(id);\n    expect(useOfflineStore.getState().queue[0].retryCount).toBe(1);\n  });\n\n  it('clearQueue vac├¡a toda la cola', () => {\n    useOfflineStore.getState().addToQueue({\n      type: 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA',\n      payload: { pedidoId: 'p1' },\n    });\n    useOfflineStore.getState().addToQueue({\n      type: 'CANCELAR_PEDIDO',\n      payload: { pedidoId: 'p2' },\n    });\n    expect(useOfflineStore.getState().queue).toHaveLength(2);\n    useOfflineStore.getState().clearQueue();\n    expect(useOfflineStore.getState().queue).toHaveLength(0);\n  });\n});\n

That's correct.\n\nBut why did the try block fail? Let me look at the test order:\n\n```ts\nit('queryFn should call service then fallback on error', async () => {\n    mockGetPedidosDelDia.mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error('fail'));\n    mockMockPedidosDelDia.mockResolvedValueOnce(['mock']);\n    usePedidosDelDia();\n    const opts = getQueryOptions();\n    const result = await opts.queryFn();\n    expect(mockGetPedidosDelDia).toHaveBeenCalledWith('user-1');\n    expect(mockMockPedidosDelDia).toHaveBeenCalledWith();\n    expect(result).toEqual(['mock']);\n});\n\nit('queryFn should return service result when successful', async () => {\n    mockGetPedidosDelDia.mockResolvedValueOnce(['real']);\n    usePedidosDelDia();\n    const opts = getQueryOptions();\n    const result = await opts.queryFn();\n    expect(mockGetPedidosDelDia).toHaveBeenCalledWith('user-1');\n    expect(mockMockPedidosDelDia).not.toHaveBeenCalled();\n    expect(result).toEqual(['real']);\n});\n```\n\nThese are two sequential tests. The first test calls `mockMockPedidosDelDia.mockResolvedValueOnce(['mock'])`. In the second test, `mockMockPedidosDelDia` still has the resolved value from the first test unless it was reset.\n\nWait, `beforeEach` does `vi.clearAllMocks()`. This clears all mock function calls but does NOT clear mock implementations. It calls `mockClear` on all mocks.\n\nActually, `vi.clearAllMocks()` resets `mock.calls`, `mock.instances`, `mock.contexts`, `mock.results` for all mocks. But for `vi.fn()` that's just a mock function, calling `.mockClear()` on it clears the call history.\n\nBut `mockResolvedValueOnce` is about the mock implementation, not the call history. The `mockResolvedValueOnce` is not cleared by `vi.clearAllMocks()`. \n\nActually wait, let me check vitest docs... `vi.clearAllMocks()` calls `.mockClear()` on all mocks. Actually, I think the behavior is:\n- `mockClear()` clears call history but keeps implementation\n- `mockReset()` clears call history and replaces implementation with `() => undefined`  \n- `mockRestore()` restores original implementation (not applicable for vi.fn())\n\n`vi.clearAllMocks()` = calls `mockClear()` on all mocks. So `mockResolvedValueOnce` should persist.\n\nHmm wait, but `mockResolvedValueOnce` means it resolves the next call. If the first test uses it, it's consumed. BUT wait, the mock might still have its default behavior.\n\nActually, let me re-read the code. `mockGetPedidosDelDia` is from `vi.fn()` in the mock factory:\n\n```ts\nconst mockGetPedidosDelDia = vi.fn();\nvi.mock('@/features/pedidos/services/pedidoService', () => ({\n  getPedidosDelDiaRequest: (...args: unknown[]) => mockGetPedidosDelDia(...args),\n  ...\n}));\n```\n\nSo `mockGetPedidosDelDia` is a `vi.fn()` that was defined at module level. When `vi.clearAllMocks()` runs in `beforeEach`, it calls `.mockClear()` on all vi.fn() instances. `.mockClear()` resets the call count BUT also resets the mock implementations that were set via `.mockResolvedValueOnce()`.\n\nWait, no. `.mockClear()` only resets the `.mock` property (calls, instances, results). It does NOT reset the internal implementation. But `.mockResolvedValueOnce()` sets up a one-time implementation that gets consumed when the mock is called. After consumption, the fallback is the default behavior (returning undefined).\n\nActually, I think I was confusing things. Let me look at the actual behavior:\n\n1. First test: `mockGetPedidosDelDia.mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error('fail'))` - sets up one-time rejection\n2. Then `opts.queryFn()` is called, which calls `mockGetPedidosDelDia('user-1')` - consumes the one-time rejection\n3. After this call, `mockGetPedidosDelDia` has consumed its only rejection. It still has no default implementation set (the `vi.fn()` default returns undefined).\n\n4. `beforeEach` in next test: `vi.clearAllMocks()` - this clears the call history but... hmm, does it remove the consumed `mockRejectedValueOnce`? I believe `mockClear` does remove `mockResolvedValueOnce`/`mockRejectedValueOnce` chains.\n\nActually wait, looking at the Vitest/Jest docs more carefully:\n- `.mockClear()` - Does everything `mockReset()` does (resets all instances, results, calls) but it does NOT reset the mock's implementation. However, it DOES clear any `mockResolvedValueOnce`/`mockRejectedValueOnce` configurations.\n\nNo actually, I think I'm wrong. Let me think about this more carefully.\n\nIn Jest/Vitest:\n- `mockClear()` only resets `mock.calls`, `mock.instances`, `mock.results`.\n- `mockReset()` resets everything above AND resets the implementation to an empty function (which returns `undefined`), and removes any `.mockResolvedValue()` or `.mockReturnValue()` etc.\n- `mockRestore()` does everything `mockReset()` does and restores the original implementation.\n\nSo `mockClear()` should NOT remove `mockResolvedValueOnce`. But the `mockResolvedValueOnce` was ALREADY CONSUMED in the first test. After consumption, the mock falls back to its default implementation, which for `vi.fn()` is to return `undefined`.\n\nIn the second test:\n- `mockGetPedidosDelDia.mockResolvedValueOnce(['real'])` - setup\n- `opts.queryFn()` is called\n- Inside queryFn: `getPedidosDelDiaRequest(getRepartidorId())` ÔåÆ calls `mockGetPedidosDelDia(...)` ÔåÆ resolves to `['real']` \n- Then `AsyncStorage.setItem(PEDIDOS_CACHE_KEY, JSON.stringify(data))` is called\n\nThe question is: does `AsyncStorage.setItem` work? If called with `await`, it would resolve to `undefined`, which is fine.\n\nWait, auto-mocks in vitest: vitest automatically mocks native modules when they can't be resolved.
