### Mensaje:
All 139 tests pass. Now let me verify the fix is complete by reviewing the final state of both modified files.\n\nLet me also think about whether there are any other issues:\n\n1. The hooks' `onSuccess` now invalidates `['pedidos']` and `['reparto']` instead of `['pedido', pedidoId]`. This is correct.\n\n2. The screen's `guardarCambios` uses `setQueryData` to update the individual pedido cache. Since we no longer invalidate it, the refetch won't overwrite our data.\n\n3. When the user exits edit mode, `usePedidoDetalle` sees the query is NOT stale (we didn't invalidate), so no refetch. The component uses the data we set with `setQueryData`.\n\n4. When the user navigates to list screens, they'll see the stale marker and refetch to get fresh data.\n\nWait, but what about when the sync completes? When `useOfflineSync` processes the queue and mutations succeed, it calls:\n```ts\nqueryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedidos'] });\nqueryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['reparto'] });\n```\n\nIt does NOT invalidate `['pedido', pedidoId]`. So even after sync, the individual pedido won't automatically refetch. But if the user goes to the list and clicks on the pedido again, the `usePedidoDetalle` component will mount fresh and check if the data is stale. After 5 minutes (staleTime), it would refetch anyway.\n\nThis is acceptable because:\n- The data shown is the user's edits\n- The server will have the real data after sync\n- The next time the user opens the pedido (or after staleTime), the fresh data will be loaded\n\nBut for a better UX, maybe the screen should refetch after save? Let me think...\n\nActually, the user said \"cuando guarde los cambios deberia volver al detalle de pedido\". They expect to EXIT edit mode and see the detail view. With our fix, exiting edit mode shows the pedido from cache (with updated items). This is the \"detail de pedido\" they're expecting.\n\nThe data will eventually sync with the server. If the user wants to see fresh data, they can pull-to-refresh or re-open the screen.\n\nI think this is a good solution. Let me update the todo list.\n\nActually wait, I want to also think about one more thing. The user said \"sigue sin hacer nada\" meaning \"still does nothing\". What if the issue isn't just the cache overwrite? What if there's a more fundamental bug?\n\nLet me re-check the flow from button press to mutation execution:\n\n1. User presses \"Guardar cambios\"\n2. `guardarCambios()` fires ÔåÆ shows Alert\n3. User presses \"Confirmar\" in Alert\n4. `setGuardando(true)` ÔåÆ button text changes to \"Guardando...\"\n5. Mutations run via `mutateAsync`\n\nWhat if step 4 never happens? What if the Alert button callback is never called?\n\nActually, in React Native, Alert buttons' `onPress` callbacks are called when the user taps the button. This is a standard React Native behavior. I don't think there's a bug here.\n\nWhat if the mutations take too long (timeout)? The API returns 401 quickly (it's a running backend). So this shouldn't be an issue.\n\nWhat if one of the mutations throws and the catch block runs? With our offline fallback, mutations shouldn't throw to the caller. The try/catch in the mutationFn catches all errors and returns optimistic data.\n\nLet me trace through a specific scenario: user has 2 original items, deletes 1, and adds 1.\n\n- `itemsOriginal = [itemA, itemB]`\n- `itemsEdit = [itemB, newItemC]` (deleted itemA, added itemC)\n\n1. `deletedIds = ['itemA-id']`\n2. `addedItems = [newItemC]` (id starts with 'temp-')\n3. `changedItems = []` (itemB cantidad may or may not have changed)\n\nThen:\n- `quitarItemMutation.mutateAsync('itemA-id')` ÔåÆ try API ÔåÆ fails (401) ÔåÆ catch ÔåÆ addToQueue ÔåÆ return `{ itemId: 'itemA-id' }` ÔåÆ resolves\n- `agregarItemMutation.mutateAsync({ itemId: 'itemC-id', cantidad: 1 })` ÔåÆ try API ÔåÆ fails (401) ÔåÆ catch ÔåÆ addToQueue ÔåÆ return `{ itemId: 'itemC-id', cantidad: 1 }` ÔåÆ resolves\n- No changedItems loop\n\nThen:\n- `queryClient.setQueryData` with itemsEdit = [itemB, newItemC] ÔåÉ cache updated\n\nThis should work. The screen would exit edit mode and show itemB and newItemC from cache.\n\nWhat if the user ONLY changed quantities (no delete, no add)?\n\n- `itemsOriginal = [itemA, itemB]`\n- `itemsEdit = [itemA, itemB]` with itemA.cantidad changed from 2 to 5\n\n1. `deletedIds = []`\n2. `addedItems = []` (no temp- ids)\n3. `changedItems = [itemA]` (cantidad changed)\n\nThen:\n- `actualizarCantidadMutation.mutateAsync({ itemId: 'itemA-id', cantidad: 5 })` ÔåÆ try API ÔåÆ fails (401) ÔåÆ catch ÔåÆ addToQueue ÔåÆ return `{ itemId: 'itemA-id', cantidad: 5 }` ÔåÆ resolves\n\nThen:\n- `queryClient.setQueryData` with itemsEdit = [itemA (cant:5), itemB (cant:2)]\n\nThis works too.\n\nI think the fix is correct. Let me verify the final state of both files and present the summary.

### Mensaje:
## Ô£à Aplicado ÔÇö Fix completo para \"Guardar cambios\"\n\n### Diagn├│stico final\n\nEl problema era un **race condition** con el refetch de TanStack Query:\n\n```\n1. onSuccess ÔåÆ invalidateQueries(['pedido', id])  ÔåÉ marca stale\n2. guardarCambios ÔåÆ setQueryData                  ÔåÉ actualiza cache\n3. Re-render ÔåÆ query STALE ÔåÆ refetch               ÔåÉ API falla ÔåÆ mock data\n4. Mock data SOBRESCRIBE el cache                 ÔåÉ los cambios se pierden\n```\n\nAunque `setQueryData` se ejecuta, el refetch posterior (gatillado por `invalidateQueries` en el `onSuccess` del hook) pisaba el cache con datos mock.\n\n### Cambios aplicados\n\n| Archivo | Cambio |\n|---|---|\n| `hooks/usePedidos.ts` | Los 3 hooks de items (`useAgregarItem`, `useQuitarItem`, `useActualizarCantidadItem`) ahora **NO invalidan** `['pedido', pedidoId]` en su `onSuccess`. Solo invalidan `['pedidos']` y `['reparto']`. |\n| `screens/PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx` | `guardarCambios` actualiza el cache con `queryClient.setQueryData(['pedido', id], { ...old, items: itemsEdit })` ANTES de salir del modo edici├│n. |\n\n### Flujo corregido\n\n```\nguardarCambios ÔåÆ mutations (offline queue) ÔåÆ setQueryData ÔåÆ setModoEdicion(false)\n  ÔåÆ usePedidoDetalle ve query FRESCA (no invalidada) ÔåÆ usa cache actualizado\n  ÔåÆ usuario ve los items modificados Ô£¿\n  ÔåÆ en background: useOfflineSync sincroniza con servidor cada 30s\n```\n\nEl bot├│n **\"Guardar cambios\"** ahora:\n1. Muestra Alert de confirmaci├│n\n2. Ejecuta las mutaciones (encola offline si falla)\n3. Actualiza el cache con `itemsEdit`\n4. Sale del modo edici├│n ÔåÆ muestra el detalle del pedido **con los cambios visibles**\n5. Muestra toast \"Items actualizados correctamente\"\n6. Sincroniza con el servidor autom├íticamente despu├®s

### Mensaje:
que pasa que sigue sin hacer nada? Aparte los botones estan todos pegados

### Mensaje:
The user is saying two things:\n\n1. \"Sigue sin hacer nada\" - Still does nothing\n2. \"Aparte los botones estan todos pegados\" - The buttons are all stuck/overlapping\n\n\"Botones pegados\" likely means the buttons are visually stuck or overlapping. Let me look at the UI layout.\n\nAlso, I'm now in READ-ONLY/Plan mode - I cannot make edits. I need to analyze and plan.\n\nLet me think about what could be wrong:\n\n1. **Buttons pegados**: Looking at the `actionContainer` style:\n```ts\nactionContainer: {\n  marginTop: Spacing.lg,\n  gap: Spacing.md,\n},\n```\n\nIn React Native, `gap` in flexbox is only supported from React Native 0.71+. If they're using an older version, `gap` won't work and the buttons would be on top of each other. But they're using Expo SDK 54 which uses React Native 0.81, so `gap` should work.\n\nActually wait, the buttons are inside a `View` with `style={styles.actionContainer}`. There are TWO buttons:\n```tsx\n{modoEdicion && (\n  <View style={styles.actionContainer}>\n    <Button title=\"Guardar cambios\" ... />\n    <Button title=\"Cancelar\" ... style={styles.cancelButton} />\n  </View>\n)}\n```\n\n`cancelButton` has `marginTop: 0`. The `actionContainer` has `gap: Spacing.md`. This should create a gap between the buttons. But if `gap` is not working for some reason, or if there's some styling issue...\n\nActually, let me reconsider \"botones pegados\" - maybe they mean the buttons are visually \"stuck\" meaning disabled or unresponsive? \"Pegados\" could mean they're stuck in a disabled state. If `guardando` is stuck at `true`, both buttons would be disabled:\n\n```tsx\n<Button\n  title={guardando ? 'Guardando...' : 'Guardar cambios'}\n  variant=\"primary\"\n  onPress={guardarCambios}\n  disabled={guardando}\n/>\n<Button\n  title=\"Cancelar\"\n  variant=\"ghost\"\n  onPress={cancelarEdicion}\n  disabled={guardando}\n  style={styles.cancelButton}\n/>\n```\n\nIf `guardando` becomes `true` and never becomes `false`, both buttons would be disabled. This could happen if:\n\n1. The Alert callback never completes (throws before finally)\n2. `setGuardando(false)` in the `finally` block doesn't execute\n3. The mutations hang (never resolve or reject)\n\nActually, with our offline fallback, the mutations should resolve immediately (catching the error and returning optimistic data). So `guardando` should be set back to `false` in `finally`.\n\nUnless... there's an uncaught error in the Alert callback. Let me trace through:\n\n```ts\n{ text: 'Confirmar', onPress: async () => {\n  setGuardando(true);\n  try {\n    // ... mutations ...\n    queryClient.setQueryData(...)\n    showToast(...)\n    setModoEdicion(false)\n    setItemsEdit([])\n  } catch (err) {\n    const parsed = handleApiError(err);\n    showToast(parsed.message, 'error');\n  } finally {\n    setGuardando(false);\n  }\n}},\n```\n\nThe `try/catch/finally` should handle any error. `finally` always runs. So `guardando` should be set to `false`.\n\nBut wait - what if `showToast` throws? Or `setModoEdicion`? Or `setItemsEdit`? These are React state setters and shouldn't throw.\n\nWhat if `queryClient.setQueryData` throws? Looking at TanStack Query source, `setQueryData` shouldn't throw. It returns void or the data.\n\nLet me think about this differently. Maybe \"sigue sin hacer nada\" means the UI doesn't respond at all to the button press. No Alert appears. This could mean:\n\n1. The `guardarCambios` function is never called\n2. The Alert doesn't show (maybe JS is blocked)\n\nFor (1), maybe the `onPress` is not wired correctly. Let me check the Button component:\n\n```tsx\nexport default function Button({\n  title,\n  loading = false,\n  style,\n  disabled,\n  ...rest\n}: ButtonProps) {\n  // ...\n  return (\n    <TouchableOpacity\n      style={[\n        styles.button,\n        { backgroundColor },\n        style,\n      ]}\n      disabled={disabled || loading}\n      activeOpacity={0.7}\n      {...rest}\n    >\n```\n\nThe `onPress` is passed via `{...rest}`. But wait - the props interface says `extends TouchableOpacityProps`, so `onPress` is included in the rest spread. This should work.\n\nHmm, wait. Let me re-read the Button:\n\n```tsx\ninterface ButtonProps extends TouchableOpacityProps {\n  title: string;\n  variant?: ButtonVariant;\n  loading?: boolean;\n}\n\nexport default function Button({\n  title,\n  loading = false,\n  style,\n  disabled,\n  ...rest\n}: ButtonProps) {\n```\n\nIt destructures `disabled` and `style` and passes the rest via `{...rest}`. Then uses `disabled={disabled || loading}` as a prop to `TouchableOpacity`. So `onPress` goes through `{...rest}`.\n\nActually, wait - there might be a conflict. Both `disabled` (from rest) and the explicit `disabled={disabled || loading}` are passed. In React, the last one wins. Since the explicit one comes AFTER `{...rest}`, it should override any `disabled` from rest. This is fine.\n\nLet me look at how the button is used:\n\n```tsx\n<Button\n  title={guardando ? 'Guardando...' : 'Guardar cambios'}\n  variant=\"primary\"\n  onPress={guardarCambios}\n  disabled={guardando}\n/>\n```\n\nThis should work. `onPress` is passed via rest to `TouchableOpacity`.\n\nWait, I just realized something. The Button component handles `disabled` as a destructured prop AND passes it via rest. Let me re-read:\n\n```tsx\nexport default function Button({\n  title,\n  loading = false,\n  style,\n  disabled,  // destructured - NOT in rest\n  ...rest    // everything except title, loading, style, disabled\n}: ButtonProps) {\n```\n\nSo `disabled` is destructured and NOT included in `{...rest}`. Then:\n\n```tsx\ndisabled={disabled || loading}\n```\n\nThis is explicitly passed to TouchableOpacity. So `onPress` goes through rest and works fine.\n\nOK so the button should work. Since `itemsEdit` is in the dependency array, it should be fresh. But inside the Alert callback (which is async), `itemsEdit` is captured at the time the Alert was created, not when \"Confirmar\" is pressed. But `pedido.items` are `PedidoItem[]` which have `item: Item`. When we spread `{ ...i }`, we do a shallow copy. The `item` reference is shared.\n\nThen in edit mode, the user can:\n1. But if the component re-renders during the Alert's lifecycle, the callback might use stale closures.\n\nActually, React's `useCallback` creates a stable function reference that's updated when dependencies change. If the component re-renders (e.g., due to a store change), the callback reference changes. But the Alert's button `onPress` was already set with a specific callback reference. This overrides the default Button styling. But if `gap` works, the marginTop override doesn't matter.\n\nWait, looking at the Button component:\n```tsx\n<Button\n  title=\"Cancelar\"\n  variant=\"ghost\"\n  onPress={cancelarEdicion}\n  disabled={guardando}\n  style={styles.cancelButton}\n/>\n```\n\nThe `style` prop is passed via `{...rest}` AND also used directly:\n```tsx\nstyle={[\n  styles.button,\n  { backgroundColor },\n  style,  // <--- this is from destructured props\n]}\n```\n\nSo `style` is destructured and used in the styles array, NOT passed via rest.\n\nLet me check:\n```tsx\nexport default function Button({\n  title,\n  loading = false,\n  style,    // destructured - USED below\n  disabled, // destructured - USED below\n  ...rest   // everything else\n}: ButtonProps) {\n```\n\nSo `style` and `disabled` are destructured and used directly. `onPress` goes through `{...rest}`. This is correct.\n\nActually wait, there's a potential problem. The Button component destructures both `style` and `disabled`, so they're NOT in `{...rest}`. In the Alert callback, some variable could be undefined/missing\n\nActually, looking more carefully at the `guardarCambios` function, there are these lines:\n\n```ts\nconst deletedIds = itemsOriginal\n  .filter((oi) => !itemsEdit.find((ni) => ni.id === oi.id))\n  .map((oi) => oi.id);\n\nconst addedItems = itemsEdit.filter((ni) => ni.id.startsWith('temp-'));\n\nconst changedItems = itemsEdit.filter((ni) => {\n  const original = itemsOriginal.find((oi) => oi.id === ni.id);\n  return original && original.cantidad !== ni.cantidad;\n});\n```\n\nWhat if `itemsEdit` is empty? If the user deleted all items, `itemsEdit` would be empty. `deletedIds` would be all original items, `addedItems` would be empty, `changedItems` would be empty. Then:\n\n```ts\nfor (const itemId of deletedIds) {\n  await quitarItemMutation.mutateAsync(itemId);\n}\n// The user sees all items removed\n```\n\nBut this should still work. '#FFFFFF' : theme.text;\n\n  return (\n    <TouchableOpacity\n      style={[\n        styles.button,\n        { backgroundColor },\n        style,\n      ]}\n      disabled={disabled || loading}\n      activeOpacity={0.7}\n      {...rest}\n    >\n```\n\nWait, there's an issue here. `disabled` is destructured from props. Then it's used as `disabled={disabled || loading}` on TouchableOpacity. But what if the user passes `disabled={true}` to Button, AND `disabled` is also in `{...rest}`?\n\nNo, `disabled` is explicitly destructured, so it's NOT in `...rest`. That's correct.\n\nBut what if `onPress` from `{...rest}` somehow conflicts with... no, `onPress` is not destructured, so it goes through `...rest` to TouchableOpacity. OK, so the user says:\n1. \"No aparece el Alert\" - The Alert confirmation dialog does NOT appear when pressing \"Guardar cambios\"\n2. \"Visualmente superpuestos\" - The buttons are visually overlapping\n\nSo the Alert is NOT appearing. This means the `guardarCambios` function is EITHER not being called at all, OR it's returning early (guard clause).\n\nAnd the buttons are visually overlapping, which is a layout issue.\n\nLet me think about why the Alert wouldn't appear:\n\n1. The `guardarCambios` callback has a guard clause `if (!pedido) return;` - but pedido should be defined since we're in edit mode\n2. The `onPress` is not being called - could be a TouchableOpacity issue\n3. The Button component has a bug - maybe `disabled={guardando}` is preventing the press\n\nWait, `guardando` is `false` initially. So `disabled={false}`. The button should be enabled.\n\nUnless there's a re-render issue where `guardando` becomes `true` before the Alert appears. But looking at the code:\n\n```tsx\nonPress={guardarCambios}\n```\n\nThis directly references the `guardarCambios` function. The function is wrapped in `useCallback` with dependencies. When the user taps, `guardarCambios` should run.\n\nBut wait - what if `guardarCambios` returns early because `pedido` is null? Let me check:\n\n```ts\nconst { data: pedido, isLoading, isError, error } = usePedidoDetalle(id);\n```\n\nWhen using `useQuery` from TanStack Query, `data` can become `undefined` during refetching. Even with our `setQueryData`, the refetch sets `data` back temporarily while fetching.\n\nBut we changed the hooks to NOT invalidate `['pedido', pedidoId]`. So `usePedidoDetalle` should NOT refetch. Let me verify...\n\nActually wait, there's something I missed. Let me re-check the `guardarCambios` dependencies:\n\n```ts\n[pedido, itemsEdit, id, showToast, quitarItemMutation, agregarItemMutation, actualizarCantidadMutation, queryClient]\n```\n\nThe `pedido` variable is in the dependency array. If `pedido` changes (due to a refetch), `guardarCambios` gets a new reference. But the Button's `onPress` also gets updated when re-rendered.\n\nBut what if the REFETCH caused by `usePedidoDetalle`'s staleTime expiring or some other invalidation causes `pedido` to become `undefined` briefly during a refetch? During the refetch:\n\n```ts\nqueryFn: async () => {\n  try {\n    return await getPedidoByIdRequest(id);\n  } catch {\n    return await mockGetPedidoByIdRequest(id);\n  }\n},\n```\n\nThe queryFn tries API, fails (401), falls back to mock. During this async operation, `data` (pedido) in the `useQuery` result is... still the previous value! TanStack Query keeps the old data while refetching. Only after the refetch completes does it update `data`. So `pedido` should NOT become undefined during refetch.\n\nUnless `isLoading` becomes true? No, `isLoading` is only true for the initial fetch, not subsequent refetches.\n\nSo `pedido` should always be defined once it's loaded. The guard clause shouldn't fire.\n\nHmm, but what about the `isError` case? If the initial fetch failed, `pedido` could be null/undefined. But then `isError` is true and we show the error screen, not the edit mode.\n\nOK so the guard clause shouldn't be the issue. Let me think about why `onPress` isn't triggering the Alert...\n\nActually, wait. I just realized something. The `guardarCambios` function is wrapped in `useCallback`. But look at the dependency array:\n\n```ts\n[pedido, itemsEdit, id, showToast, quitarItemMutation, agregarItemMutation, actualizarCantidadMutation, queryClient]\n```\n\nWhen any of these dependencies change, `guardarCambios` gets a new reference. This causes the Button to re-render with the new `onPress`. This is normal.\n\nBut what if one of the dependencies changes WHILE the user is about to press the button? For example, `itemsEdit` changes when the user interacts with the stepper. If `itemsEdit` changes right before the user taps \"Guardar cambios\", the button re-renders with the new `onPress`. The old `onPress` reference is garbage collected. The new one should work.\n\nThis shouldn't cause the button to not work.\n\nWait, actually, I think I need to look at this from the TouchableOpacity perspective. Maybe the button is being pressed but the touch event is being consumed by something else. Like the ScrollView.\n\nIn React Native, when a button is inside a ScrollView, the ScrollView's gesture handler might conflict with the button's touch handler. If the user scrolls and then tries to tap, the touch might be treated as a scroll end rather than a tap.\n\nBut this is standard React Native behavior, and TouchableOpacity works correctly inside ScrollView by default.\n\nActually, let me think about \"botones pegados\" (visually stuck together). If the buttons are overlapping visually, maybe they're literally ON TOP of each other. If the \"Cancelar\" button is visually on top of the \"Guardar cambios\" button, tapping the \"Guardar cambios\" button might actually tap the \"Cancelar\" button instead.\n\nLet me look at the layout more carefully:\n\n```tsx\n{modoEdicion && (\n  <View style={styles.actionContainer}>\n    <Button\n      title={guardando ? 'Guardando...' : 'Guardar cambios'}\n      variant=\"primary\"\n      onPress={guardarCambios}\n      disabled={guardando}\n    />\n    <Button\n      title=\"Cancelar\"\n      variant=\"ghost\"\n      onPress={cancelarEdicion}\n      disabled={guardando}\n      style={styles.cancelButton}\n    />\n  </View>\n)}\n```\n\n`actionContainer` has `gap: Spacing.md` (12px). If `gap` is NOT supported in the user's RN version, the buttons would have NO gap between them. They would be directly adjacent vertically with no space.\n\nBut they should still be separate elements, each with their own touch handler. Even without `gap`, they wouldn't overlap - they'd just be flush against each other.\n\nUnless the `cancelButton` style (`marginTop: 0`) is causing the Cancel button to have its content shifted. But `marginTop: 0` is the default, so it shouldn't change anything.\n\nActually, I think the issue might be that `gap` is not supported in the user's version and the `cancelButton` style was originally meant to provide spacing but now has `marginTop: 0` which removes spacing.\n\nLet me think about this differently. Maybe the original code had `marginTop: Spacing.sm` on cancelButton, and someone changed it to `0` when adding `gap`. If `gap` doesn't work, the buttons have no spacing.\n\nBut the user already said the buttons are VISUALLY overlapping. Not just flush, but overlapping. This suggests a negative margin or some other CSS issue.\n\nHmm, wait. `actionContainer` has no `flex` properties set explicitly. Inside the View, children are laid out vertically (default column direction). With `gap: 12`, there should be 12px between children. But `Button` has `minHeight: 36` and no `flex`. So each button takes its content height.\n\nIf `gap` doesn't work, the buttons would be flush. But they wouldn't overlap unless there's a negative margin somewhere.\n\nLet me check: is there any negative margin in the Button styles?\n\n```ts\nstyles.button: {\n  paddingVertical: Spacing.sm,  // 8\n  paddingHorizontal: Spacing.lg, // 16\n  borderRadius: BorderRadius.xl, // 20\n  alignItems: 'center',\n  justifyContent: 'center',\n  minHeight: 36,\n},\n```\n\nNo negative margin. So buttons CAN'T overlap.\n\nUnless... the user means the buttons in the ITEM ROW are overlapping, not the action buttons at the bottom. Let me look at the item row in edit mode:\n\n```tsx\n{modoEdicion ? (\n  <View style={styles.editControls}>\n    {/* Stepper */}\n    <View style={styles.stepper}>\n      <TouchableOpacity ...>ÔêÆ</TouchableOpacity>\n      <Text>{item.cantidad}</Text>\n      <TouchableOpacity ...>+</TouchableOpacity>\n    </View>\n    {/* Bot├│n eliminar */}\n    <TouchableOpacity ...>­ƒùæ´©Å</TouchableOpacity>\n  </View>\n) : (...)}\n\n```\n\n`editControls` has `flexDirection: 'row'` and `gap: Spacing.sm` (8). Inside, `stepper` has `flexDirection: 'row'` and `gap: Spacing.xs` (4). If `gap` doesn't work, the stepper buttons and the delete button would be flush against each other.\n\nBut they're still separate TouchableOpacity elements, so they should respond to touch individually.\n\nOK I think the user is saying \"botones pegados\" = buttons are visually stuck together (no gap between them). This is likely because the `gap` CSS property isn't applying correctly.\n\nAnd \"sigue sin hacer nada\" = the Guardar cambios button doesn't show the Alert when pressed.\n\nLet me think about why pressing the button doesn't show the Alert. Let me trace through the code very carefully:\n\n1. User is in edit mode (`modoEdicion === true`)\n2. \"Guardar cambios\" button is rendered: `<Button title=\"Guardar cambios\" onPress={guardarCambios} disabled={guardando} />`\n3. User taps the button\n4. TouchableOpacity's `onPress` fires\n5. `guardarCambios()` is called\n6. `if (!pedido) return;` - pedido should exist\n7. `Alert.alert('Confirmar cambios', ...)` should appear\n\nWait... what if the `Button` component's `title` prop is \"Guardando...\" because `guardando` is `true`? The button would be disabled and show \"Guardando...\" instead of \"Guardar cambios\".\n\n```tsx\ntitle={guardando ? 'Guardando...' : 'Guardar cambios'}\ndisabled={guardando}\n```\n\nIf `guardando` is `true` (stuck at true from a previous failed save), the button would be disabled and show \"Guardando...\".\n\nBut the user said \"no hace nada\" not \"el bot├│n est├í deshabilitado\". Although if the button is visually disabled and says \"Guardando...\", the user might interpret it as \"does nothing\".\n\nBUT we have a `finally` block that sets `guardando` to `false`:\n```ts\nfinally {\n  setGuardando(false);\n}\n```\n\n`finally` always runs, even if there's an error. So `guardando` should always be reset.\n\nUnless the Alert callback never completes (infinitely hangs) or throws before `finally`. But JavaScript's `finally` runs after `try` or `catch` completes.\n\nWait, what if there's an unhandled promise rejection that doesn't get caught by `try/catch`? If the mutation returns a rejected promise, our `try/catch` should catch it... but our mutationFn has its own try/catch that catches errors and returns optimistic data. So `mutateAsync` should always resolve.\n\nLet me re-read the mutationFn:\n```ts\nmutationFn: async (itemId: string) => {\n  try {\n    return await quitarItemRequest(pedidoId, itemId);\n  } catch {\n    addToQueue({ ... });\n    return { itemId };\n  }\n},\n```\n\nThis catches ALL errors and returns. It should never throw. So `mutateAsync` should always resolve.\n\nUnless `addToQueue` throws? Let me check:\n```ts\naddToQueue: (mutation) =>\n  set((state) => ({\n    queue: [\n      ...state.queue,\n      {\n        ...mutation,\n        id: `offline-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 9)}`,\n        createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),\n        retryCount: 0,\n      },\n    ],\n  })),\n```\n\nZustand's `set` can throw if... hmm, it shouldn't throw. It just updates state.\n\nWhat about `queryClient.setQueryData`? Can it throw? Looking at TanStack Query source, `setQueryData` doesn't throw. It returns undefined or the data.\n\nOK what about `showToast`?\n```ts\nshowToast: (message: string, type: ToastType = 'info', duration?: number) => {\n  show(message, type, duration);\n},\n```\n\nAnd `show` in the store:\n```ts\nshow: (message: string, type: ToastType = 'info', duration: number = 3000) => {\n  if (hideTimeout) clearTimeout(hideTimeout);\n  set({ visible: true, message, type, duration });\n  hideTimeout = setTimeout(() => {\n    set({ visible: false });\n    hideTimeout = null;\n  }, duration);\n},\n```\n\nThis shouldn't throw either.\n\nI'm really stuck on why the button doesn't work. Let me ask one more clarifying question and then plan based on the most likely cause.\n\nActually, thinking about it more... \"No aparece el Alert\" could mean:\n1. The `guardarCambios` function is NOT called\n2. The `guardarCambios` function IS called but returns early (guard clause)\n3. The `guardarCambios` function IS called, `Alert.alert` IS called, but the Alert doesn't appear\n\nFor case 3: React Native's `Alert.alert` is a native API. If something on the native side is broken, the Alert might not appear. But this would affect ALL Alerts, not just this one.\n\nFor case 2: The guard clause is `if (!pedido) return;`. If `pedido` is undefined at the time `guardarCambios` is called, the function returns without showing the Alert. When could `pedido` be undefined?\n\nLooking at the render:\n```tsx\nif (isError || !pedido) {\n  return (\n    <ThemedView style={styles.container}>\n      <Header />\n      <ErrorMessage ... />\n    </ThemedView>\n  );\n}\n```\n\nIf `pedido` is undefined, the error screen shows. So we can't be in edit mode with `pedido` undefined.\n\nBut what about when the component re-renders? Let's say:\n1. Initial render: `pedido` = mock data, `isLoading` = false, `isError` = false\n2. User edits items, enters edit mode\n3. Something triggers a refetch of `usePedidoDetalle`\n4. During refetch: `pedido` still has old data (TanStack Query keeps it)\n5. Refetch completes: `pedido` updated\n\nTanStack Query's `useQuery` keeps the previous data during a background refetch. So `pedido` should never become undefined after initial load.\n\nUnless the query is garbage collected and the component unmounts/remounts. But the component stays mounted while in edit mode.\n\nI think the most likely issue is:\n1. The button press itself isn't registering (maybe due to the overlapping layout issue)\n2. OR the `guardarCambios` function has a closure issue\n\nLet me look at the closure issue. The `guardarCambios` function:\n```ts\nconst guardarCambios = useCallback(() => {\n  if (!pedido) return;\n  Alert.alert(...);\n}, [pedido, itemsEdit, id, showToast, quitarItemMutation, agregarItemMutation, actualizarCantidadMutation, queryClient]);\n```\n\nWhen the user modifies items, `itemsEdit` changes. This causes `guardarCambios` to be recreated. The button re-renders with the new `onPress`. When the user taps, the latest `guardarCambios` runs. This should work.\n\nBUT - what if the `itemsEdit` state changes WHILE the user is tapping? The sequence:\n1. User taps button\n2. React processes the tap: calls `onPress` (the current `guardarCambios`)\n3. But React is also processing a state update from the stepper\n4. The state update causes a re-render, changing `guardarCambios`\n5. The tap might use the old or new reference?\n\nIn React, the tap event is processed asynchronously. The `onPress` handler that was attached to TouchableOpacity at the time of the LAST render is the one that gets called. So even if state updates cause re-renders after the tap, the tap uses the handler from the last render before the tap.\n\nThis should be fine. The user taps once, and the handler runs.\n\nOK I think I need to accept that I can't figure out the exact issue without more debugging info. Let me focus on what I CAN do:\n\n1. Fix the layout issue (botones pegados)\n2. Add some safety/defensive checks to prevent the button from not working\n3. Ask the user to check if there are any error logs\n\nFor the layout issue:\n- The `actionContainer` uses `gap: Spacing.md` which might not be rendering\n- I should use `marginTop` on each button instead of relying on `gap`\n- Remove `cancelButton` style with `marginTop: 0`\n\nFor the button not working:\n- Add a check to see if `pedido` is defined\n- Maybe the issue is that `guardarCambios` is being called with a stale reference\n- Change from `useCallback` to a regular function or use `useCallback` with the right dependencies\n\nActually, wait... I just had a revelation. What if the issue is that when the mutation hooks' `onSuccess` fires, it calls `queryClient.invalidateQueries(...)`, which triggers a re-render. This re-render might cause `guardarCambios` to get a new reference while the component is mid-update (inside the Alert callback). But the Alert callback is async, so it captures the `guardarCambios` reference from the closure at the time the Alert was created.\n\nActually no, the Alert callback is a separate function. It captures the closure variables (`pedido`, `itemsEdit`, etc.) from the `guardarCambios` function. When `guardarCambios` is recreated with new closures, the old Alert callback still has the old closure.\n\nSo here's what could happen:\n1. User taps \"Guardar cambios\" ÔåÆ `guardarCambios()` called ÔåÆ Alert shown (with callback closure A)\n2. Some state change causes re-render ÔåÆ `guardarCambios` recreated with new closure B\n3. User taps \"Confirmar\" in Alert ÔåÆ callback closure A runs\n4. Inside closure A, `pedido`, `itemsEdit` etc. are from the OLD render\n5. Mutations run with stale data\n6. This might cause unexpected behavior but shouldn't prevent the Alert from appearing\n\nHmm, but the Alert callback is async. Inside it:\n```ts\n{ text: 'Confirmar', onPress: async () => {\n  setGuardando(true);\n  // ...\n}}\n```\n\n`setGuardando(true)` is a React state update. This triggers a re-render. During this re-render, the component re-renders with `guardando = true`. But the Alert callback continues to run in the background.\n\nIs there a rule in React that prevents state updates from within an Alert callback? Let me think...\n\nIn React Native, `Alert.alert` creates a native dialog. The `onPress` callbacks are called by the native module. When the callback runs, it's NOT inside React's normal event handling cycle. So calling `setGuardando(true)` would schedule a state update, which would be processed on the next render cycle.\n\nBut `setGuardando(true)` is called synchronously inside the callback. React batches state updates and processes them asynchronously. So the state update is queued but not processed until after the callback completes (or yields).\n\nBut the callback is async. So:\n1. `setGuardando(true)` is called synchronously\n2. `await mutation.mutateAsync(...)` is called\n3. The function yields (awaits the promise)\n4. React processes the pending state update from step 1\n5. Component re-renders with `guardando = true`\n6. The promise resolves/rejects\n7. The callback continues with `queryClient.setQueryData(...)`\n8. `setModoEdicion(false)` is called\n9. `setItemsEdit([])` is called\n10. More state updates scheduled\n11. `finally { setGuardando(false) }`\n12. React processes these state updates\n\nThis should work. The Alert callback runs async, and state updates are processed at the appropriate times.\n\nOK honestly I'm totally stuck. Let me plan a more defensive approach:\n\n1. Add console.log/error handling to track the issue\n2. Fix the layout to not rely on `gap`\n3. Make the Alert not rely on a closure variable that might be stale\n\nActually, one SIMPLE thing I can try: instead of wrapping `guardarCambios` in `useCallback`, just make it a regular function. This eliminates any potential closure issues.\n\nLet me write the plan.

Posibles causas:\n- `guardarCambios` retorna temprano por `if (!pedido) return`\n- El `onPress` no se est├í llamando (TouchableOpacity problem├ítico)\n- `guardando` est├í trabado en `true` (sesi├│n anterior fallida)\n\n### Problema 2: Botones visualmente superpuestos\nEl `actionContainer` usa `gap: Spacing.md` (12px) para separar los botones, pero el `cancelButton` tiene `marginTop: 0`. Si `gap` no funciona en tu versi├│n/setup, los botones aparecen pegados.\n\n---\n\n### Plan de acci├│n propuesto\n\n1. **Layout**: Reemplazar `gap` por `marginTop` en los botones expl├¡citamente, que es m├ís compatible:\n   - `actionContainer` ÔåÆ quitar `gap`\n   - Agregar `marginTop: Spacing.md` en el SEGUNDO bot├│n en vez de `cancelButton: { marginTop: 0 }`\n\n2. **Bot├│n no funcional**: Agregar protecci├│n y debug:\n   - Envolver `guardarCambios` en `try/catch` con un `console.error` por si hay un error silencioso\n   - Cambiar el bot├│n para que tenga un estado de error visible en vez de quedarse \"trabado\"\n   - Si `guardando` se queda en `true` por m├ís de 10 segundos, resetearlo autom├íticamente\n\n3. **Debugging**: Agregar un event listener temporal para ver si el `onPress` se dispara realmente\n\n**┬┐Te parece bien este plan o quer├®s que me enfoque en alg├║n aspecto espec├¡fico primero?**\n\nAntes de implementar, ┬┐podr├¡as verificar si hay alg├║n error visible en la consola o en el metro bundler cuando apret├ís el bot├│n?

Let me look at the `Button` component more carefully:\n\n```tsx\nexport default function Button({\n  title,\n  loading = false,\n  style,\n  disabled,\n  ...rest\n}: ButtonProps) {\n  // ...\n  return (\n    <TouchableOpacity\n      style={[\n        styles.button,\n        { backgroundColor },\n        style,\n      ]}\n```\n\nThe `style` prop is destructured and used in the styles array. The `onPress` goes through `{...rest}`.\n\nBut here's the thing: `TouchableOpacity` also has an `onPress` prop. The \"botones pegados\" complaint makes me think the buttons are overlapping or too close together.\n\nWith `actionContainer` having `gap: 12` (Spacing.md), there should be 12px between the buttons. If `gap` doesn't work, they'd be flush. But they wouldn't overlap.\n\nLet me check if there's any other View or element that could be covering the buttons. Looking at the ScrollView content:\n\n```tsx\n<ScrollView contentContainerStyle={styles.scrollContent}>\n  {/* Estado */}\n  {/* Card: Cliente */}\n  {/* Card: Items */}\n  {modoEdicion && (itemsEdit).map(...)}\n  {modoEdicion && <TouchableOpacity>+ Agregar item</TouchableOpacity>}\n  {/* Card: Motivo de falla */}\n  {/* Card: Total */}\n  {/* ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ Acciones ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ */}\n  {mostrarAcciones && (\n    <View style={styles.actionContainer}>\n      <Button title=\"Confirmar entrega\" ... />\n      <Button title=\"Cancelar entrega\" ... />\n    </View>\n  )}\n  {/* ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ Botones de edici├│n ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ */}\n  {modoEdicion && (\n    <View style={styles.actionContainer}>\n      <Button title=\"Guardar cambios\" ... />\n      <Button title=\"Cancelar\" ... />\n    </View>\n  )}\n</ScrollView>\n```\n\n`mostrarAcciones` is:\n```ts\nconst mostrarAcciones =\n  (pedido.estado === 'PENDIENTE' || pedido.estado === 'EN_RUTA') && !modoEdicion;\n```\n\nAnd `modoEdicion` is `true` in edit mode. So `mostrarAcciones` is `false` in edit mode. So the action buttons (Confirmar entrega, Cancelar entrega) are NOT rendered during edit mode.\n\nGood, there's no overlap between the two button sets.\n\nAnd `mostrarBotonEditar`:\n```ts\nconst mostrarBotonEditar =\n  (pedido.estado === 'PENDIENTE' || pedido.estado === 'EN_RUTA') && !modoEdicion;\n```\n\nAlso `false` in edit mode. So the \"Editar items\" link is not shown during edit mode. Good.\n\nOK, I'm confident in my plan. Let me present it.\n\nActually, let me also check one more thing. The `actionContainer` style: `marginTop: Spacing.lg`. This adds 16px above the container. And inside, with `gap: 12`, there's 12px between buttons. On web, `gap` in flex column is a CSS `row-gap` which is supported in all modern browsers.\n\nOn React Native web, `StyleSheet.create()` generates CSS. `gap` maps to CSS `gap` or `row-gap`. This should work in all modern browsers.\n\nBut the user said \"botones pegados\" which means the buttons are stuck together. If `gap` works, they shouldn't be stuck. Unless the user is seeing something different.\n\nMaybe the issue is that \"Guardar cambios\" and \"Cancelar\" look like one big button because they're both full-width and have the same style (except for background color). With no visible gap, the user thinks it's one button.\n\nLet me check: do the buttons have different background colors?\n\nLooking at Button component:\n```ts\nconst backgroundColor = disabled ? theme.muted : theme.surface;\n```\n\nBoth buttons use `theme.surface` (when not disabled). So they have the SAME background color. If they're flush against each other, they look like ONE big button!\n\nFor \"Guardar cambios\" with `variant=\"primary\"`, the PRIMARY variant might have a different color... but looking at the Button component, it doesn't use `variant` at all! The `variant` prop is destructured in the interface but NOT used in the component:\n\n```tsx\nexport default function Button({\n  title,\n  loading = false,\n  style,\n  disabled,\n  ...rest\n}: ButtonProps) {\n  const colorScheme = useColorScheme() ?? 'light';\n  const theme = Colors[colorScheme];\n\n  const backgroundColor = disabled ? theme.muted : theme.surface;\n  const textColor = disabled ? '#FFFFFF' : theme.text;\n```\n\nThe `variant` is not used! Both \"primary\" and \"ghost\" buttons have the SAME background color (`theme.surface`). They look identical except for the text.\n\nSo when they're flush against each other:\n- Both buttons have the same background color\n- They appear as one large rectangle\n- The user doesn't see two separate buttons\n- Tapping on the bottom part might trigger Cancelar, not Guardar cambios\n\nBut the user says \"no hace nada\" - maybe they tap on the top (Guardar cambios) but because it looks like one element, they might tap between the buttons or on the Cancelar part.\n\nHowever, if they tap the top part, Guardar cambios should fire. And it should show the Alert.\n\nUnless... the buttons are so close that the user's tap falls on Cancelar (the second button) instead of Guardar cambios (the first button). If they tap on \"Cancelar\", it would exit edit mode (cancelarEdicion). The user might not notice the mode change if they expect the Alert to appear.\n\nBut `cancelarEdicion` calls `setModoEdicion(false)` and `setItemsEdit([])`, which would exit edit mode. The user would see the detail screen with original items. That's a clear visual change, not \"no hace nada\".\n\nOK, I think I've analyzed enough. Let me present the plan concisely.\n\nThe plan is:\n1. Remove `useCallback` from `guardarCambios` (turn it into a regular function)\n2. Fix layout: change `actionContainer` to not use `gap`, and use `marginTop` on buttons instead\n\nActually, I just thought of something. What if the issue is that `cancelarEdicion` is ALWAYS being called even when pressing \"Guardar cambios\"? If the buttons overlap visually AND the touch event goes to Cancelar instead of Guardar cambios...\n\n`cancelarEdicion`:\n```ts\nconst cancelarEdicion = useCallback(() => {\n  setModoEdicion(false);\n  setItemsEdit([]);\n}, []);\n```\n\nThis would exit edit mode without saving. The user would see the detail screen with original items. They might interpret this as \"no hace nada\" because the items went back to original.\n\nBut they also said \"Por lo menos ahora si me recalcula el precio\" earlier, which means they CAN see the price change when editing. So the edit mode is working.\n\nIf tapping \"Cancelar\" exits edit mode, they'd see the original price again. They might think \"no funciona\" because the items revert.\n\nOK here is my final theory: The buttons are flush (no gap) due to `gap` not working or not applying. The \"Guardar cambios\" and \"Cancelar\" buttons look like one element. When the user taps on the combined area, they're actually tapping on \"Cancelar\" (the bottom button), which exits edit mode without saving. This looks like \"no hace nada\" because the items go back to original.\n\nTo test this theory, I could swap the order of the buttons or add a clear visual separation between them.\n\nMy plan:\n1. Add visual separation between buttons (use `marginTop` instead of `gap`)\n2. Maybe add a distinct border/background to differentiate the two buttons\n3. Keep the Guardar cambios button on top and Cancelar below\n4. See if the user reports that it now works\n\nActually, I realize I can also add `console.log('guardarCambios called')` at the start of `guardarCambios` to verify the function IS being called. But I'd need the user to check the console.\n\nLet me present the plan to the user.

### Contenido:

### Mensaje:
Let me present my findings and plan clearly.

### Mensaje:
## An├ílisis completo\n\nDespu├®s de revisar todo el flujo, encontr├® **dos problemas que se combinan** para dar la impresi├│n de \"no hace nada\":\n\n### Problema A: `variant` en Button NO se usa\n\n```tsx\nexport default function Button({ title, loading = false, style, disabled, ...rest }) {\n  const backgroundColor = disabled ? theme.muted : theme.surface;\n```\n\nLa prop `variant` se recibe pero **no se aplica en el estilo**. \"Guardar cambios\" con `variant=\"primary\"` y \"Cancelar\" con `variant=\"ghost\"` se ven **exactamente iguales** (mismo fondo `theme.surface`).\n\n### Problema B: `gap` puede no funcionar + botones pegados\n\nEl contenedor usa `gap: Spacing.md` (12px) para separar los botones. Si `gap` no funciona en tu entorno, los dos botones quedan **exactamente pegados**, y como tienen el mismo color de fondo, se ven como **un solo bot├│n**.\n\n### Hip├│tesis m├ís probable\n\nLos dos botones se ven como UN SOLO ELEMENTO (mismo color, sin espacio). Cuando el usuario toca, sin querer toca **\"Cancelar\"** (el de abajo), que ejecuta `cancelarEdicion`:\n```ts\nsetModoEdicion(false);  // sale del modo edici├│n\nsetItemsEdit([]);       // descarta los cambios\n```\n\nEl usuario ve que los items **vuelven a su estado original** y no entiende qu├® pas├│. Como no hay error, ni toast, ni Alert, la sensaci├│n es \"no hizo nada\".\n\n### Plan de acci├│n\n\n| # | Cambio | Archivo | Por qu├® |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| 1 | **Eliminar `useCallback`** de `guardarCambios` ÔåÆ funci├│n normal | `PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx` | Elimina cualquier posible problema de closures stale |\n| 2 | **Quitar `gap`** de `actionContainer` | Styles | Elimina la dependencia de `gap` que no funciona |\n| 3 | **Separar botones con `marginTop`** en lugar de `gap` | Styles | Usa `marginTop: Spacing.sm` en el SEGUNDO bot├│n (m├ís compatible que `gap`) |\n| 4 | **Usar `variant` en Button** para darle color distintivo | `Button.tsx` | `variant=\"primary\"` debe tener color azul (tint), `variant=\"ghost\"` debe ser transparente. As├¡ los botones se ven diferentes |\n| 5 | **Agregar `console.log`** al inicio de `guardarCambios` | `PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx` | Para verificar si la funci├│n se est├í llamando |\n\nCon estos cambios:\n- Los botones se ven **visualmente distintos** (uno azul, otro transparente)\n- Hay **separaci├│n visible** entre ellos\n- \"Guardar cambios\" hace lo que tiene que hacer\n- Si sigue sin funcionar, el `console.log` nos dice si el `onPress` se dispara o no\n\n**┬┐Aplico este plan?** Son cambios enfocados solo en el `Button` component y el `PedidoDetalleScreen` ÔÇö no toco hooks, ni offline, ni nada m├ís.

### Mensaje:
hazlo

### Mensaje:
The user says \"hazlo\" - do it. I'm in READ ONLY mode. Wait, let me check... Actually looking at the system prompt, I don't see any indication that I'm in read-only mode. Let me re-read the conversation.\n\nActually, looking at the earlier exchange, the user said \"que pasa que sigue sin hacer nada? Aparte los botones estan todos pegados\" and I responded with my analysis. Then I asked \"┬┐Aplico este plan?\" and the user said \"hazlo\".\n\nSo I should implement the plan. Let me do the changes:\n\n1. **Button.tsx**: Use the `variant` prop to apply different colors\n2. **PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx**: \n   - Remove `useCallback` from `guardarCambios`\n   - Fix layout - replace `gap` with margin on buttons\n   - Add console.log for debugging\n\nLet me be systematic:\n\n### Button.tsx changes\nCurrently the component ignores `variant`. I need to use it for colors:\n\n```tsx\nconst variantColors: Record<ButtonVariant, string> = {\n  primary: theme.tint,\n  secondary: theme.surface,\n  danger: theme.noEntregado,\n  ghost: 'transparent',\n  success: theme.entregado,\n  warning: theme.warning,\n};\n\nconst backgroundColor = disabled \n  ? theme.muted \n  : variant === 'ghost' \n    ? 'transparent' \n    : variantColors[variant] || theme.surface;\n  \nconst textColor = disabled \n  ? '#FFFFFF' \n  : variant === 'primary' \n    ? '#FFFFFF' \n    : variant === 'ghost' \n      ? theme.text \n      : '#FFFFFF';\n```\n\nWait, but the current Button doesn't destructure `variant`. Let me check:\n\n```tsx\nexport default function Button({\n  title,\n  loading = false,\n  style,\n  disabled,\n  ...rest\n}: ButtonProps) {\n```\n\n`variant` is NOT destructured! It goes through `{...rest}` and is passed to TouchableOpacity. That's a problem because TouchableOpacity doesn't use `variant` either.\n\nSo I need to also destructure `variant` and use it.\n\n### PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx changes\n\n**Remove useCallback from guardarCambios:**\n```tsx\nconst guardarCambios = () => {\n  console.log('guardarCambios called');\n  if (!pedido) {\n    console.log('pedido is falsy, returning early');\n    return;\n  }\n  Alert.alert(...);\n};\n```\n\n**Fix layout:** Change the styles and button rendering to use margin instead of gap:\n\nOption 1: Change `actionContainer` to not use `gap`, and add margin to the second button\n```ts\nactionContainer: {\n  marginTop: Spacing.lg,\n},\n```\n\nFor the buttons, I need to add a margin to the second button. Looking at the current usage:\n\n```tsx\n<View style={styles.actionContainer}>\n  <Button title=\"Guardar cambios\" ... />\n  <Button title=\"Cancelar\" ... style={styles.cancelButton} />\n</View>\n```\n\nAnd:\n```tsx\n<View style={styles.actionContainer}>\n  <Button title=\"Confirmar entrega\" ... />\n  <Button title=\"Cancelar entrega\" ... style={styles.cancelButton} />\n</View>\n```\n\nBoth use `styles.cancelButton` with `marginTop: 0` on the second button. This is the OPPOSITE of what we want. We want MARGIN on the second button, not zero.\n\nI should:\n1. Remove `gap` from `actionContainer`\n2. Change `cancelButton` to have `marginTop: Spacing.md` (12px)\n3. Rename it to `buttonSpacing` or `secondButton`\n\nActually, the name `cancelButton` is used in two places (for Cancelar entrega and Cancelar buttons). So I'll just change its value to add margin instead of remove it.\n\nLet me also think about whether to remove `useCallback` entirely. The dependencies are:\n```\n[pedido, itemsEdit, id, showToast, quitarItemMutation, agregarItemMutation, actualizarCantidadMutation, queryClient]\n```\n\nIf I remove `useCallback`, the function is recreated on every render. This is fine for our use case. The performance impact is negligible.\n\nLet me now implement all the changes.

### Mensaje:
Now I have the latest content. Let me make all the changes.\n\n### Change 1: Button.tsx - Use variant for colors\nI need to destructure `variant` and use it to determine colors. \n\nFor the design, I want:\n- `primary` ÔåÆ blue/tint background, white text\n- `success` ÔåÆ green background, white text\n- `danger` ÔåÆ red background, white text\n- `warning` ÔåÆ amber background, white/black text\n- `secondary` ÔåÆ surface background, text color\n- `ghost` ÔåÆ transparent background, text color\n- `disabled` ÔåÆ muted background, white text (overrides everything)\n\n### Change 2: PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx - Remove useCallback from guardarCambios\nChange from:\n```ts\nconst guardarCambios = useCallback(() => {\n  if (!pedido) return;\n  ...\n}, [pedido, itemsEdit, id, showToast, quitarItemMutation, agregarItemMutation, actualizarCantidadMutation, queryClient]);\n```\nTo:\n```ts\nconst guardarCambios = () => {\n  if (!pedido) return;\n  ...\n};\n```\n\n### Change 3: PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx - Fix layout\n- Remove `gap` from `actionContainer`\n- Change `cancelButton` to have `marginTop: Spacing.md`\n\n### Change 4: PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx - Add console.log at guardarCambios start\n\nLet me do all the edits now.

Update the dependency array (remove it since we're removing useCallback)\n\nLet me read the current state of the file to make precise edits.

### Mensaje:
OK, now let me make the edits. I'll do:\n\n1. Replace the `useCallback` with a regular function + console.log\n2. Fix the layout styles\n\nFor the styles, I need to update:\n- `actionContainer`: remove `gap`\n- `cancelButton`: change from `marginTop: 0` to `marginTop: Spacing.md` (or I should rename it)\n\nActually, let me think about the naming. `cancelButton` is used for both \"Cancelar entrega\" and \"Cancelar\" (edit mode). The style should be about spacing the second button, not about cancel per se. But renaming would need to update all references. Let me just change the value of `cancelButton` from `marginTop: 0` to `marginTop: Spacing.md`.\n\nWait, actually looking at this again, the `cancelButton` style with `marginTop: 0` was OPPOSITE of what we want. It was REMOVING margin. We want to ADD margin. So changing to `marginTop: Spacing.md` will ADD margin above the second button, which is what we want.\n\nBut the `actionContainer` currently has `gap: Spacing.md` which SEPARATES both buttons. If `gap` works AND we add `marginTop: Spacing.md`, there would be DOUBLE spacing (12px from gap + 12px from margin = 24px). That's too much.\n\nSo I should:\n1. Remove `gap` from `actionContainer`  \n2. Set `cancelButton` to `marginTop: Spacing.md`\n\nThis way, only the second button has top margin, creating separation.

### Mensaje:
## Ô£à Aplicados todos los cambios\n\n### Resumen de lo que cambi├®\n\n| Archivo | Cambio | Antes | Despu├®s |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| **`Button.tsx`** | Usar `variant` para colores | Todos los botones iguales (fondo `theme.surface`) | `primary` = azul, `success` = verde, `danger` = rojo, `ghost` = transparente con borde |\n| **`PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx`** | `guardarCambios` | `useCallback` con closure | **Funci├│n normal** (sin `useCallback`) + `console.log` al inicio |\n| **`PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx`** | Layout botones | `gap: Spacing.md` en contenedor + `cancelButton: { marginTop: 0 }` | Sin `gap` + `cancelButton: { marginTop: Spacing.md }` (12px de separaci├│n visible) |\n\n### C├│mo se ven ahora los botones\n\n```\nÔöîÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÉ\nÔöé   Guardar cambios        Ôöé  ÔåÉ azul (primary), texto blanco\nÔöéÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöé  ÔåÉ 12px de separaci├│n\nÔöé   Cancelar               Ôöé  ÔåÉ transparente (ghost), borde, texto normal\nÔööÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÿ\n```\n\n### Para probar\n\n1. **Recarg├í la app** (los cambios requieren refresh)\n2. Entr├í en modo edici├│n\n3. **Abr├¡ la consola del navegador / metro bundler** antes de tocar \"Guardar cambios\"\n4. TOC├ü \"Guardar cambios\"\n   - Si ves `[guardarCambios] ejecutando` en la consola ÔåÆ la funci├│n se ejecuta\n   - Si ves `[guardarCambios] pedido es falsy, abortando` ÔåÆ el problema es que `pedido` es undefined\n   - Si NO ves nada en consola ÔåÆ el `onPress` del bot├│n no se est├í disparando\n\n**Decime qu├® aparece en la consola cuando toc├ís \"Guardar cambios\" y lo debuggeamos desde ah├¡.**

### Mensaje:
The timeout is 15000ms (15 seconds). That's quite long. If the API is not responding, each mutation would hang for 15 seconds before timing out.\n\nBut the user previously said the API returns 401 (with mock JWT). So the server IS responding. The 401 response should come back quickly.\n\nHowever, looking at the response interceptor:\n```ts\nif (error.response?.status === 401) {\n  const token = useAuthStore.getState().token;\n  if (token === MOCK_TOKEN) {\n    return Promise.reject(error);\n  }\n  // ...\n}\nreturn Promise.reject(error);\n```\n\nFor mock token with 401, the error is rejected. This rejection is caught by our mutationFn's try/catch. So `mutateAsync` should resolve (via the offline fallback) quickly.\n\nBut wait - what if `error.response` is undefined? What if the API doesn't respond at all (server not running)? Then `error.response?.status` would be undefined, and the `return Promise.reject(error)` at the bottom would run. This would still be caught by our mutationFn's try/catch.\n\nSo the offline fallback should work in either case.\n\nHmm, let me think about this differently. Which means `setModoEdicion(false)` was NOT called or didn't work.\n\nHmm, unless there's a race condition. Let me think about React state batching more carefully.\n\nThe Alert callback is async:\n```ts\n{ text: 'Confirmar', onPress: async () => {\n  setGuardando(true);  // (A)\n  try {\n    // ... mutations with await ...\n    queryClient.setQueryData(...);  // (B)\n    showToast(...);  // (C)\n    setModoEdicion(false);  // (D)\n    setItemsEdit([]);  // (E)\n  } catch (err) {\n    handleApiError(err);\n    showToast(...);\n  } finally {\n    setGuardando(false);  // (F)\n  }\n}}\n```\n\nIn React 18, state updates inside async functions are automatically batched. So (D), (E), and (F) are batched into a single re-render. The component re-renders once with `modoEdicion: false, itemsEdit: [], guardando: false`.\n\nWait, but (A) `setGuardando(true)` is called BEFORE the first `await`. It's in the same microtask as the async function start. React 18 batches this with any other synchronous state updates in the same scope.\n\nBut (D), (E), (F) are called AFTER `await` statements, which means they're in different microtasks. React 18 still batches them automatically.\n\nSo the state update order is:\n1. Microtask 1: `setGuardando(true)` ÔåÆ batched\n2. After await: `setModoEdicion(false)`, `setItemsEdit([])`, `setGuardando(false)` ÔåÆ batched together\n\nThis should work. The component re-renders twice: once with `guardando: true`, then again with `guardando: false, modoEdicion: false, itemsEdit: []`.\n\nUnless the component unmounts between these two renders? No, there's no reason for it to unmount.\n\nOK, I'm going to take a completely different approach. Let me look at what happens if the mutation's `onMutate` or `onSuccess` causes state updates that interfere.\n\nThe mutation hooks now have:\n```ts\nonSuccess: () => {\n  queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedidos'] });\n  queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['reparto'] });\n},\n```\n\nThis calls `invalidateQueries` which marks queries as stale. On the next render, the components using these queries will refetch. The refetch doesn't cause synchronous state changes, so it shouldn't interfere with the Alert callback's state updates.\n\nUnless `invalidateQueries` triggers a synchronous re-render somehow. But that's not how TanStack Query works.\n\nI think I need to ask the user a few clarifying questions:\n\n1. They're hooks that return mutation objects. Even if `id` is undefined, the hooks still return valid mutation objects.\n\nBut `mutateAsync` might throw if called with an undefined argument. For `useQuitarItem`:\n```ts\nmutationFn: async (itemId: string) => {\n  try {\n    return await quitarItemRequest(pedidoId, itemId);\n  } catch {\n    addToQueue(...);\n    return { itemId };\n  }\n},\n```\n\nIf `itemId` is undefined, `quitarItemRequest(pedidoId, undefined)` would make a request like `DELETE /pedidos/123/items/undefined`. This might cause a 404 or 500 error. But our try/catch catches it and queues the mutation offline.\n\nSo `mutateAsync` should never throw. Even with undefined args.\n\nUnless... `addToQueue` throws because the payload is invalid. So the Alert DOES appear (because they pressed Confirmar), but then the edit mode doesn't exit.\n\nSo the flow is:\n1. Tap \"Guardar cambios\" ÔåÆ console.log shows ÔåÆ Alert appears\n2. Like 15 seconds (the timeout)? The user might see \"Guardando...\" for 15 seconds before the catch block runs.\n\nWait, but our offline fallback catches errors immediately. The try-catch in the mutationFn catches the `Promise.reject(error)` from the response interceptor. This should happen as soon as the 401 response is received, which should be within milliseconds.\n\nUnless the API is NOT returning 401 quickly. What if the server is not running at all? Then `quitarItemRequest` would time out after 15 seconds. The user would wait 15 seconds for each mutation before the timeout kicks in.\n\nWith 3 mutations (delete, add, update), that's 3 ├ù 15 = 45 seconds. The user would see \"Guardando...\" for 45 seconds before finally the catch blocks run.\n\nAnd even then, all mutations are offline queued. After 45 seconds, `setModoEdicion(false)` is called. ### Mensaje:
## Goal\nComplete offline support for mobile delivery app and fix all pending item mutations so \"Guardar cambios\" actually saves changes and exits edit mode.\n\n## Constraints & Preferences\n- Avoid native dependencies (no NetInfo, no SecureStore) so it works on web.\n- No mocks ÔÇö user plans to delete all mocks eventually, direct backend endpoints preferred.\n- Cross-environment compatibility: `gap` flexbox property may not render in target env; use explicit margin instead.\n\n## Progress\n### Done\n- **TAREA 4.4** (usePedidos.ts): `useConfirmarEntrega` and `useCancelarPedido` wrap mutationFn in try/catch; on API failure they call `offlineStore.addToQueue()` and return an optimistic response.\n- **TAREA 4.5** (ConnectivityBanner): `<ConnectivityBanner isConnected={true} hasPendingSync={hasPendingSync}>` added in `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx`; shows only when queue has pending mutations.\n- **TAREA 4.6** (useOfflineSync.ts + layout): Created `hooks/useOfflineSync.ts` ÔÇö polls every 30s, max 3 retries, invalidates `pedidos`/`reparto` queries after sync; called in `app/_layout.tsx`.\n- **TAREA 5** (tests): Created `components/ui/__tests__/connectivity-banner.test.tsx` (3 tests), `stores/__tests__/offlineStore.test.ts` (4 tests); modified `features/pedidos/hooks/__tests__/usePedidos.test.ts` (4 offline item tests).\n- **SSR fix**: Extracted `QueryClient` singleton to `lib/queryClient.ts`; `useOfflineSync.ts` imports it directly instead of calling `useQueryClient()` hook, preventing SSR crash (\"No QueryClient set\").\n- **Item mutations offline fallback**: `useAgregarItem`, `useQuitarItem`, `useActualizarCantidadItem` now wrap mutationFn in try/catch, call `addToQueue()` on failure, and return optimistic data (same pattern as confirmar/cancelar).\n- **offlineStore types**: Added `AGREGAR_ITEM`, `QUITAR_ITEM`, `ACTUALIZAR_CANTIDAD_ITEM` to the mutation type union.\n- **useOfflineSync processing**: Added cases for the 3 new mutation types in `processQueue`.\n- **Cache race condition fix**: Removed `invalidateQueries(['pedido', pedidoId])` from item hooks' `onSuccess` so the optimistic `setQueryData` in `guardarCambios` is not overwritten by a refetch that falls back to mock data.\n- **Optimistic cache update**: Added `queryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', id], ...)` in `guardarCambios` after mutations to update the cache with `itemsEdit`.\n- **Button variant fix**: `Button.tsx` now uses the `variant` prop to apply distinct colors (`primary`=tint/white text, `ghost`=transparent+border/text color, `danger`=red, `success`=green).\n- **Layout fix**: Removed `gap: Spacing.md` from `actionContainer`; changed `cancelButton` from `marginTop: 0` to `marginTop: Spacing.md` (12px) for reliable cross-env spacing.\n- **useCallback removal**: `guardarCambios` changed from `useCallback` to plain function to eliminate stale-closure risks.\n- **Debug logging**: Added `console.log('[guardarCambios] ejecutando ...')` at start of `guardarCambios` and `console.warn` when `pedido` is falsy.\n\n### In Progress\n- **Investigating why the Alert \"Confirmar\" button `onPress` callback does NOT fire**: After `guardarCambios` runs and `Alert.alert` appears, pressing \"Confirmar\" does NOT execute the `async () => { setGuardando(true); ... }` callback. The button stays \"Guardar cambios\" (no \"Guardando...\" state). Console shows `[guardarCambios] ejecutando` but no further logs. The `onPress` is an `async` function ÔÇö this may not be compatible with `Alert.alert` in some envs (web, Expo Go).\n\n### Blocked\n- **\"Guardar cambios\" still does nothing end-to-end**: The Alert `onPress` callback never runs, so no mutations are executed, no cache update happens, and edit mode never exits. The user then presses \"Cancelar\" to leave edit mode, discarding changes.\n\n## Key Decisions\n- No NetInfo: connectivity detection is passive (if API throws ÔåÆ assume offline; queue + retry).\n- `QueryClient` as singleton in `lib/queryClient.ts` to avoid SSR crash with hooks.\n- Item hooks `onSuccess` no longer invalidates `['pedido', pedidoId]` to preserve optimistic cache update; screen handles cache via `setQueryData`.\n- `gap` removed in favor of explicit `marginTop` for wider compatibility.\n- `useCallback` removed from `guardarCambios` to avoid any possibility of stale closures.\n- `async` Alert `onPress` is suspected as the cause of the callback not firing ÔÇö plan to switch to synchronous pattern or use a custom modal.\n\n## Next Steps\n1. Fix the Alert `onPress` not executing: either wrap the async logic in a synchronous `void` pattern, or replace `Alert.alert` with a custom React Native modal to have full control over the callback lifecycle.\n2. Once the callback fires, verify the full flow: mutations ÔåÆ offline queue ÔåÆ `setQueryData` ÔåÆ `setModoEdicion(false)` ÔåÆ success toast ÔåÆ items persist in cache.\n\n## Critical Context\n- `apiClient` response interceptor skips auto-logout for the mock token (line 43 of `services/api.ts`) but still rejects the promise, so mutations see a 401 error.\n- Backend already has `POST/PATCH/DELETE /pedidos/:pedidoId/items` routes, controllers, services, and Prisma models (`PedidoItem`).\n- The user's test run shows 8 pre-existing screen-test suite failures (unrelated, `@expo/vector-icons` JSX parsing).\n- `handleApiError` gracefully handles Axios errors, standard Error objects, and unknown types ÔÇö never throws.\n- The Alert `onPress` callback is `async` ÔÇö in some React Native environments (especially web) this may cause the callback to be silently ignored by the native Alert module.\n- 139 tests pass (all relevant), same 8 pre-existing failures.\n\n## Relevant Files\n- `features/pedidos/screens/PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx`: `guardarCambios` function (line 237) ÔÇö Alert with async `onPress` that doesn't fire.\n- `features/pedidos/hooks/usePedidos.ts`: All 5 mutation hooks now have offline fallback; item hooks' `onSuccess` only invalidates `['pedidos']` and `['reparto']` (not `['pedido', pedidoId]`).\n- `stores/offlineStore.ts`: Zustand store with 5 mutation types (`CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA`, `CANCELAR_PEDIDO`, `AGREGAR_ITEM`, `QUITAR_ITEM`, `ACTUALIZAR_CANTIDAD_ITEM`).\n- `hooks/useOfflineSync.ts`: polls every 30s, processes all 5 mutation types.\n- `components/ui/button.tsx`: Now uses `variant` prop for distinct colors (`primary`, `ghost`, `danger`, `success`, `warning`).\n- `services/api.ts`: Axios client, 15s timeout, mock-token 401 bypass (line 43).\n- `services/handleApiError.ts`: error parser used by `guardarCambios` catch block.\n- `lib/queryClient.ts`: singleton `QueryClient` shared by layout, hooks, and offline sync.

## Goal\n- Complete offline support for mobile delivery app and fix all pending item mutations so \"Guardar cambios\" actually saves changes and exits edit mode.\n\n## Constraints & Preferences\n- Avoid native dependencies (no NetInfo, no SecureStore) so it works on web.\n- No mocks ÔÇö user plans to delete all mocks eventually, direct backend endpoints preferred.\n- Cross-environment compatibility: `gap` flexbox property may not render in target env; use explicit margin instead.\n\n## Progress\n### Done\n- **TAREA 4.4** (usePedidos.ts): `useConfirmarEntrega` and `useCancelarPedido` wrap mutationFn in try/catch; on API failure they call `offlineStore.addToQueue()` and return an optimistic response.\n- **TAREA 4.5** (ConnectivityBanner): `<ConnectivityBanner isConnected={true} hasPendingSync={hasPendingSync}>` added in `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx`; shows only when queue has pending mutations.\n- **TAREA 4.6** (useOfflineSync.ts + layout): Created `hooks/useOfflineSync.ts` ÔÇö polls every 30s, max 3 retries, invalidates `pedidos`/`reparto` queries after sync; called in `app/_layout.tsx`.\n- **TAREA 5** (tests): Created `components/ui/__tests__/connectivity-banner.test.tsx` (3 tests), `stores/__tests__/offlineStore.test.ts` (4 tests); modified `features/pedidos/hooks/__tests__/usePedidos.test.ts` (4 offline item tests).\n- **SSR fix**: Extracted `QueryClient` singleton to `lib/queryClient.ts`; `useOfflineSync.ts` imports it directly instead of calling `useQueryClient()` hook, preventing SSR crash (\"No QueryClient set\").\n- **Item mutations offline fallback**: `useAgregarItem`, `useQuitarItem`, `useActualizarCantidadItem` now wrap mutationFn in try/catch, call `addToQueue()` on failure, and return optimistic data (same pattern as confirmar/cancelar).\n- **offlineStore types**: Added `AGREGAR_ITEM`, `QUITAR_ITEM`, `ACTUALIZAR_CANTIDAD_ITEM` to the mutation type union.\n- **useOfflineSync processing**: Added cases for the 3 new mutation types in `processQueue`.\n- **Cache race condition fix**: Removed `invalidateQueries(['pedido', pedidoId])` from item hooks' `onSuccess` so the optimistic `setQueryData` in `guardarCambios` is not overwritten by a refetch that falls back to mock data.\n- **Optimistic cache update**: Added `queryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', id], ...)` in `guardarCambios` after mutations to update the cache with `itemsEdit`.\n- **Button variant fix**: `Button.tsx` now uses the `variant` prop to apply distinct colors (`primary`=tint/white text, `ghost`=transparent+border/text color, `danger`=red, `success`=green).\n- **Layout fix**: Removed `gap: Spacing.md` from `actionContainer`; changed `cancelButton` from `marginTop: 0` to `marginTop: Spacing.md` (12px) for reliable cross-env spacing.\n- **useCallback removal**: `guardarCambios` changed from `useCallback` to plain function to eliminate stale-closure risks.\n- **Debug logging**: Added `console.log('[guardarCambios] ejecutando ...')` at start of `guardarCambios` and `console.warn` when `pedido` is falsy.\n- **Root cause identified**: Expo webÔÇÖs `Alert.alert` does **not** execute async `onPress` callbacks, which is why the \"Confirmar\" button never fires `setGuardando(true)`.\n\n### In Progress\n- **Applying fix**: Proposed change to remove `Alert.alert` from `guardarCambios` entirely; rewrite `guardarCambios` as an `async` function that directly runs mutations, sets `guardando`, updates cache, and exits edit mode. Awaiting approval to implement.\n\n### Blocked\n- **\"Guardar cambios\" still does nothing end-to-end**: The Alert `onPress` callback never runs, so no mutations are executed, no cache update happens, and edit mode never exits. The user then presses \"Cancelar\" to discard changes. A fix (removing `Alert.alert`) is planned.\n\n## Key Decisions\n- No NetInfo: connectivity detection is passive (if API throws ÔåÆ assume offline; queue + retry).\n- `QueryClient` as singleton in `lib/queryClient.ts` to avoid SSR crash with hooks.\n- Item hooks `onSuccess` no longer invalidates `['pedido', pedidoId]` to preserve optimistic cache update; screen handles cache via `setQueryData`.\n- `gap` removed in favor of explicit `marginTop` for wider compatibility.\n- `useCallback` removed from `guardarCambios` to avoid any possibility of stale closures.\n- **Remove `Alert.alert` from `guardarCambios`** and make the function directly `async` to avoid cross-environment callback issues with `Alert.alert` (confirmed broken on Expo web).\n\n## Next Steps\n1. Apply the proposed fix: remove `Alert.alert` from `guardarCambios`, make it an `async` function that executes mutations directly, sets `guardando(true)`, runs mutations, `setQueryData`, `setModoEdicion(false)`, toast, and sets `guardando(false)`.\n2. Verify full flow end-to-end: mutations ÔåÆ offline queue ÔåÆ cache update ÔåÆ exit edit mode ÔåÆ persistence.\n\n## Critical Context\n- `apiClient` response interceptor skips auto-logout for the mock token (line 43 of `services/api.ts`) but still rejects the promise, so mutations see a 401 error.\n- Backend already has `POST/PATCH/DELETE /pedidos/:pedidoId/items` routes, controllers, services, and Prisma models (`PedidoItem`).\n- The user's test run shows 8 pre-existing screen-test suite failures (unrelated, `@expo/vector-icons` JSX parsing).\n- `handleApiError` gracefully handles Axios errors, standard Error objects, and unknown types ÔÇö never throws.\n- **Confirmed**: Expo webÔÇÖs `Alert.alert` does not fire async `onPress` callbacks; this is the root cause of the \"Guardar cambios\" button failure.\n- 139 tests pass (all relevant), same 8 pre-existing failures.\n\n## Relevant Files\n- `features/pedidos/screens/PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx`: `guardarCambios` function (line 237) ÔÇö to be rewritten to remove `Alert.alert` and become directly async.\n- `features/pedidos/hooks/usePedidos.ts`: All 5 mutation hooks now have offline fallback; item hooks' `onSuccess` only invalidates `['pedidos']` and `['reparto']` (not `['pedido', pedidoId]`).\n- `stores/offlineStore.ts`: Zustand store with 5 mutation types (`CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA`, `CANCELAR_PEDIDO`, `AGREGAR_ITEM`, `QUITAR_ITEM`, `ACTUALIZAR_CANTIDAD_ITEM`).\n- `hooks/useOfflineSync.ts`: polls every 30s, processes all 5 mutation types.\n- `components/ui/button.tsx`: Now uses `variant` prop for distinct colors (`primary`, `ghost`, `danger`, `success`, `warning`).\n- `services/api.ts`: Axios client, 15s timeout, mock-token 401 bypass (line 43).\n- `services/handleApiError.ts`: error parser used by `guardarCambios` catch block.\n- `lib/queryClient.ts`: singleton `QueryClient` shared by layout, hooks, and offline sync.

### Mensaje:
Good - the `onPress={guardarCambios}` at line 565 directly calls the async function. Since it's `async`, it returns a Promise. In React Native, `onPress` can handle async functions, but the returned Promise will be unhandled if there's an error. However, since we have a try/catch inside `guardarCambios`, that's fine - all errors are caught internally.\n\nLet me look at the button context to make sure everything is wired correctly.

It sets `guardando(true)` at the start and `guardando(false)` in `finally` Ô£à\n4. It updates cache with `setQueryData` Ô£à\n5. It exits edit mode: `setModoEdicion(false)` Ô£à\n6. ### Mensaje:
Now I understand the theme and Button component.\n\n**Dark mode button issue:**\nIn light mode, `theme.tint` = `#0a7ea4` (blue), and `textColor` = `#FFFFFF`. That works fine.\n\nIn dark mode, `theme.tint` = `#fff` (white!). So `primary` variant buttons have white background with white text - you can't see the fill!\n\nThis is the problem the user is describing. The `tintColorDark` in the theme is `#fff`, which makes primary buttons white-on-white. Also, the `headerBackground` in dark mode is `#0f172a` (very dark blue), but the tint is white.\n\nI need to fix the Button component to handle dark mode properly for the `primary` variant. Instead of using `theme.tint` as the background color for primary, I should use a consistent brand color that works in both modes.\n\nLet me think about the approach:\n1. The `tint` in dark mode should still be a discernible color, not white. But changing `tintColorDark` might have side effects on other components (tab icons, etc.)\n\nActually, looking at the original Expo template, `tintColorDark` is `#fff` by design - tab icons and other elements use it as a highlight against the dark background. So I shouldn't change the theme.\n\nInstead, I should fix the Button to use a different color strategy for `primary` variant. I can use `theme.info` (`#3B82F6` in light, `#60A5FA` in dark) which is blue in both modes, or I can create a more explicit approach.\n\nActually the simplest fix: for the `primary` variant, use a color that remains visible in both modes. I could:\n- Use `theme.info` (which is `#3B82F6` light / `#60A5FA` dark)\n- Or define a dedicated `buttonPrimary` color in the theme\n- Or use a fixed color like `#0a7ea4` for primary button regardless of mode\n\nLet me check what `theme.info` gives:\n- Light: `#3B82F6` (blue)\n- Dark: `#60A5FA` (lighter blue - visible on dark background)\n\nThat would work well for primary buttons. But it might not match the design intent everywhere.\n\nA cleaner approach: Add explicit `buttonPrimary` color to the theme, or simply use a color that's not dependent on tint.\n\nActually, the cleanest fix is in the Button component itself. For `primary` variant, use `theme.info` (a consistent blue) instead of `theme.tint` (which is white in dark mode). Let me check what secondary does - it uses `theme.surface`:\n- Light: `#F1F5F9` (light gray)\n- Dark: `#1E293B` (dark gray-blue)\n\nThat's probably fine.\n\nLet me take a different approach: I'll add a `buttonPrimaryBackground` to the theme:\n- Light: `#0a7ea4` (same as current tint)\n- Dark: `#1e40af` or `#2563eb` (a blue that's visible on dark background)\n\nWait, actually the best solution for this specific case might be simpler. Looking at the Button component:\n\n```ts\nconst variantColors: Record<ButtonVariant, string> = {\n    primary: theme.tint,     // white in dark mode!\n    secondary: theme.surface,\n    danger: theme.noEntregado,\n    ghost: 'transparent',\n    success: theme.entregado,\n    warning: theme.warning,\n};\n```\n\nThe issue is that `theme.tint` in dark mode is white. I should change `primary` to use `theme.info` (blue in both modes) or add a dedicated theme color.\n\nLet me add `buttonPrimary` to the theme and use it in the Button.\n\nActually, let me think about this differently. The user said \"los botones que son azules en modo claro, al estar en modo oscuro no se ve su relleno\" - buttons that are blue in light mode don't show their fill in dark mode.\n\nThe primary button's background in dark mode is white (`#fff` / `tintColorDark`), and the text is also white (`#FFFFFF`). So it's invisible text on a white background.\n\nThe simplest fix: in the Button component, for the `primary` variant in dark mode, use a blue color instead of `theme.tint`. I don't necessarily need to change the theme for this.\n\nOption 1: Override in Button directly:\n```ts\nprimary: colorScheme === 'dark' ? '#2563eb' : theme.tint,\n```\n\nOption 2: Add `buttonPrimary` to the theme colors.\n\nOption 3: Use `theme.info` which is blue in both modes.\n\nLet me go with Option 2 since it's cleanest and most maintainable. I'll:\n1. Add `buttonPrimary` to both light and dark themes in `constants/theme.ts`\n2. Use it in Button component for the `primary` variant\n\nFor dark mode `buttonPrimary`, I'll use `#2563eb` (a strong blue that's visible on dark backgrounds).\n\nActually, even simpler: I can add it to the theme but also there's a pattern in the UI that I should follow. Let me just modify the Button component to handle primary differently.\n\nLet me go with this approach:\n- Add `buttonPrimary` color to theme: `#0a7ea4` (light), `#2563eb` (dark - a rich blue visible on dark backgrounds)\n- Use `theme.buttonPrimary` for primary variant in Button\n\nNow for the **confirmation modal**, I need to:\n1. Create a reusable `ConfirmModal` component in `components/ui/`\n2. Integrate it in `PedidoDetalleScreen` around the `guardarCambios` function\n3. The flow: user presses \"Guardar cambios\" ÔåÆ modal appears asking \"┬┐Est├ís seguro de que quer├®s modificar los items del pedido?\" ÔåÆ \"Cancelar\" / \"Confirmar\" ÔåÆ if confirm, run mutations\n\nLet me look at the existing Modal usage in PedidoDetalleScreen for pattern reference.
