### Contenido:
import { Modal, StyleSheet, Text, View } from 'react-native';\nimport { useColorScheme } from '@/hooks/use-color-scheme';\nimport { Colors, BorderRadius, Spacing, FontSizes } from '@/constants/theme';\nimport Button from './button';\n\ninterface ConfirmModalProps {\n  visible: boolean;\n  title: string;\n  message: string;\n  confirmText?: string;\n  cancelText?: string;\n  onConfirm: () => void;\n  onCancel: () => void;\n  loading?: boolean;\n  variant?: 'primary' | 'danger';\n}\n\nexport default function ConfirmModal({\n  visible,\n  title,\n  message,\n  confirmText = 'Confirmar',\n  cancelText = 'Cancelar',\n  onConfirm,\n  onCancel,\n  loading = false,\n  variant = 'primary',\n}: ConfirmModalProps) {\n  const colorScheme = useColorScheme() ?? 'light';\n  const theme = Colors[colorScheme];\n\n  return (\n    <Modal\n      visible={visible}\n      transparent\n      animationType=\"fade\"\n      onRequestClose={onCancel}\n    >\n      <View style={[styles.overlay, { backgroundColor: 'rgba(0,0,0,0.5)' }]}>\n        <View\n          style={[\n            styles.content,\n            { backgroundColor: theme.background },\n          ]}\n        >\n          <Text style={[styles.title, { color: theme.text }]}>\n            {title}\n          </Text>\n          <Text style={[styles.message, { color: theme.muted }]}>\n            {message}\n          </Text>\n          <View style={styles.actions}>\n            <Button\n              title={cancelText}\n              variant=\"ghost\"\n              onPress={onCancel}\n              disabled={loading}\n              style={styles.actionButton}\n            />\n            <Button\n              title={loading ? 'Guardando...' : confirmText}\n              variant={variant === 'danger' ? 'danger' : 'primary'}\n              onPress={onConfirm}\n              loading={loading}\n              style={styles.actionButton}\n            />\n          </View>\n        </View>\n      </View>\n    </Modal>\n  );\n}\n\nconst styles = StyleSheet.create({\n  overlay: {\n    flex: 1,\n    justifyContent: 'center',\n    alignItems: 'center',\n    padding: Spacing.xl,\n  },\n  content: {\n    width: '100%',\n    maxWidth: 380,\n    borderRadius: BorderRadius.lg,\n    padding: Spacing.xxl,\n  },\n  title: {\n    fontSize: FontSizes.lg,\n    fontWeight: 'bold',\n    marginBottom: Spacing.sm,\n    textAlign: 'center',\n  },\n  message: {\n    fontSize: FontSizes.md,\n    marginBottom: Spacing.xxl,\n    textAlign: 'center',\n    lineHeight: 22,\n  },\n  actions: {\n    flexDirection: 'row',\n    justifyContent: 'center',\n    gap: Spacing.md,\n  },\n  actionButton: {\n    flex: 1,\n  },\n});\n

Since I added `buttonPrimary` to both, it works.\n\nNow let me integrate the ConfirmModal into PedidoDetalleScreen. I need to:\n\n1. Add import for `ConfirmModal`\n2. Add `showConfirmModal` state\n3. ### Mensaje:
## Hecho Ô£à\n\n### 1. Modal de confirmaci├│n custom (`components/ui/confirm-modal.tsx`)\n\nNuevo componente reutilizable que reemplaza el `Alert.alert`:\n\n- Usa `Modal` nativo con overlay centrado (no slide desde abajo como los otros modals)\n- Props: `visible`, `title`, `message`, `confirmText`/`cancelText`, `onConfirm`/`onCancel`, `loading`, `variant`\n- Tiene tema aplicado (fondo, texto, colores seg├║n dark/light mode)\n- El bot├│n de confirmar es `variant=\"primary\"` por defecto, o `\"danger\"` si se necesita\n\n**Integraci├│n en `PedidoDetalleScreen`:**\n- `guardarCambios` ahora solo hace `setShowConfirmModal(true)`\n- La l├│gica de mutations se movi├│ a `handleConfirmGuardar`, que se ejecuta cuando el usuario confirma\n- El modal se cierra autom├íticamente al presionar \"Confirmar\"\n\n### 2. Botones azules visibles en dark mode\n\n**Problema:** `theme.tint` en dark mode es `#fff`, entonces los botones `primary` ten├¡an fondo blanco con texto blanco ÔåÆ invisibles.\n\n**Soluci├│n:** Agregu├® `buttonPrimary` al tema:\n- **Light:** `#0a7ea4` (mismo azul que antes)\n- **Dark:** `#2563eb` (azul vibrante visible sobre fondo oscuro)\n\nEl `Button` ahora usa `theme.buttonPrimary` en lugar de `theme.tint` para el variant `primary`.\n\n### 3. Tests\n\n**139 tests pasan** (8 failures pre-existentes de `@expo/vector-icons`).

Found the issues:\n\n1. **PedidosListScreen.tsx** (list screen with \"Agregar pedido\" FAB): The add button uses `theme.tint` as background color - which is white in dark mode! That's the \"agregar pedido\" button.\n\n2. **PedidoAltaScreen.tsx** (form screen): The submit button uses styles but doesn't set a variant. Let me look at how Button is used there.\n\nLet me read both files to see the full picture.

Now I can see the issues:\n\n1. **PedidosListScreen.tsx** (line 90): The \"+\" FAB button uses `theme.tint` as background. In dark mode, tint is `#fff`, so the button is white with a white \"+\" text. Fix: use `theme.buttonPrimary` instead.\n\n2. **PedidoAltaScreen.tsx** (line 324-328): The \"Crear Pedido\" Button doesn't specify a `variant`, so it defaults to `'primary'`. That means it uses `theme.buttonPrimary` now (after my fix). But wait - let me check... The Button component defaults to `variant = 'primary'`, and I already changed primary to use `theme.buttonPrimary` which is `#2563eb` in dark mode. So this should actually be fine now! Unless the PedidoAltaScreen Button import is different...\n\nWait, let me check the import:\n```ts\nimport { Button, Header, LoadingSpinner, ErrorMessage } from '@/components/ui';\n```\nYes, it uses the same Button component, so the \"Crear Pedido\" button should now use `theme.buttonPrimary` which is `#2563eb` in dark mode. That should be visible.\n\nBut the user says it's still not visible. Let me think about what else could be wrong...\n\nOh wait - the user said \"los botones dentro del alta de pedido\". But let me confirm by checking the Button component defaults...\n\nYes, looking at button.tsx:\n```ts\nexport default function Button({\n  title,\n  variant = 'primary',\n  ...\n}: ButtonProps) {\n  ...\n  const variantColors: Record<ButtonVariant, string> = {\n    primary: theme.buttonPrimary,  // ÔåÉ Already fixed\n```\n\nSo \"Crear Pedido\" should be using `theme.buttonPrimary` = `#2563eb` in dark mode. That should be visible. Unless the user is referring to other buttons...\n\nActually, the user said \"el boton de agregar pedido y los botones dentro del alta de pedido\". Let me think about what \"botones dentro del alta de pedido\" means. Looking at PedidoAltaScreen:\n1. \"Crear Pedido\" - variant=\"primary\" ÔåÆ theme.buttonPrimary ÔåÆ OK\n2. \"+\" and \"ÔêÆ\" stepper buttons - use theme.surface ÔåÆ should be visible\n3. \"Agregar\" button next to unselected items - uses theme.tint ÔåÆ BROKEN in dark mode!\n\nSo the fix is:\n1. `PedidosListScreen.tsx` line 90: `theme.tint` ÔåÆ `theme.buttonPrimary`\n2. `PedidoAltaScreen.tsx` line 282: `theme.tint` ÔåÆ `theme.buttonPrimary`\n\nLet me make both changes.

Let me search for this pattern.\n\nActually, let me also check if the `theme.tint` usage in other screens might have the same issue. The key patterns to look for are:\n- `backgroundColor: theme.tint` - used as button background\n- `color: theme.tint` - used as text color on a background (this is fine since tint is white in dark mode, and would need a dark background)\n\nFor background colors, `theme.tint` being white in dark mode makes elements invisible. But `theme.tint` as text color on a dark background is actually fine (it would show as white text).\n\nLet me search for `backgroundColor.*theme.tint` across the codebase to catch all instances.

When present, render a `<` button on the left side.\n\n2. **Screens that need back button:**\n   - `PedidoDetalleScreen` (used by both `pedidos/[id]` and `mapa/[id]`) ÔåÆ back to list\n   - `PedidoAltaScreen` ÔåÆ back to pedidos list\n\n3. Screens that do NOT need back button:\n   - `PedidosListScreen` (root of pedidos tab)\n   - `MapaScreen` (root of mapa tab)\n   - `InicioScreen` (root of inicio tab)\n   - `RepartosListScreen` (root of repartos tab)\n   - `PerfilScreen` (root of perfil tab)\n   - `LoginScreen` (standalone)\n\nLet me update the Header component to add a back button, then update the screens that need it.\n\nThe Header currently has:\n```tsx\n<View style={[styles.header, { backgroundColor: theme.headerBackground }]}>\n  <Text style={[styles.title, { color: theme.headerText }]}>{title}</Text>\n</View>\n```\n\nI need to change it to:\n```tsx\n<View style={[styles.header, { backgroundColor: theme.headerBackground }]}>\n  {onBack && (\n    <TouchableOpacity onPress={onBack} style={styles.backButton}>\n      <Text style={[styles.backButtonText, { color: theme.headerText }]}>{'<'}</Text>\n    </TouchableOpacity>\n  )}\n  <Text style={[styles.title, { color: theme.headerText }]}>{title}</Text>\n</View>\n```\n\nBut wait, in Expo Router, the standard way to go back is `router.back()`. So I should import `router` from `expo-router` inside the Header? No, that would be a dependency on expo-router in a UI component. Better to pass `onBack` as a prop and let the screen decide what to do.\n\nActually, even better: I can make the Header component accept `showBack` as a boolean, and internally use `router.back()` when the back button is pressed. This keeps it simple since all back buttons do the same thing.\n\nBut that creates a dependency on expo-router in the UI component. Let me just pass `onBack` and have the screens pass `() => router.back()`.\n\nHmm, actually the simplest approach: I'll pass `onBack` as an optional prop. The screens that need back navigation will pass `onBack={() => router.back()}`.\n\nLet me also think about the icon. The user said `<` which is just the less-than character. I'll use that as text since we're avoiding icon dependencies (that's what broke the tests). Actually, let me use a unicode arrow like `ÔåÉ` or the chevron `ÔÇ╣` or just `<`. The user said `<` so I'll use that.\n\nWait, actually I should consider if I want to use a nicer icon. But since `@expo/vector-icons` causes test issues, I'll just use text. A simple `<` in a bold font looks fine.\n\nLet me also think about layout - with the back button, the header title should still be centered. Let me use `position: 'absolute'` on the back button or use a flex row with the title centered.\n\nHere's my approach:\n- The header container uses `flexDirection: 'row'` with `justifyContent: 'center'`\n- The back button is positioned absolutely on the left\n- The title is centered\n\nOr simpler:\n- The header is a row\n- Back button on the left, title centered, and a spacer on the right for balance\n\nLet me look at how the header currently renders and think about the best layout.\n\nCurrent layout:\n```\n<View style={[styles.header, { backgroundColor: theme.headerBackground }]}>\n  <Text style={[styles.title, { color: theme.headerText }]}>{title}</Text>\n</View>\n```\n\nWith back button:\n```\n<View style={[styles.header, { backgroundColor: theme.headerBackground }]}>\n  {onBack && (\n    <TouchableOpacity onPress={onBack} style={styles.backButton}>\n      <Text style={[styles.backText, { color: theme.headerText }]}>{'<'}</Text>\n    </TouchableOpacity>\n  )}\n  <Text style={[styles.title, { color: theme.headerText }]}>{title}</Text>\n  {/* Spacer to keep title centered when back button is present */}\n  {onBack && <View style={styles.backButton} />}\n</View>\n```\n\nActually, simpler - just use absolute positioning for the back button:\n\n```\n<View style={[styles.header, { backgroundColor: theme.headerBackground }]}>\n  <Text style={[styles.title, { color: theme.headerText }]}>{title}</Text>\n  {onBack && (\n    <TouchableOpacity onPress={onBack} style={styles.backButton}>\n      <Text style={[styles.backText, { color: theme.headerText }]}>{'<'}</Text>\n    </TouchableOpacity>\n  )}\n</View>\n```\n\nWith absolute positioning on the back button. Let me implement this.\n\nLet me write the updated Header component.

### Contenido:
import { StyleSheet, Text, TouchableOpacity, View } from 'react-native';\nimport { Colors, Spacing, FontSizes } from '@/constants/theme';\nimport { useColorScheme } from '@/hooks/use-color-scheme';\n\ninterface HeaderProps {\n  title?: string;\n  onBack?: () => void;\n}\n\nexport default function Header({ title = 'SupplyCycle', onBack }: HeaderProps) {\n  const colorScheme = useColorScheme() ?? 'light';\n  const theme = Colors[colorScheme];\n\n  return (\n    <View style={[styles.header, { backgroundColor: theme.headerBackground }]}>\n      {onBack && (\n        <TouchableOpacity onPress={onBack} style={styles.backButton} activeOpacity={0.7}>\n          <Text style={[styles.backIcon, { color: theme.headerText }]}>{'<'}</Text>\n        </TouchableOpacity>\n      )}\n      <Text\n        style={[\n          styles.title,\n          { color: theme.headerText },\n          onBack && styles.titleWithBack,\n        ]}\n        numberOfLines={1}\n      >\n        {title}\n      </Text>\n    </View>\n  );\n}\n\nconst styles = StyleSheet.create({\n  header: {\n    flexDirection: 'row',\n    alignItems: 'center',\n    justifyContent: 'center',\n    paddingTop: Spacing.xxl,\n    paddingBottom: Spacing.lg,\n    paddingHorizontal: Spacing.xl,\n  },\n  backButton: {\n    position: 'absolute',\n    left: Spacing.lg,\n    bottom: Spacing.lg,\n    width: 36,\n    height: 36,\n    borderRadius: 18,\n    justifyContent: 'center',\n    alignItems: 'center',\n  },\n  backIcon: {\n    fontSize: 22,\n    fontWeight: '700',\n  },\n  title: {\n    fontSize: FontSizes.xl,\n    fontWeight: 'bold',\n    flexShrink: 1,\n  },\n  titleWithBack: {\n    marginHorizontal: 40,\n  },\n});\n

### Mensaje:
a que se refiere con que los items se encolan offline porque el mock JWT 401 lo rechaza? que seria hacerlo real?

### Mensaje:
The user is asking me to explain a statement from our earlier summary: \"los items se encolan offline porque el mock JWT 401 rechaza las mutations\". They want to understand what this means and what it would take to make it \"real\" (i.e., connect to the actual backend instead of using mocks).\n\nLet me explain the situation clearly based on what I've seen in the codebase.\n\nThe flow is:\n1. The app uses a mock JWT token for authentication\n2. When mutations (like confirmar entrega, agregar item, etc.) are executed, they call the backend API\n3. The backend API has an auth middleware that validates the JWT\n4. The mock JWT token is not a real signed JWT, so the backend rejects it with a 401\n5. The apiClient interceptor catches the 401... but it has a special case to NOT auto-logout for mock tokens (line 43 of api.ts)\n6. However, the request still fails with a 401, so the mutation hooks catch the error\n7. The offline fallback in the mutation hooks kicks in: they call `offlineStore.addToQueue()` and return an optimistic response\n8. The mutation is queued for later sync\n\nTo make it \"real\", the app would need:\n1. A real backend running (Express + Prisma + PostgreSQL)\n2. Real authentication (login returns a real JWT signed by the backend)\n3. The API calls would work against the real backend endpoints\n4. The offline queue would only be used when the device is actually offline (no network), not as a fallback for auth failures\n\nLet me also look at the api.ts interceptor to check the mock token bypass.

### Mensaje:
Let me also look at the auth flow to understand how the mock token is generated and used.

### Mensaje:
Now I have the complete picture to explain to the user. Let me summarize clearly:\n\nThe issue:\n1. The mobile app uses a hardcoded mock token `\"mock-jwt-token-supplycycle-2026\"` for development\n2. When the app tries to call real backend endpoints (like `POST /api/v1/pedidos/:id/confirmar-entrega`) with this mock token, the backend's JWT middleware rejects it with a 401 because it's not a real signed JWT\n3. The apiClient interceptor sees the 401 but has a special case: if the token is the mock token, it does NOT auto-logout (so the user stays logged in)\n4. But the request still fails with an error, so the mutation hooks catch it\n5. The offline fallback in each mutation hook (useConfirmarEntrega, useCancelarPedido, etc.) kicks in: they call `offlineStore.addToQueue()` and return an optimistic response\n6. So mutations are \"queued offline\" not because the device is actually offline, but because the mock token causes all API calls to fail with 401\n\nTo make it \"real\" = connect to the actual backend:\n1. Start the backend (Express + Prisma + PostgreSQL) with `npm run dev`\n2. Have a real user in the database (seeded with the mock credentials)\n3. Login returns a real signed JWT from the backend\n4. All subsequent API calls use this real JWT, which the backend validates successfully\n5. The offline queue is only used when the device is actually offline (no network), not as a fallback for auth failures\n6. Remove the mock auth service fallback and the MOCK_TOKEN special case in api.ts

### Mensaje:
## Explicaci├│n\n\nEl problema es que **toda la app opera contra un backend que no est├í corriendo**. En lugar de eso, usa datos mockeados y un token JWT ficticio.\n\n### El ciclo actual:\n\n```\nLogin con mock\n  ÔåÆ se guarda token \"mock-jwt-token-supplycycle-2026\" en SecureStore\n  ÔåÆ la app intenta llamar al backend real (POST /pedidos/123/confirmar-entrega)\n  ÔåÆ el backend rechaza con 401 porque \"mock-jwt...\" no es un JWT v├ílido\n  ÔåÆ api.ts interceptor VE el 401, pero NO hace logout (porque detecta que es el token mock)\n  ÔåÆ la mutaci├│n falla igual ÔåÆ entra el offline fallback ÔåÆ se encola en offlineStore\n```\n\n**Resultado:** las mutaciones siempre se encolan \"offline\" no porque el dispositivo est├® sin conexi├│n, sino porque el token mock hace que el backend rechace todos los requests.\n\n### Qu├® significa \"hacerlo real\"\n\nConectar la app al backend real (Express + Prisma + PostgreSQL). Esto implica:\n\n#### 1. Backend corriendo\n```bash\ncd backend\nnpm run dev\n# Express escucha en http://localhost:3000\n```\n\n#### 2. Base de datos con datos reales\n```bash\ncd backend\nnpx prisma migrate dev\nnpx prisma db seed  # crea usuario: repartidor@supplycycle.com / 12345678\n```\n\n#### 3. Login real\n- `POST /api/v1/auth/login` devuelve un **JWT firmado real** (`jwt.sign({ userId, email, rol }, secret)`)\n- La app guarda ese JWT real en SecureStore\n\n#### 4. API calls reales\n- Todas las llamadas (`GET /pedidos`, `POST /pedidos/123/confirmar-entrega`, etc.) se autentican con el JWT real\n- El middleware `auth.middleware.ts` verifica la firma ÔåÆ OK ÔåÆ 200\n- **Ya no hay 401 por token inv├ílido**\n\n#### 5. Offline real\n- La cola de offlineStore solo se usa cuando **no hay conexi├│n de red** (no cuando el token es inv├ílido)\n- Se puede eliminar el `MOCK_TOKEN` y su bypass en `api.ts` (l├¡neas 43-45)\n- Se puede eliminar el fallback a mock en `useLogin.ts` y `app/_layout.tsx`\n\n### Resumen de cambios necesarios\n\n| Archivo | Cambio |\n|---|---|\n| `services/api.ts` | Eliminar `MOCK_TOKEN` y el bypass de 401 (l├¡neas 6, 40-45) |\n| `features/auth/hooks/useLogin.ts` | Sacar el try/catch que fallbackea a `mockLoginRequest` |\n| `features/auth/services/mockAuthService.ts` | Eliminar (o dejar solo para tests) |\n| `app/_layout.tsx` | Sacar el fallback a `mockGetMeRequest` en el bootstrap |\n| N/A | Asegurar que el backend est├® corriendo y tenga datos seed |\n\n### Estado actual del backend\n\nEl backend **ya est├í completo** y listo para usar:\n- `backend/src/features/auth/` ÔÇö login, JWT, middleware\n- `backend/src/features/pedidos/` ÔÇö CRUD de pedidos, items, confirmar/cancelar\n- `backend/prisma/schema.prisma` ÔÇö modelos `Usuario`, `Pedido`, `PedidoItem`, `Reparto`, etc.\n- Migraciones y seed listos\n\n┬┐Quer├®s que arme un plan detallado para la transici├│n de mock ÔåÆ real?

### Mensaje:
Ac├í est├í el plan completo para la transici├│n mock ÔåÆ real.\n\n---\n\n## Plan: Transici├│n completa de mock a real\n\n### Contexto actual\n\nEl backend ya est├í corriendo en Docker y responde correctamente (confirmado por tus logs). Sin embargo, la app mobile a├║n tiene **mecanismos de fallback a mock** en casi toda llamada API. Esto significa que **nunca se usa realmente el backend** - la app siempre cae al mock por cualquier error transitorio.\n\nLa transici├│n consiste en **eliminar esos fallbacks** y dejar que los errores reales (network, 500, etc.) se propaguen normalmente.\n\n---\n\n### ­ƒôª Fase 1: Autenticaci├│n (fundacional)\n\n| Archivo | Cambio |\n|---|---|\n| `features/auth/hooks/useLogin.ts` | Eliminar el `catch` que llama a `mockLoginRequest`. Dejar solo `loginRequest()` |\n| `app/_layout.tsx` | Eliminar el `import()` din├ímico de `mockAuthService` en el bootstrap. Si `GET /auth/me` falla, hacer logout |\n| `services/api.ts` | Eliminar la constante `MOCK_TOKEN` (l├¡nea 6) y el bypass de 401 (l├¡neas 40-45). Cualquier 401 = logout |\n\n**Riesgo:** Si el backend no est├í disponible al hacer login o al bootstrappear la app, el usuario no puede ingresar. Pero como tu backend est├í en Docker, esto solo pasar├¡a si el contenedor no est├í levantado.\n\n---\n\n### ­ƒôª Fase 2: Queries de datos (pedidos, repartos)\n\n| Archivo | Cambio |\n|---|---|\n| `features/pedidos/hooks/usePedidos.ts` | Eliminar el fallback a `mockGetPedidosDelDiaRequest`, `mockGetPedidoByIdRequest`, `mockGetPedidosRequest` en las 3 funciones query |\n| `features/repartos/hooks/useReparto.ts` | Eliminar el fallback a `mockGetRepartosRequest`, `mockGetRepartoByIdRequest` |\n| `features/pedidos/screens/PedidoAltaScreen.tsx` | Eliminar los dos try/catch con fallback a mock (clientes/items y repartos). Dejar solo la llamada real |\n\n**Riesgo:** Si el backend falla temporalmente (por ejemplo, la DB se cae), la pantalla muestra error en lugar de datos mock. El `ErrorMessage` con reintento ya est├í implementado.\n\n---\n\n### ­ƒôª Fase 3: MOCK_MOTIVOS (caso especial)\n\n`MOCK_MOTIVOS` en `PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx` **no es un fallback** ÔÇö es la fuente de datos real para el selector de motivos de cancelaci├│n. No hay un endpoint en el backend que devuelva esta lista.\n\n**Opciones:**\n- **Opci├│n A (recomendada):** Crear un endpoint `GET /api/v1/motivos-cancelacion` en el backend, devolver la lista desde el seed de la DB o desde una tabla `MotivoCancelacion`\n- **Opci├│n B (mantener como est├í):** Dejar `MOCK_MOTIVOS` como un archivo de configuraci├│n est├ítica (no es un mock en sentido estricto, es data est├ítica). Renombrarlo a `motivosCancelacion.ts` y moverlo a `constants/`\n\nSi vas por la Opci├│n A, hay que:\n1. Agregar modelo `MotivoCancelacion` a `prisma/schema.prisma` (o devolver desde un seed)\n2. Crear ruta + controller + service en `backend/src/features/pedidos/`\n3. Reemplazar el import en `PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx`\n\n---\n\n### ­ƒôª Fase 4: Limpieza\n\n| Archivo | Acci├│n |\n|---|---|\n| `features/auth/services/mockAuthService.ts` | Eliminar |\n| `features/pedidos/mocks/pedidoMockData.ts` | Eliminar |\n| `features/repartos/mocks/repartoMockData.ts` | Eliminar |\n| `mocks/services/clientes.mock.ts` | Eliminar |\n| `mocks/services/items.mock.ts` | Eliminar |\n| `mocks/services/repartos.mock.ts` | Eliminar |\n| `mocks/mockData.ts` | Mantener solo `MOCK_MOTIVOS` si se opta por Opci├│n B, o eliminar por completo si se opta por Opci├│n A |\n| `mocks/__tests__/mockData.test.ts` | Eliminar |\n\n---\n\n### ­ƒôª Fase 5: Tests\n\nLos tests que verifican el comportamiento de fallback a mock **dejan de ser v├ílidos** y deben ser actualizados:\n\n| Archivo | Acci├│n |\n|---|---|\n| `features/auth/hooks/__tests__/useLogin.test.ts` | Eliminar tests de fallback a mockLoginRequest |\n| `features/auth/__tests__/mockAuthService.test.ts` | Eliminar archivo |\n| `features/pedidos/hooks/__tests__/usePedidos.test.ts` | Actualizar: mockear el servicio real en lugar del fallback |\n| `features/repartos/hooks/__tests__/useReparto.test.ts` | Actualizar: mockear el servicio real |\n| `features/pedidos/screens/__tests__/PedidoAltaScreen.test.tsx` | Actualizar: mockear servicios reales |\n| `services/__tests__/api.test.ts` | Eliminar test de MOCK_TOKEN bypass |\n| `mocks/__tests__/mockData.test.ts` | Eliminar |\n\n---\n\n### ­ƒôª Fase 6: Verificaci├│n\n\n1. Hacer login con credenciales reales del backend\n2. Verificar que `GET /auth/me` funcione en el bootstrap\n3. Verificar que las queries de pedidos y repartos devuelvan datos reales\n4. Verificar que las mutaciones (confirmar, cancelar, items) funcionen contra el backend\n5. Verificar que un 401 leg├¡timo (token expirado) haga logout autom├ítico\n\n---\n\n### Resumen de archivos a modificar (14) y eliminar (8)\n\n```\nMODIFICAR (14):\n  features/auth/hooks/useLogin.ts\n  app/_layout.tsx\n  services/api.ts\n  features/pedidos/hooks/usePedidos.ts\n  features/repartos/hooks/useReparto.ts\n  features/pedidos/screens/PedidoAltaScreen.tsx\n  features/pedidos/screens/PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx  (si Opci├│n A)\n  features/auth/hooks/__tests__/useLogin.test.ts\n  features/pedidos/hooks/__tests__/usePedidos.test.ts\n  features/repartos/hooks/__tests__/useReparto.test.ts\n  features/pedidos/screens/__tests__/PedidoAltaScreen.test.tsx\n  services/__tests__/api.test.ts\n  backend/src/features/pedidos/routes.ts  (si Opci├│n A)\n  backend/prisma/schema.prisma  (si Opci├│n A)\n\nELIMINAR (8):\n  features/auth/services/mockAuthService.ts\n  features/auth/__tests__/mockAuthService.test.ts\n  features/pedidos/mocks/pedidoMockData.ts\n  features/repartos/mocks/repartoMockData.ts\n  mocks/services/clientes.mock.ts\n  mocks/services/items.mock.ts\n  mocks/services/repartos.mock.ts\n  mocks/__tests__/mockData.test.ts\n\nOPCIONAL (mantener o eliminar):\n  mocks/mockData.ts  (solo MOCK_MOTIVOS)\n```\n\n---\n\n┬┐Te parece bien el plan? ┬┐Quer├®s que arranquemos con la Fase 1 (auth) que es la m├ís cr├¡tica, o prefer├¡s hacerlo todo de una?

Now `app/_layout.tsx`. The current logic is:\n\n1. Get token from SecureStore\n2. If token exists:\n   a. Try fetch to `/auth/me`\n   b. If response.ok ÔåÆ setAuth and return\n   c. If error or !response.ok ÔåÆ fall back to mockGetMeRequest\n3. If no token ÔåÆ setLoading(false)\n\nThe new logic should be:\n1. Get token from SecureStore\n2. If token exists:\n   a. Try fetch to `/auth/me`\n   b. If response.ok ÔåÆ setAuth and return\n   c. Otherwise ÔåÆ setLoading(false) (go to login, let the user re-authenticate)\n3. If no token ÔåÆ setLoading(false)\n\nSo I need to:\n- Remove the catch block (lines 42-44) and the dynamic import fallback (lines 46-50)\n- Replace the fallback with `setLoading(false)` so the login screen shows\n- If the token is invalid/expired, the user is redirected to login, where they can log in again\n\nLet me also check: does the auth store have a `setLoading` action?\n\nI already saw the code. ### Mensaje:
The error handling in LoginScreen works like this:\n\n```ts\nconst serverError =\n    loginMutation.error && typeof loginMutation.error === 'object'\n      ? (() => {\n          const err = loginMutation.error as { response?: { data?: { error?: { message?: string } } } };\n          return err?.response?.data?.error?.message;\n        })()\n      : null;\n```\n\nThis extracts `error.response.data.error.message` from the Axios error. The backend `POST /auth/login` returns:\n```json\n{ \"error\": { \"code\": \"UNAUTHORIZED\", \"message\": \"Credenciales inv├ílidas\", \"timestamp\": \"...\" } }\n```\n\nSo when the backend returns 401, the Axios error will have `error.response.data.error.message = \"Credenciales inv├ílidas\"`, which will be displayed. Good.\n\nBut what if the error is NOT an Axios error (e.g., network error, backend not running)? The `catch` in `useLogin`'s mutationFn won't catch anymore (we removed the try/catch). So the error will propagate to `onError`, which TanStack Query handles by setting `mutation.error`. The error from `loginRequest` will be an Axios error or a network error.\n\nFor network errors (backend not running):\n- `loginMutation.error` will be an Axios error object\n- `error.response` will be `undefined` (no response received)\n- The `err?.response?.data?.error?.message` will be `null`/`undefined`\n- So `serverError` will be `null`, and no error message will be displayed\n\nThis is a problem - if the backend is not running, the user won't see any error message. Let me check what happens in the current UI for the error case.\n\nLet me also check how `serverError` is displayed.

### Mensaje:
Good, the UI already renders `serverError` in a styled error banner. But the current extraction logic only gets `error.response.data.error.message`. For network errors or non-Axios errors, it returns `null`, so no message is shown.\n\nI should update the error extraction to show a fallback message for network errors. Let me update the `serverError` logic to handle this case:\n\n```ts\nconst serverError =\n    loginMutation.error && typeof loginMutation.error === 'object'\n      ? (() => {\n          const err = loginMutation.error as any;\n          // Try Axios error with backend message first\n          const backendMessage = err?.response?.data?.error?.message;\n          if (backendMessage) return backendMessage;\n          // Network error (backend not running)\n          if (err?.code === 'ERR_NETWORK' || !err?.response) {\n            return 'No se pudo conectar con el servidor';\n          }\n          // Fallback gen├®rico\n          return err?.message || 'Error al iniciar sesi├│n';\n        })()\n      : null;\n```\n\nWait, but the user's backend IS running in Docker. So network errors would only happen if the containers are down. But still, it's better to handle this gracefully.\n\nLet me update the LoginScreen to improve error handling.

### Mensaje:
El manejo de errores del login actual solo muestra mensajes del backend, pero si el backend no responde no muestra nada. Lo mejoro:

### Mensaje:
Now let me also check if we need to update the login screen to handle the case where the response doesn't have the expected format (e.g., if the backend returns the user data directly instead of wrapped in an object).\n\nActually, let me check the backend's login response format to make sure the mobile app parses it correctly.

### Mensaje:
Let me check the controller to see how the response is formatted (wrapped in `{ data: ... }` or just sent directly).

### Mensaje:
The backend returns:\n- `POST /auth/login`: `res.status(200).json(result)` where `result = { token, usuario }` ÔÇö the AuthResponse directly, NOT wrapped in `{ data: ... }`\n- `GET /auth/me`: `res.status(200).json(result)` where `result = { id, email, nombre, apellido, rol, activo }` ÔÇö the UsuarioResponse directly, also NOT wrapped in `{ data: ... }`\n\nNow let me check how the mobile auth service (`authService.ts`) processes the login response.

### Mensaje:
The mobile auth service uses `apiClient.post<AuthResponse>('/auth/login', credentials)`. The `apiClient` is an Axios instance. Axios automatically unwraps `response.data`, so:\n\n- Backend returns: `res.json({ token: \"...\", usuario: {...} })` \n- Axios `response.data` = `{ token: \"...\", usuario: {...} }`\n- `loginRequest` returns `response.data` which is the `AuthResponse`\n\nBut wait ÔÇö there was a conversation earlier about the API response format (ADR-0000). Let me check the types for `ApiResponse` to see if there's a wrapping issue.\n\nActually, looking at the types from what the agent explored earlier:\n- `AuthResponse` type: `{ token: string; usuario: Usuario }`\n- Backend returns: `{ token: string; usuario: UsuarioResponse }`\n\nThese match! The response is not wrapped in `{ data: ... }` for auth endpoints.\n\nAnd for `GET /auth/me`:\n- Backend returns: `{ id, email, nombre, apellido, rol, activo }` (UsuarioResponse)\n- `getMeRequest` does `apiClient.get<Usuario>('/auth/me')` and returns `response.data`\n- `response.data` = `{ id, email, nombre, apellido, rol, activo }`\n\nThis matches the `Usuario` type. Good.\n\nBut wait ÔÇö in `app/_layout.tsx`, the bootstrap does a raw `fetch()` call, not using `apiClient`. The fetch gets the response and calls `response.json()`. So the bootstrap gets the UsuarioResponse directly (no wrapper). And then calls `setAuth(token, usuario)`.\n\nLet me check the auth store to see what `setAuth` expects.

### Mensaje:
Good, `setAuth(token, usuario)` expects a `Usuario` type, which matches what the backend returns. The login flow should work correctly now.\n\nNow let me run the tests.

Si `GET /auth/me` falla (token inv├ílido, backend no disponible), va directo a login |\n| `services/api.ts` | Eliminado `MOCK_TOKEN` y el bypass de 401. Cualquier 401 ahora hace logout autom├ítico |\n| `features/auth/screens/LoginScreen.tsx` | Mejorado el mensaje de error: ahora muestra \"No se pudo conectar con el servidor\" cuando hay error de red |\n| `features/auth/hooks/__tests__/useLogin.test.ts` | Eliminado test de fallback a mock |\n| `services/__tests__/api.test.ts` | Eliminado test de `MOCK_TOKEN` bypass |\n\n**┬┐Siguiente paso?** Podemos continuar con la **Fase 2** (queries de pedidos y repartos), o verificar que el login real funcione contra tu Docker.

### Mensaje:
Consiste en probar que el flujo de autenticaci├│n real funciona de punta a punta contra tu backend en Docker, asegur├índonos de que los cambios de la Fase 1 no rompieron nada.\n\n### Pasos de verificaci├│n\n\n#### 1. Login real\n- Abrir la app ÔåÆÕ║öÞ»Ñµÿ» ver la pantalla de login\n- Ingresar credenciales que existen en la base de datos del backend (`repartidor@supplycycle.com` / `12345678`)\n- La app llama a `POST /api/v1/auth/login` (ya no hay mock)\n- Esperado: login exitoso, redirecci├│n a tabs\n\n#### 2. Bootstrap con token real\n- Cerrar sesi├│n o recargar la app\n- El `useEffect` en `app/_layout.tsx` lee el token guardado y llama a `GET /api/v1/auth/me`\n- Esperado: reconoce al usuario sin mostrar pantalla de login\n\n#### 3. 401 ÔåÆ logout autom├ítico\n- Forzar un 401 (ej: esperar a que expire el token, o modificarlo manualmente en SecureStore)\n- Esperado: la app detecta el 401, limpia el token y redirige a login\n\n#### 4. Error de red\n- Apagar Docker (o desconectar la app del backend)\n- Esperado: la app no crashea. Login muestra \"No se pudo conectar con el servidor\". Las queries de pedidos muestran `ErrorMessage`. El usuario no se queda colgado.\n\n#### 5. Tests\n- `npm run test` ÔåÆ 137 tests pasan\n\n### ┬┐Qu├® se necesita de tu parte?\n\n- La app corriendo (Expo web, iOS simulator, o Android emulator)\n- El backend funcionando en Docker (ya est├í)\n- Que la base de datos tenga al menos un usuario con las credenciales de prueba\n\nSi quer├®s, podemos hacer la verificaci├│n juntos. O si prefer├¡s, podemos pasar directamente a la **Fase 2** (eliminar mocks de pedidos y repartos). ┬┐Qu├® opinas?

\"eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJ1c2VySWQiOiJlOTliYzBiMC1mNjE0LTQwMTEtOGFlYi03OWQ2NzE3Njk3MjQiLCJlbWFpbCI6InJlcGFydGlkb3JAc3VwcGx5Y3ljbGUuY29tIiwicm9sIjoiUkVQQVJUSURPUiIsImlhdCI6MTc3OTc2NzQyMCwiZXhwIjoxNzc5ODUzODIwfQ.rT3NDbL7P3pAQ4xitS70biJaFJiyqgSL1lyZ1BBY3-U\",\n ### Mensaje:
The user is saying that:\n\n1. The login works - they get a real JWT token from the backend\n2. They can enter the app and edit everything\n3. But there's a warning: \"saveToken failed, continuing without persistent session\"\n\nThe \"saveToken failed\" warning means `expo-secure-store` is failing in the web environment. Since the user is running on Expo web, `expo-secure-store` doesn't work natively - it uses a polyfill or may throw.\n\nThe token is received correctly from the backend (`POST /auth/login` returns `{ token, usuario }`), and the `setAuth` call works (they can enter and use the app), but `saveToken` fails because it uses `expo-secure-store` which doesn't work on web.\n\nThis means:\n- The session works in-memory (Zustand store)\n- But it's not persisted - if they refresh the page, they'll be logged out\n- The bootstrap won't find a stored token, so it will show the login screen again\n\nThis is a separate issue from the mock-to-real transition. The user already knows about the constraint of avoiding native dependencies (SecureStore doesn't work on web). \n\nThe user seems satisfied that Phase 1 works correctly - they can login with real credentials and use the app. The \"saveToken failed\" warning is expected on web and doesn't block functionality.\n\nLet me explain what's happening and what options they have.

### Mensaje:
El login real funciona perfecto. El mensaje `saveToken failed` es esperado en **Expo web** porque `expo-secure-store` es un m├│dulo nativo que no funciona en el navegador.\n\n### ┬┐Qu├® est├í pasando?\n\n1. Ô£à **Login real funciona**: `POST /api/v1/auth/login` devuelve un JWT firmado v├ílido ÔåÆ\n   ```json\n   { \"token\": \"eyJhbGci...\", \"usuario\": { \"id\": \"e99bc0b0...\", ... } }\n   ```\n\n2. Ô£à **setAuth funciona**: entr├ís a la app, ves los datos, editas, todo bien\n\n3. ÔÜá´©Å **solo falla `saveToken`** porque intenta guardar en `expo-secure-store`, que no existe en web. El catch del `onSuccess` lo captura y muestra el warning, pero no bloquea nada\n\n### Consecuencia\n\nLa sesi├│n vive **solo en memoria** (Zustand). Si recarg├ís la p├ígina en el browser, volv├®s al login. Si corr├®s en un dispositivo real (iOS/Android), `SecureStore` funciona y la sesi├│n persiste.\n\n### Opciones para web\n\nSi quer├®s que persista la sesi├│n tambi├®n en web, podemos agregar un fallback a `localStorage` (o `AsyncStorage`) cuando `SecureStore` falle:\n\n| Opci├│n | Qu├® hace |\n|---|---|\n| **A) No hacer nada** | En web, al recargar se pide login de nuevo. En dispositivos reales funciona normal |\n| **B) Fallback a AsyncStorage** | En `authStorage.ts`, si `SecureStore.setItemAsync` falla, guardar en `AsyncStorage`. Al leer, probar SecureStore primero, si falla leer de AsyncStorage |\n| **C) Usar `expo-secure-store` con polyfill** | Instalar un polyfill para web, pero agrega dependencia |\n\nLa **Opci├│n B** es la m├ís pr├íctica y consistente con la filosof├¡a de \"no mock, funcionar en web\". \n\n┬┐Quer├®s que implemente el fallback a AsyncStorage?

### Mensaje:
Interesting! The current `authStorage.ts` ALREADY has a fallback to `localStorage` when SecureStore is not available. It checks `typeof SecureStore.setItemAsync === 'function'` and if false, uses `localStorage`.\n\nBut the user is getting `saveToken failed` on web. This means the detection failed ÔÇö `SecureStore.setItemAsync` IS a function (it exists, it's just that calling it throws because the native module isn't available on web).\n\nSo the `isSecureStoreAvailable()` check returns `true` (because `typeof SecureStore.setItemAsync === 'function'`), then `saveToken` calls `SecureStore.setItemAsync(TOKEN_KEY, token)`, which throws because the native implementation is missing on web.\n\nThe fix is to change the approach: instead of checking if the function exists, try to use it and catch the error. Or use a try/catch approach:\n\n```ts\nexport async function saveToken(token: string): Promise<void> {\n  try {\n    await SecureStore.setItemAsync(TOKEN_KEY, token);\n  } catch {\n    // Fallback a localStorage si SecureStore no est├í disponible (web)\n    localStorage.setItem(TOKEN_KEY, token);\n  }\n}\n```\n\nSame for `getToken` and `clearToken`.\n\nThis is a much simpler and more reliable approach. Let me also check if `AsyncStorage` is already installed as a dependency.\n\nActually, looking at the current code, it already uses `localStorage` as fallback, not `AsyncStorage`. Since the user asked for AsyncStorage fallback (Option B), I should plan to use AsyncStorage instead of `localStorage` for consistency with the rest of the app (pedidos cache uses AsyncStorage).\n\nLet me check if AsyncStorage is available.\n\nActually, looking more carefully, the current code already uses `localStorage` as fallback. The problem is that the `isSecureStoreAvailable()` function returns `true` on web because the function exists even if it throws when called.\n\nThe simplest fix with minimal changes: wrap each SecureStore call in try/catch, and fallback to localStorage (or AsyncStorage) when it throws.\n\nFor Option B the user asked about AsyncStorage. But AsyncStorage might need an import. Let me check what's available.\n\nActually, the question says \"Fallback a AsyncStorage\" but the current code already uses `localStorage`. Since the user mentioned AsyncStorage, let me plan to use that instead. But first, let me check if AsyncStorage is already in the project dependencies.\n\nActually let me re-read the user's message: \"buneo planea la opcion b y luego la fase 2 ┬┐ambas se hacen desde mobile?\"\n\nThey're asking me to PLAN both things. Since we're in Plan Mode (read-only), I should NOT make any edits. I should present the plan.\n\nLet me create a clear plan for both.\n\nFor **Option B**, the current `authStorage.ts` already has a `localStorage` fallback, but the detection logic is broken on web because `SecureStore.setItemAsync` exists as a function even though calling it throws. The fix is simple: wrap in try/catch instead of pre-checking.\n\nFor **Fase 2**, the 4 files need to have their mock fallback logic removed.\n\nLet me present the plan.

### Mensaje:
Ac├í est├í el plan completo para ambas cosas. **Todo es mobile** (`@mobile`).\n\n---\n\n## ­ƒôï Opci├│n B ÔÇö Fallback a AsyncStorage en `authStorage.ts`\n\n### Problema actual\n\n`authStorage.ts` ya tiene un fallback a `localStorage`, pero el detection check falla:\n\n```ts\nfunction isSecureStoreAvailable(): boolean {\n  try {\n    return typeof SecureStore.setItemAsync === 'function';  // true en web\n  } catch {\n    return false;\n  }\n}\n```\n\nEn web, `SecureStore.setItemAsync` **existe como funci├│n** (es parte del bundle), pero al ejecutarla tira error porque el m├│dulo nativo no est├í. Entonces `useSecureStore = true`, llama a `SecureStore.setItemAsync()` y explota.\n\n### Soluci├│n\n\nReemplazar el detection-check por **try/catch directo** en cada funci├│n:\n\n```ts\nexport async function saveToken(token: string): Promise<void> {\n  try {\n    await SecureStore.setItemAsync(TOKEN_KEY, token);\n  } catch {\n    await AsyncStorage.setItem(TOKEN_KEY, token);  // fallback\n  }\n}\n```\n\nIgual para `getToken` y `clearToken`. El `localStorage` se reemplaza por `AsyncStorage` que ya es una dependencia del proyecto.\n\n### Archivos a modificar (1)\n\n| Archivo | Cambio |\n|---|---|\n| `features/auth/services/authStorage.ts` | Eliminar `isSecureStoreAvailable()` y `useSecureStore`. Cada funci├│n (`saveToken`, `getToken`, `clearToken`) usa try/catch: intenta SecureStore ÔåÆ fallback a AsyncStorage |\n\n---\n\n## ­ƒôï Fase 2 ÔÇö Eliminar mock fallbacks de queries\n\n### Resumen\n\n4 archivos, todos en `mobile/`. Eliminar imports de mock y los try/catch que caen a mock. Si el backend falla, el error se propaga normalmente ÔåÆ TanStack Query lo maneja ÔåÆ el `ErrorMessage` con bot├│n de reintento ya est├í implementado en todas las pantallas.\n\n### Archivos a modificar (4)\n\n#### 1. `features/pedidos/hooks/usePedidos.ts`\n\n| L├¡neas | Cambio |\n|---|---|\n| 16-20 | Eliminar `import { mockGetPedidosDelDiaRequest, mockGetPedidoByIdRequest, mockGetPedidosRequest }` |\n| 39-48 | `usePedidosDelDia`: cambiar el catch a solo leer cache de AsyncStorage, sin mock. Si no hay cache ÔåÆ mostrar error |\n| 59-63 | `usePedidoDetalle`: eliminar try/catch, dejar solo `return await getPedidoByIdRequest(id)` |\n| 78-82 | `useBuscarPedidos`: eliminar try/catch, dejar solo `return await getPedidosRequest(params)` |\n\n#### 2. `features/repartos/hooks/useReparto.ts`\n\n| L├¡neas | Cambio |\n|---|---|\n| 7-10 | Eliminar `import { mockGetRepartosRequest, mockGetRepartoByIdRequest }` |\n| 20-28 | `useReparto`: eliminar try/catch, dejar solo `const repartos = await getRepartosRequest(...)` |\n| 36-44 | `useRepartoDetalle`: eliminar try/catch, dejar solo `return await getRepartoByIdRequest(id)` |\n\n#### 3. `features/pedidos/screens/PedidoAltaScreen.tsx`\n\n| L├¡neas | Cambio |\n|---|---|\n| 23-25 | Eliminar `import { mockGetClientesRequest, mockGetItemsRequest, mockGetRepartosDisponiblesRequest }` |\n| 71-91 | Eliminar el try/catch anidado de clientes/items. Dejar solo `Promise.all([getClientesRequest(), getItemsRequest()])` con un solo catch que setea `dataError` |\n| 94-104 | Eliminar el try/catch anidado de repartos. Dejar solo `getRepartosDisponiblesRequest(...)`. Si falla, el formulario sigue sin reparto |\n\n### Tests a actualizar (3)\n\n| Archivo | Cambio |\n|---|---|\n| `features/pedidos/hooks/__tests__/usePedidos.test.ts` | Tests de mock fallback (3 tests) ÔåÆ eliminar o reemplazar con test de error propagado |\n| `features/repartos/hooks/__tests__/useReparto.test.ts` | Tests de mock fallback ÔåÆ eliminar o reemplazar |\n| `features/pedidos/screens/__tests__/PedidoAltaScreen.test.tsx` | Test de fallback ÔåÆ ajustar |\n\n---\n\n### Resumen total\n\n| | Archivos a modificar | Archivos de test a modificar |\n|---|---|---|\n| **Opci├│n B** | 1 (`authStorage.ts`) | 0 |\n| **Fase 2** | 4 (usePedidos, useReparto, PedidoAltaScreen, api.test) | 3 |\n| **Total** | **5** | **3** |\n\nTodo se ejecuta con `@mobile`. ┬┐Te parece bien el plan? ┬┐Arrancamos?

### Contenido:
import * as SecureStore from 'expo-secure-store';\nimport AsyncStorage from '@react-native-async-storage/async-storage';\n\nconst TOKEN_KEY = 'auth_token';\n\nexport async function saveToken(token: string): Promise<void> {\n  try {\n    await SecureStore.setItemAsync(TOKEN_KEY, token);\n  } catch {\n    // Fallback a AsyncStorage cuando SecureStore no est├í disponible (ej: web)\n    await AsyncStorage.setItem(TOKEN_KEY, token);\n  }\n}\n\nexport async function getToken(): Promise<string | null> {\n  try {\n    return await SecureStore.getItemAsync(TOKEN_KEY);\n  } catch {\n    // Fallback a AsyncStorage\n    try {\n      return await AsyncStorage.getItem(TOKEN_KEY);\n    } catch {\n      return null;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nexport async function clearToken(): Promise<void> {\n  try {\n    await SecureStore.deleteItemAsync(TOKEN_KEY);\n  } catch {\n    // Fallback a AsyncStorage\n    try {\n      await AsyncStorage.removeItem(TOKEN_KEY);\n    } catch {\n      // Ignorar error en limpieza\n    }\n  }\n}\n

### Mensaje:
Good. Now I also need to remove the `console.warn('saveToken failed...')` from `useLogin.ts` since the catch block there was the old fallback. Actually, looking at useLogin.ts again:\n\n```ts\nonSuccess: async (data) => {\n  try {\n    await saveToken(data.token);\n  } catch {\n    console.warn('saveToken failed, continuing without persistent session');\n  }\n  setAuth(data.token, data.usuario);\n},\n```\n\nThe try/catch here is still valid because `saveToken` could theoretically still fail (e.g., AsyncStorage full). But with the new implementation, it should never throw since AsyncStorage is always available on web. I'll keep the try/catch as a safety net but remove the warning since it's misleading.

### Contenido:
import { useQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query';\nimport { useAuthStore } from '@/stores/authStore';\nimport {\n  getRepartosRequest,\n  getRepartoByIdRequest,\n} from '@/features/repartos/services/repartoService';\nimport type { Reparto } from '@/types';\n\nfunction getRepartidorId(): string {\n  return useAuthStore.getState().usuario?.id ?? '';\n}\n\nexport function useReparto() {\n  return useQuery<Reparto>({\n    queryKey: ['reparto', 'hoy'],\n    queryFn: async () => {\n      const repartos = await getRepartosRequest(getRepartidorId());\n      return repartos[0];\n    },\n    staleTime: 5 * 60 * 1000,\n  });\n}\n\nexport function useRepartoDetalle(id: string) {\n  return useQuery<Reparto>({\n    queryKey: ['reparto', id],\n    queryFn: () => getRepartoByIdRequest(id),\n    enabled: !!id,\n  });\n}\n

Let me analyze the conversation history and update the summary accordingly.\n\nThe key changes from the conversation:\n\n1. **Fix applied: guardarCambios now works**: Removed `Alert.alert` entirely, made `guardarCambios` a direct `async` function that executes mutations immediately. Removed `Alert` import.\n\n2. **ConfirmModal created**: New reusable confirmation modal component at `components/ui/confirm-modal.tsx`. Used in PedidoDetalleScreen for \"Guardar cambios\" confirmation.\n\n3. **Dark mode fixes**: \n   - Added `buttonPrimary` theme color (`#0a7ea4` light, `#2563eb` dark)\n   - Button `primary` variant now uses `theme.buttonPrimary` instead of `theme.tint`\n   - Fixed all hardcoded `theme.tint` backgrounds across screens (PedidosListScreen \"+\" FAB, PedidoAltaScreen \"Agregar\" btn, MapaScreen \"Ver detalle\" btn, PerfilScreen avatar)\n   - Fixed itemRow border and backgroundColor hardcoded colors in PedidoDetalleScreen\n   - Added border to stepper buttons for dark mode visibility\n\n4. **Back button in Header**: Added `onBack` prop to Header component. Applied to PedidoDetalleScreen and PedidoAltaScreen.\n\n5. **Fase 1 - MockÔåÆReal transition (auth)**:\n   - Removed mock fallback from `useLogin.ts`\n   - Removed mock bootstrap fallback from `app/_layout.tsx`\n   - Removed MOCK_TOKEN bypass from `api.ts`\n   - Improved error message in LoginScreen for network errors\n\n6. **Option B - AsyncStorage fallback**: authStorage.ts now uses try/catch with AsyncStorage fallback (instead of broken SecureStore detection)\n\n7. **Fase 2 - MockÔåÆReal transition (queries)**:\n   - Removed mock fallbacks from `usePedidos.ts` (3 query functions)\n   - Removed mock fallbacks from `useReparto.ts`\n   - Removed mock fallbacks from `PedidoAltaScreen.tsx`\n\nLet me now build the updated summary.

### Mensaje:
## Goal\nComplete offline support, fix item mutations end-to-end, migrate fully to real backend (remove all mock fallbacks), and polish dark mode UI.\n\n## Constraints & Preferences\n- Avoid native dependencies where possible (SecureStore fallback to AsyncStorage for web).\n- Cross-environment compatibility: `gap` replaced with explicit margin, AsyncStorage over localStorage.\n- No mock data in production code paths ÔÇö only real backend endpoints.\n\n## Progress\n### Done\n- **guardarCambios fix**: Removed `Alert.alert` confirmation entirely. `guardarCambios` is now `async`, directly executes mutations (DELETE ÔåÆ POST ÔåÆ PATCH), updates cache via `setQueryData`, exits edit mode on success. Toast for success/error. `Alert` import removed.\n- **ConfirmModal component**: Created `components/ui/confirm-modal.tsx` ÔÇö reusable themed modal with props (`visible`, `title`, `message`, `onConfirm`/`onCancel`, `loading`, `variant`). Used in PedidoDetalleScreen replacing the removed `Alert.alert`.\n- **Dark mode button fix**: Added `buttonPrimary` theme color (`#0a7ea4` light, `#2563eb` dark). Button `primary` variant now uses `theme.buttonPrimary` instead of `theme.tint` (white in dark mode ÔåÆ invisible).\n- **All hardcoded `theme.tint` backgrounds replaced**: PedidosListScreen \"+\" FAB, PedidoAltaScreen \"Agregar\" button, MapaScreen \"Ver detalle\" button, PerfilScreen avatar ÔåÆ all use `theme.buttonPrimary`.\n- **Item row dark mode**: `itemRow` border uses `theme.border` instead of hardcoded `#E2E8F0`. `itemRowEditing` uses `theme.info + '15'` instead of hardcoded `#F0F9FF`. Stepper buttons have `borderWidth: 1` + `borderColor: theme.border` for visibility in dark mode.\n- **Header back button**: Added optional `onBack` prop to Header. `<` chevron appears top-left. Applied to PedidoDetalleScreen (`pedidos/[id]`, `mapa/[id]`) and PedidoAltaScreen.\n- **Fase 1 ÔÇö MockÔåÆReal (auth)**: `useLogin.ts` removed try/catch fallback to `mockLoginRequest`. `app/_layout.tsx` removed dynamic import fallback to `mockGetMeRequest`. `api.ts` removed `MOCK_TOKEN` constant and 401 bypass. `LoginScreen.tsx` improved error extraction (shows \"No se pudo conectar con el servidor\" on network error).\n- **Opci├│n B ÔÇö AsyncStorage fallback**: `authStorage.ts` rewritten: each function tries `SecureStore.*` ÔåÆ catches ÔåÆ falls back to `AsyncStorage`. Removed broken `isSecureStoreAvailable()` detection. `useLogin.ts` simplified `saveToken` call to `.catch(() => {})`.\n- **Fase 2 ÔÇö MockÔåÆReal (queries)**: `usePedidos.ts` removed 3 mock imports and try/catch fallbacks from `usePedidosDelDia`, `usePedidoDetalle`, `useBuscarPedidos`. Cache-from-AsyncStorage kept as offline fallback (without mock). `useReparto.ts` removed 2 mock imports and try/catch fallbacks. `PedidoAltaScreen.tsx` removed 3 mock imports and nested try/catch in `loadData`.\n- **Tests updated**: `useLogin.test.ts` removed mock-fallback test and `mockAuthService` mock. `api.test.ts` removed MOCK_TOKEN constant and test.\n\n### In Progress\n- *(none)*\n\n### Blocked\n- *(none)*\n\n## Key Decisions\n- No `Alert.alert` for async callbacks (broken on Expo web). ### Mensaje:
## Goal\nComplete offline support, fix item mutations end-to-end, migrate fully to real backend (remove all mock fallbacks), and polish dark mode UI.\n\n## Constraints & Preferences\n- Avoid native dependencies where possible (SecureStore fallback to AsyncStorage for web).\n- Cross-environment compatibility: `gap` replaced with explicit margin, AsyncStorage over localStorage.\n- No mock data in production code paths ÔÇö only real backend endpoints.\n\n## Progress\n### Done\n- **guardarCambios fix**: Removed `Alert.alert` confirmation entirely. `guardarCambios` is now `async`, directly executes mutations (DELETE ÔåÆ POST ÔåÆ PATCH), updates cache via `setQueryData`, exits edit mode on success. Toast for success/error. `Alert` import removed.\n- **ConfirmModal component**: Created `components/ui/confirm-modal.tsx` ÔÇö reusable themed modal with props (`visible`, `title`, `message`, `onConfirm`/`onCancel`, `loading`, `variant`). Used in PedidoDetalleScreen replacing the removed `Alert.alert`.\n- **Dark mode button fix**: Added `buttonPrimary` theme color (`#0a7ea4` light, `#2563eb` dark). Button `primary` variant now uses `theme.buttonPrimary` instead of `theme.tint` (white in dark mode ÔåÆ invisible).\n- **All hardcoded `theme.tint` backgrounds replaced**: PedidosListScreen \"+\" FAB, PedidoAltaScreen \"Agregar\" button, MapaScreen \"Ver detalle\" button, PerfilScreen avatar ÔåÆ all use `theme.buttonPrimary`.\n- **Item row dark mode**: `itemRow` border uses `theme.border` instead of hardcoded `#E2E8F0`. `itemRowEditing` uses `theme.info + '15'` instead of hardcoded `#F0F9FF`. Stepper buttons have `borderWidth: 1` + `borderColor: theme.border` for visibility in dark mode.\n- **Header back button**: Added optional `onBack` prop to Header. `<` chevron appears top-left. Applied to PedidoDetalleScreen (`pedidos/[id]`, `mapa/[id]`) and PedidoAltaScreen.\n- **Fase 1 ÔÇö MockÔåÆReal (auth)**: `useLogin.ts` removed try/catch fallback to `mockLoginRequest`. `app/_layout.tsx` removed dynamic import fallback to `mockGetMeRequest`. `api.ts` removed `MOCK_TOKEN` constant and 401 bypass. `LoginScreen.tsx` improved error extraction (shows \"No se pudo conectar con el servidor\" on network error).\n- **Opci├│n B ÔÇö AsyncStorage fallback**: `authStorage.ts` rewritten: each function tries `SecureStore.*` ÔåÆ catches ÔåÆ falls back to `AsyncStorage`. Removed broken `isSecureStoreAvailable()` detection. `useLogin.ts` simplified `saveToken` call to `.catch(() => {}`).\n- **Fase 2 ÔÇö MockÔåÆReal (queries)**: `usePedidos.ts` removed 3 mock imports and try/catch fallbacks from `usePedidosDelDia`, `usePedidoDetalle`, `useBuscarPedidos`. Cache-from-AsyncStorage kept as offline fallback (without mock). `useReparto.ts` removed 2 mock imports and try/catch fallbacks. `PedidoAltaScreen.tsx` removed 3 mock imports and nested try/catch in `loadData`.\n- **Tests updated (mock fallbacks)**: `useLogin.test.ts` removed mock-fallback test and `mockAuthService` mock. `api.test.ts` removed `MOCK_TOKEN` constant and test.\n\n### In Progress\n- **Updating test files to remove mock fallbacks**: Removed `@/features/pedidos/mocks/pedidoMockData` import block from `usePedidos.test.ts`. Still need to remove fallback tests (usePedidosDelDia, usePedidoDetalle, useBuscarPedidos) from same file. Still need to remove `@/features/repartos/mocks/repartoMockData` import block and fallback tests from `useReparto.test.ts`.\n\n### Blocked\n- *(none)*\n\n## Key Decisions\n- No `Alert.alert` for async callbacks (broken on Expo web). Replaced with custom `ConfirmModal` component.\n- `theme.buttonPrimary` added instead of reusing `theme.tint` (white in dark mode).\n- AsyncStorage as SecureStore fallback instead of `localStorage` (consistent with rest of app, works across all platforms).\n- `usePedidosDelDia` keeps AsyncStorage cache fallback (provides stale data when offline) but removes mock data fallback.\n- Backend (Express + Prisma + PostgreSQL) running in Docker with real JWT auth ÔÇö all mock fallbacks now removed.\n- Removing `@/features/*/mocks/*MockData` imports and their fallback tests from test files, keeping only real-service-based tests.\n\n## Next Steps\n1. Remove fallback tests (`it('usa mock...')`) from `usePedidos.test.ts` ÔÇö replace with error-throwing assertions.\n2. Remove `@/features/repartos/mocks/repartoMockData` mock + fallback tests from `useReparto.test.ts`.\n3. Run tests to confirm no regressions.\n\n## Critical Context\n- Backend runs in Docker, responds correctly. Real JWT issued on login, validated by backend middleware.\n- 137 tests pass (2 removed: mock-fallback tests), same 8 pre-existing failures (`@expo/vector-icons` JSX parsing).\n- `saveToken` previously logged warning on web (SecureStore unavailable). Now silently falls back to AsyncStorage ÔÇö persists across page reloads.\n- `MOCK_MOTIVOS` in `PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx` is static config (motives for cancellation), not a mock ÔÇö kept as-is.\n- Both `usePedidos.test.ts` and `useReparto.test.ts` still contain mock-import blocks and fallback tests that must be removed to match the no-mock policy of the production hooks.\n\n## Relevant Files\n- `features/pedidos/hooks/__tests__/usePedidos.test.ts`: Removed mock import block; fallback tests for `usePedidosDelDia`, `usePedidoDetalle`, `useBuscarPedidos` still need removal.\n- `features/repartos/hooks/__tests__/useReparto.test.ts`: Still has `@/features/repartos/mocks/repartoMockData` import block and fallback tests for `useReparto` and `useRepartoDetalle` ÔÇö pending removal.\n- `features/pedidos/hooks/usePedidos.ts`: No mock fallbacks (already updated in Fase 2).\n- `features/repartos/hooks/useReparto.ts`: No mock fallbacks (already updated in Fase 2).

No regressions.\n\n### State after these changes\n- Ô£à All mock imports & fallback tests removed from both `usePedidos.test.ts` and `useReparto.test.ts`\n- Ô£à Real backend endpoints only ÔÇö no mock data in production code paths or test assertions\n- Ô£à Offline cache fallback (AsyncStorage) retained in `usePedidosDelDia` hook and tested via error-throwing assertion\n- Ô£à Offline queue fallback (offlineStore) retained in mutation hooks (confirmar, cancelar, items) ÔÇö tests kept as-is

Let me first look at the PedidoAltaScreen (creating orders) and PedidoDetalleScreen (editing orders) to understand the flow.\n\nLet me start by exploring the relevant files.

### Mensaje:
Now let me understand both issues:\n\n**Issue 1: Creating pedido without cliente doesn't show error**\n\nLooking at `PedidoAltaScreen.tsx`, the `handleSubmit` function (lines 128-160) has early validation:\n```ts\nif (!selectedCliente) {\n  Alert.alert('Faltan datos', 'Seleccion├í un cliente para el pedido');\n  return;\n}\n```\n\nIt uses `Alert.alert` which was previously identified as broken on Expo web. But the user is on mobile, so `Alert.alert` should work. However, looking more carefully... the user says \"no sale cartel indicando por qu├® falla\" ÔÇö maybe the issue is that when the backend returns an error (e.g., validation error), the toast message doesn't show what the actual error is.\n\nWait, re-reading: \"cuando intento crear un pedido sin cliente no me sale cartel indicando por qu├® falla\". So when they try to create an order without selecting a client, there's no feedback message. This could mean:\n\n1. The `Alert.alert` validation works but perhaps the user means the API error handling?\n2. Or perhaps they're clicking \"Crear Pedido\" without a cliente, and it actually submits to the backend where the backend returns an error, and that error message is displayed via toast (line 156-158).\n\nActually, looking at the validation in `handleSubmit`:\n```ts\nif (!selectedCliente) {\n  Alert.alert('Faltan datos', 'Seleccion├í un cliente para el pedido');\n  return;\n}\n```\n\nThis should work and show an Alert. But the user says no cartel appears. Maybe the issue is different - perhaps the mutation `onError` handler doesn't properly display the error message from the backend.\n\nLet me look at how `handleApiError` works and how the toast is shown.\n\nActually wait, let me re-read the user's message: \"cuando intento crear un pedido sin cliente no me sale cartel indicando por qu├® falla\". \n\nIf no cliente is selected and they hit submit, the Alert fires. But the user says no cartel appears. Maybe the Alert is not working in their environment (expo web? upgraded SDK?). The earlier change log mentioned that Alert.alert was replaced with ConfirmModal because it was broken on Expo web. But this screen still uses Alert.\n\nActually, let me look at this more carefully. The user is reporting issues after our changes. Let me check if there's a scenario where the error is being swallowed:\n\n1. If the user DOES select a cliente but the backend rejects the request (maybe clienteId is invalid, or some other validation), the `onError` callback fires. Let me check `handleApiError`.\n\n2. Let me also see if there's a scenario where `crearPedido.mutate` is called without a cliente.\n\nWait, I think I understand now. The `handleSubmit` function has the early validation using `Alert.alert`. But in the earlier work we replaced `Alert.alert` with our own toast system in other screens because `Alert.alert` was problematic. Maybe the validation SHOULD use a toast instead of Alert to be consistent, and the user is saying the Alert doesn't appear?\n\nActually, looking more carefully at the code - the validation check:\n```ts\nif (!selectedCliente) {\n  Alert.alert('Faltan datos', 'Seleccion├í un cliente para el pedido');\n  return;\n}\nif (selectedItems.length === 0) {\n  Alert.alert('Faltan datos', 'Agreg├í al menos un ├¡tem al pedido');\n  return;\n}\n```\n\nThese use `Alert.alert` which is a React Native built-in. It should work on mobile. But maybe the issue is that the validation fires, shows the alert, but there's also an error from the backend because the form was submitted somehow?\n\nActually, I think the real issue might be different. Let me re-read: \"cuando intento crear un pedido sin cliente no me sale cartel indicando por qu├® falla\". Maybe the user means that the app doesn't visually show WHERE the problem is ÔÇö the cliente field doesn't have a red border or error text below it. The alert pops up and goes away without the user knowing which field specifically needs attention.\n\nOR maybe the user is referring to the API validation ÔÇö when they select a cliente and items, submit, and the backend returns a validation error, the error message from the toast is not informative enough.\n\nLet me check the `handleApiError` service to understand error formatting.\n\nActually, let me look at this from a different angle. The user said \"cuando intento crear un pedido sin cliente no me sale cartel indicando por qu├® falla\" and \"cuando edito un pedido no se actualiza el precio\". These are two separate bugs.\n\nFor the first bug, maybe the validation is not being reached. Let me trace through the code more carefully:\n\n1. User opens PedidoAltaScreen\n2. User doesn't select a cliente\n3. User clicks \"Crear Pedido\"\n4. `handleSubmit` is called\n5. `if (!selectedCliente)` is true ÔåÆ `Alert.alert` fires\n\nBut wait, the user might mean that they DO select a cliente but it's somehow null/invalid when submitted. Or maybe the `selectedCliente` is a state that's not properly set.\n\nActually, looking more carefully, I notice the `handleSubmit` validation check for `selectedCliente` should definitely show an Alert. But maybe the user is on Expo web where `Alert.alert` is problematic (as noted in the earlier work).\n\nYou know what, let me just focus on what would help the user: replace the `Alert.alert` validation with toast-based validation, and also add inline field validation UI (showing an error below the cliente field). That way the user gets clear feedback regardless of platform.\n\nBut the user said \"no me sale cartel indicando por qu├® falla\" ÔÇö maybe the `onError` from the mutation doesn't show anything useful when the API rejects with a 400.\n\nLet me check `handleApiError`.

### Mensaje:
Now I understand the error types. The `handleApiError` function checks for `axiosError.response?.data?.error` which should match the `ApiError` shape (`{ error: { code, message, timestamp, details? } }`).\n\nNow let me think about the two issues more carefully:\n\n**Issue 1: \"cuando intento crear un pedido sin cliente no me sale cartel indicando por qu├® falla\"**\n\nI think this means:\n- When a user tries to create a pedido without selecting a client, the app uses `Alert.alert` which might work but doesn't integrate well with the UI.\n- The user might want an inline error (like a red border or error text below the cliente selector) instead of an alert, or they might be saying the Alert doesn't appear at all.\n\nLooking at the code, `handleSubmit` checks:\n```ts\nif (!selectedCliente) {\n  Alert.alert('Faltan datos', 'Seleccion├í un cliente para el pedido');\n  return;\n}\n```\n\nThe `Alert.alert` is a native React Native dialog that should appear on mobile. But if the user is testing on web, Alert.alert may not show anything useful. Or, the user might find the Alert disruptive.\n\nThe better approach: Replace the `Alert.alert` with:\n1. A visible inline error message below the cliente field, AND\n2. A toast notification\n\nThis gives the user clear, contextual feedback on what's wrong.\n\n**Issue 2: \"cuando edito un pedido no se actualiza el precio\"**\n\nLooking at `PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx`, the total calculation:\n\n```ts\nconst totalEdit = itemsEdit.reduce(\n  (sum, item) => sum + (item.precioUnitario ?? 0) * item.cantidad,\n  0\n);\n```\n\nThis calculates the total based on `itemsEdit` state, which is the local editing state. Then it's displayed:\n\n```tsx\n<Text style={[styles.totalValue, { color: theme.tint }]}>\n  {modoEdicion\n    ? formatPrecio(totalEdit)\n    : pedido.total != null\n      ? formatPrecio(pedido.total)\n      : 'ÔÇö'}\n</Text>\n```\n\nSo in edit mode, it uses `totalEdit` (computed from `itemsEdit`), and in view mode it uses `pedido.total`.\n\nThe issue might be:\n1. After saving changes (adding/removing items), the `pedido.total` is not updated in the cache because the `guardarCambios` function only updates `items` in the cache, not `total`:\n   ```ts\n   queryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', id], (old) => {\n     if (!old) return old;\n     return { ...old, items: itemsEdit };\n   });\n   ```\n   It updates `items` but NOT `total`. So when the view re-renders, it shows `pedido.total` which is stale.\n\nThe fix: After saving changes, we need to update the cached `total` as well. We should recalculate the total from the updated items and include it in the cache update.\n\nLet me also check if the backend returns a new total. The mutations for items (agregar, quitar, actualizar) return data ÔÇö but looking at the hooks:\n\n```ts\n// Agregar item a un pedido (con soporte offline)\nexport function useAgregarItem(pedidoId: string) {\n  const queryClient = useQueryClient();\n  const addToQueue = useOfflineStore((s) => s.addToQueue);\n\n  return useMutation({\n    mutationFn: async (data: { itemId: string; cantidad: number }) => {\n      try {\n        return await agregarItemRequest(pedidoId, data);\n      } catch {\n        addToQueue({ type: 'AGREGAR_ITEM', payload: { pedidoId, ...data } });\n        return data;\n      }\n    },\n    onSuccess: () => {\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['pedidos'] });\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['reparto'] });\n    },\n  });\n}\n```\n\nThe `onSuccess` invalidates `['pedidos']` and `['reparto']` but not `['pedido', pedidoId]`. This is intentional (as per the comment: \"No invalidar ['pedido', pedidoId] para no sobrescribir el cache que el screen ya actualiz├│ con setQueryData (guardarCambios)\"). \n\nBut the `guardarCambios` function itself only updates `items` in the cache, not `total`:\n```ts\nqueryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', id], (old) => {\n  if (!old) return old;\n  return { ...old, items: itemsEdit };\n});\n```\n\nThe fix should be to also compute the new total and include it in the cache update:\n\n```ts\nqueryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', id], (old) => {\n  if (!old) return old;\n  const newTotal = itemsEdit.reduce(\n    (sum, item) => sum + (item.precioUnitario ?? 0) * item.cantidad,\n    0\n  );\n  return { ...old, items: itemsEdit, total: newTotal };\n});\n```\n\nWait, but let me also check if there's a `total` property on Pedido type. Let me look at the Pedido type.

### Mensaje:
Now I have a clear picture.\n\n**Issue 1: No error when creating order without client**\n\nIn `PedidoAltaScreen.tsx`, the `handleSubmit` function uses `Alert.alert` for validation errors. This works on native but doesn't give persistent inline feedback. Also, if the user is on web/emulator, `Alert.alert` might not be reliable. \n\nThe fix: Replace `Alert.alert` with inline validation state that:\n1. Sets a validation error message in component state\n2. Shows a red error text below the cliente selector when no cliente is selected\n3. Shows a toast for additional feedback\n\nI'll add a `validationError` state and clear it on next interaction, plus show inline error text near the relevant field.\n\n**Issue 2: Price not updating after editing items**\n\nIn `PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx`, the `handleConfirmGuardar` updates the cache:\n```ts\nqueryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', id], (old) => {\n  if (!old) return old;\n  return { ...old, items: itemsEdit };\n});\n```\n\nIt doesn't update the `total` field. The view mode shows `pedido.total` which is now stale.\n\nThe fix: Include the recalculated total in the cache update.\n\nLet me also check if there's a `total` computed during display vs stored. Looking at the view mode:\n```tsx\n{modoEdicion\n  ? formatPrecio(totalEdit)\n  : pedido.total != null\n    ? formatPrecio(pedido.total)\n    : 'ÔÇö'}\n```\n\nSo in view mode, it uses `pedido.total` from the cache. After editing, the total in cache is stale because only items were updated, not the total.\n\nThe fix in `handleConfirmGuardar`:\n```ts\nqueryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', id], (old) => {\n  if (!old) return old;\n  const newTotal = itemsEdit.reduce(\n    (sum, item) => sum + (item.precioUnitario ?? 0) * item.cantidad,\n    0\n  );\n  return { ...old, items: itemsEdit, total: newTotal };\n});\n```\n\nLet me now make both fixes.

Now I need to update the `handleConfirmGuardar` function to also compute and set the new total in the cache.\n\nLooking at the current code:\n\n```ts\nqueryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', id], (old) => {\n  if (!old) return old;\n  return { ...old, items: itemsEdit };\n});\n```\n\nI need to change it to:\n\n```ts\nqueryClient.setQueryData<Pedido>(['pedido', id], (old) => {\n  if (!old) return old;\n  const newTotal = itemsEdit.reduce(\n    (sum, item) => sum + (item.precioUnitario ?? 0) * item.cantidad,\n    0\n  );\n  return { ...old, items: itemsEdit, total: newTotal };\n});\n```\n\nThis recalculates the total from the updated items and updates it in the cache, so when the view re-renders after exiting edit mode, it will show the correct total.

### Mensaje:
### Ô£à Resumen de cambios\n\n#### 1. PedidoAltaScreen ÔÇö Error visible al crear pedido sin cliente\n**Antes:** Usaba `Alert.alert` ÔåÆ desaparec├¡a sin dejar rastro, el usuario no ve├¡a qu├® campo estaba mal.\n\n**Ahora:**\n- Ô£à El borde del selector de cliente se pone **rojo** si no hay cliente seleccionado al enviar\n- Ô£à Aparece un **texto de error en rojo** debajo del campo: \"Deb├®s seleccionar un cliente para el pedido\"\n- Ô£à Tambi├®n muestra un **toast warning** como feedback adicional\n- Ô£à El error se limpia al interactuar con el selector de cliente o al tocar items\n- Ô£à Se elimin├│ `Alert` de los imports (ya no se usa)\n\n#### 2. PedidoDetalleScreen ÔÇö Precio actualizado al editar items\n**Antes:** `guardarCambios` solo actualizaba `items` en el cach├®, pero **no recalculaba el total**. Al salir del modo edici├│n, se mostraba el `pedido.total` viejo.\n\n**Ahora:**\n- Ô£à `guardarCambios` recalcula `newTotal` en base a `itemsEdit` y lo incluye en el cach├®:\n  ```ts\n  const newTotal = itemsEdit.reduce(\n    (sum, item) => sum + (item.precioUnitario ?? 0) * item.cantidad,\n    0\n  );\n  return { ...old, items: itemsEdit, total: newTotal };\n  ```\n- Ô£à Al salir del modo edici├│n, el total mostrado refleja los cambios realizados.

seguiria con la fase 3, con mobile o backend? o que falta por hacer antes?\n\nFase 3: MOCK_MOTIVOS (caso especial)\nMOCK_MOTIVOS en PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx no es un fallback ÔÇö es la fuente de datos real para el selector de motivos de cancelaci├│n. No hay un endpoint en el backend que devuelva esta lista.\nOpciones:\n- Opci├│n A (recomendada): Crear un endpoint GET /api/v1/motivos-cancelacion en el backend, devolver la lista desde el seed de la DB o desde una tabla MotivoCancelacion\n- Opci├│n B (mantener como est├í): Dejar MOCK_MOTIVOS como un archivo de configuraci├│n est├ítica (no es un mock en sentido estricto, es data est├ítica). Renombrarlo a motivosCancelacion.ts y moverlo a constants/\nSi vas por la Opci├│n A, hay que:\n1. Agregar modelo MotivoCancelacion a prisma/schema.prisma (o devolver desde un seed)\n2. Crear ruta + controller + service en backend/src/features/pedidos/\n3. Reemplazar el import en PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx

### Mensaje:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me formulate my plan and recommendations.\n\nThe current state:\n- `MOCK_MOTIVOS` is the last remaining mock-ish data in production code\n- It's used in `PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx` for the cancel reason selector\n- It's defined in `mobile/mocks/mockData.ts` alongside the old mock data (MOCK_CLIENTES, MOCK_PEDIDOS, etc.)\n- The backend has `motivoFalla` as a string field on `Pedido` model ÔÇö there's no `MotivoCancelacion` table\n- The seed and existing code just use strings like `'CLIENTE_AUSENTE'`\n\nThe OP (the plan that was described earlier) gave two options:\n- **Opci├│n A (recommended):** Create a proper backend endpoint `GET /api/v1/motivos-cancelacion` backed by a DB table\n- **Opci├│n B:** Rename `MOCK_MOTIVOS` to `motivosCancelacion.ts` and move to `constants/`\n\nLet me analyze both:\n\n**Opci├│n A** requires:\n1. Backend: New Prisma model `MotivoCancelacion`, migration, seed\n2. Backend: New feature folder `motivos-cancelacion` with schema/types/service/controller/routes\n3. Backend: Mount the route in `app.ts`\n4. Mobile: New service function `getMotivosCancelacionRequest()`\n5. Mobile: New hook `useMotivosCancelacion()` (or just use `useQuery` inline)\n6. Mobile: Replace `MOCK_MOTIVOS` import in `PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx` with the hook call\n\nThis is quite a bit of work for what amounts to 4 static values. But it follows the pattern of removing all mock fallbacks.\n\n**Opci├│n B** requires:\n1. Mobile: Create `mobile/constants/motivosCancelacion.ts`\n2. Mobile: Update the import in `PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx`\n3. Optionally: Update the import in `mocks/__tests__/mockData.test.ts` if needed\n\nThis is much simpler, but the data is still hardcoded.\n\nMy recommendation: **Opci├│n A** ÔÇö here's why:\n- The whole point of Fase 3 is that `MOCK_MOTIVOS` is the last vestige of mock data in production code\n- The OP already lists this as a recommended option\n- The backend is running and all other endpoints work\n- It's a simple CRUD endpoint (read-only, just GET), minimal effort\n- It follows the pattern of all other data being served from the backend\n- It makes the app configurable ÔÇö you could add/remove reasons without a code deploy\n\nHowever, I need to also think about what else is pending after this. The user asked \"┬┐seguiria con la fase 3, con mobile o backend? o que falta por hacer antes?\"\n\nLet me assess all remaining tasks:\n\nWhat's been done (Fase 1 and 2):\n- Ô£à MockÔåÆReal for auth (login, me)\n- Ô£à MockÔåÆReal for pedidos queries (usePedidosDelDia, usePedidoDetalle, useBuscarPedidos)\n- Ô£à MockÔåÆReal for reparto queries (useReparto, useRepartoDetalle)\n- Ô£à Mock imports and fallback tests removed from pedido and reparto hooks\n- Ô£à Mock fallback tests removed from usePedidos.test.ts and useReparto.test.ts\n- Ô£à Alert.alert replaced with inline validation in PedidoAltaScreen\n- Ô£à Price update fix in PedidoDetalleScreen (total recalculated on item edit)\n\nWhat might remain:\n1. **MOCK_MOTIVOS (Fase 3)** ÔÇö The last mock import in production code\n2. **Mock services** ÔÇö The `mobile/mocks/services/` directory still has mock files (`repartos.mock.ts`, `items.mock.ts`, `clientes.mock.ts`). These might be used somewhere or might be dead code.\n3. **mockData.ts cleanup** ÔÇö After removing MOCK_MOTIVOS, the file `mobile/mocks/mockData.ts` will only contain `MOCK_CLIENTES`, `MOCK_ITEMS`, `MOCK_PEDIDOS`, `MOCK_REPARTO` ÔÇö which are used in tests (`mocks/__tests__/mockData.test.ts` and maybe other test files). The mock data is still useful for tests, so we shouldn't delete it entirely, but `MOCK_MOTIVOS` specifically needs to be extracted.\n\nLet me check what test files still use mock data.
