### Mensaje:
## Plan para el agente mobile\n\nAntes de arrancar, una **correcci├│n importante**: investigu├® el backend y los return types del service **NO est├ín rotos**. Cada funci├│n en `pedidoService.ts` maneja correctamente el formato que devuelve cada endpoint (algunos endpoints usan `sendSuccess` ÔåÆ envuelven en `{ data }`, otros devuelven raw). La inconsistencia est├í en el backend, no en el mobile. As├¡ que H2 no es un bug mobile.\n\nEl plan real es:\n\n---\n\n### ­ƒö┤ H1: Refactor `PedidoDetalleScreen` (982 ÔåÆ <250 l├¡neas)\n\n**Objetivo**: Extraer subcomponentes y un hook para que el screen principal quede por debajo de 250 l├¡neas.\n\n**Archivos a crear** (en `features/pedidos/components/`):\n\n| Archivo | L├¡neas estimadas | Contenido |\n|---------|-----------------|-----------|\n| `InfoCliente.tsx` | ~50 | Secci├│n de datos del cliente (nombre, direcci├│n, tel├®fono, horario) |\n| `ItemsList.tsx` | ~100 | Lista de items con modo lectura/edici├│n, stepper, bot├│n eliminar |\n| `ItemsEditActions.tsx` | ~40 | Botones \"Guardar cambios\" / \"Cancelar\" en modo edici├│n |\n| `CancelModal.tsx` | ~60 | Modal de selecci├│n de motivo de cancelaci├│n |\n| `AddItemModal.tsx` | ~100 | Modal para agregar item (selector + cantidad) |\n| `ConfirmarAccionButtons.tsx` | ~30 | Botones \"Confirmar entrega\" / \"Cancelar entrega\" |\n\n**Archivo a crear** (en `features/pedidos/hooks/`):\n\n| Archivo | L├¡neas estimadas | Contenido |\n|---------|-----------------|-----------|\n| `useEdicionItems.ts` | ~80 | Hook con la l├│gica de edici├│n (entrar/salir/guardar/cambiar cantidad/eliminar/agregar) |\n\n**Qu├® queda en `PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx`** (~200 l├¡neas):\n- Imports de los nuevos subcomponentes y hooks\n- Estados loading/error\n- Render del Header, InfoCliente, ItemsList, Total, acciones\n- Llamadas a modales (CancelModal, AddItemModal)\n- `handleConfirmar` y `handleCancelar` se quedan (usan mutations)\n\n**Orden de extracci├│n recomendado**:\n1. Crear `hooks/useEdicionItems.ts` (mueve toda la l├│gica de edici├│n)\n2. Crear `components/InfoCliente.tsx`\n3. Crear `components/ItemsList.tsx` (incluye el stepper inline)\n4. Crear `components/CancelModal.tsx`\n5. Crear `components/AddItemModal.tsx`\n6. Crear `components/ConfirmarAccionButtons.tsx`\n7. Simplificar `PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx` a ~200 l├¡neas\n8. Borrar `console.log` de la l├¡nea 246 ­ƒº╣\n\n---\n\n### ­ƒö┤ H3: Tests de submit flows\n\n#### Para `PedidoDetalleScreen.test.tsx` ÔÇö agregar:\n\n```tsx\n// Mockear useQuitarItem, useAgregarItem, useActualizarCantidadItem\n// (adem├ís de los mocks existentes de usePedidoDetalle, useConfirmarEntrega, useCancelarPedido)\n\nit('should enter edit mode and show stepper', async () => { ... })\nit('should change item quantity via stepper', async () => { ... })\nit('should delete item in edit mode', async () => { ... })\nit('should open add item modal and add new item', async () => { ... })\nit('should call guardarCambios and execute mutations in order', async () => { ... })\nit('should show error toast when guardarCambios fails', async () => { ... })\n```\n\n#### Para `PedidoAltaScreen.test.tsx` ÔÇö agregar:\n\n```tsx\nit('should show validation error when no cliente selected', async () => { ... })\nit('should show validation error when no items selected', async () => { ... })\nit('should call crearPedido with correct payload on submit', async () => { ... })\nit('should show error toast when crearPedido fails', async () => { ... })\nit('should select reparto via modal', async () => { ... })\nit('should toggle item selection and show total estimado', async () => { ... })\n```\n\n**Mock necesario adicional**: para `PedidoDetalleScreen.test.tsx` hay que mockear `useQuitarItem`, `useAgregarItem`, `useActualizarCantidadItem` y el `useQueryClient`.\n\n---\n\n### Resumen de archivos a modificar/crear\n\n| Acci├│n | Archivo | L├¡neas |\n|--------|---------|--------|\n| **CREAR** | `features/pedidos/hooks/useEdicionItems.ts` | ~80 |\n| **CREAR** | `features/pedidos/components/InfoCliente.tsx` | ~50 |\n| **CREAR** | `features/pedidos/components/ItemsList.tsx` | ~100 |\n| **CREAR** | `features/pedidos/components/CancelModal.tsx` | ~60 |\n| **CREAR** | `features/pedidos/components/AddItemModal.tsx` | ~100 |\n| **CREAR** | `features/pedidos/components/ConfirmarAccionButtons.tsx` | ~30 |\n| **MODIFICAR** | `features/pedidos/screens/PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx` | 982 ÔåÆ ~200 |\n| **MODIFICAR** | `features/pedidos/screens/__tests__/PedidoDetalleScreen.test.tsx` | 111 ÔåÆ ~180 |\n| **MODIFICAR** | `features/pedidos/screens/__tests__/PedidoAltaScreen.test.tsx` | 102 ÔåÆ ~180 |\n\n---\n\n┬┐Quer├®s que ejecute este plan con el agente mobile? Antes de arrancar, dos preguntas:\n\n1. **┬┐Prefer├¡s el refactor completo (H1 + H3) de una sola vez, o primero el refactor y despu├®s los tests?**\n2. **┬┐El agente mobile puede crear archivos nuevos?** Porque vamos a crear 6 subcomponentes + 1 hook nuevos.

Create `features/pedidos/components/InfoCliente.tsx`\n2. Create `features/pedidos/components/ItemsList.tsx` \n3. Create `features/pedidos/components/CancelModal.tsx`\n4. Create `features/pedidos/components/AddItemModal.tsx`\n5. Create `features/pedidos/components/ConfirmarAccionButtons.tsx`\n6. Create `features/pedidos/hooks/useEdicionItems.ts`\n7. Rewrite `features/pedidos/screens/PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx` to use all these\n8. Update `features/pedidos/screens/__tests__/PedidoDetalleScreen.test.tsx`\n9. Update `features/pedidos/screens/__tests__/PedidoAltaScreen.test.tsx`\n10. Run tests afterward\n\nWait, let me reconsider. The `useEdicionItems` hook is tricky because the editing state is local to the screen, and the mutations (quitarItem, agregarItem, actualizarCantidadItem) might be harder to abstract.\n\nActually, looking at this more carefully, I think the best approach for the hook is to extract the editing logic (entrarEdicion, cancelarEdicion, handleCambiarCantidad, handleEliminarItem, abrirModalAgregar, guardarCambios, handleConfirmGuardar, itemsEdit, setItemsEdit, modoEdicion, setModoEdicion, guardando, setGuardando) and keep the mutations (quitarItemMutation, agregarItemMutation, actualizarCantidadMutation) in the screen.\n\nWait, but `useQuitarItem`, `useAgregarItem`, `useActualizarCantidadItem` are already hooks. The editing hook would need to accept the pedido, id, and the mutation functions.\n\nLet me think about this differently. The hook would be:\n\n```tsx\n// useEdicionItems.ts\nexport function useEdicionItems(\n  pedido: Pedido | undefined,\n  pedidoId: string,\n  queryClient: ReturnType<typeof useQueryClient>,\n  mutations: {\n    quitarItem: ReturnType<typeof useQuitarItem>,\n    agregarItem: ReturnType<typeof useAgregarItem>,\n    actualizarCantidad: ReturnType<typeof useActualizarCantidadItem>,\n  },\n  showToast: (message: string, type: 'success' | 'error' | 'warning') => void,\n) {\n  // ... all editing state and logic\n  return {\n    modoEdicion, setModoEdicion,\n    itemsEdit, setItemsEdit,\n    guardando,\n    entrarEdicion, cancelarEdicion,\n    handleCambiarCantidad, handleEliminarItem,\n    guardarCambios, handleConfirmGuardar,\n    // Modal states\n    showAddModal, setShowAddModal,\n    itemsDisponibles, setItemsDisponibles,\n    nuevoItem, setNuevoItem,\n    nuevaCantidad, setNuevaCantidad,\n    showConfirmModal, setShowConfirmModal,\n  };\n}\n```\n\nHmm, that's a lot of return values. This is getting complex. Let me simplify.\n\nActually, looking at this more carefully, I think the cleanest approach is:\n\n1. Create the visual subcomponents (stateless or with minimal state)\n2. Keep the editing logic in the screen but extract the most complex parts\n3. The screen remains the \"smart\" component orchestration layer\n\nLet me redesign the plan:\n\n**Subcomponents** (all in `features/pedidos/components/`):\n\n1. **`EstadoBadge.tsx`** (~30 lines) - Shows estado + Pedido #orden\n   - Props: `estado: EstadoPedido, orden: number, theme: ...`\n\n2. **`InfoCliente.tsx`** (~60 lines) - Client info card with clickable map/phone\n   - Props: `cliente: Pedido['cliente'], theme: ...`\n   - Handles: `handleOpenMaps`, `handleCall` internally\n\n3. **`ItemsList.tsx`** (~120 lines) - Item list with edit mode, stepper, delete\n   - Props: `items: PedidoItem[], modoEdicion: boolean, theme: ..., onCambiarCantidad?: (id, delta) => void, onEliminarItem?: (id) => void, onAgregarItem?: () => void, mostrarBotonEditar?: boolean, onEntrarEdicion?: () => void`\n\n4. **`CancelModal.tsx`** (~60 lines) - Modal with motivo selection\n   - Props: `visible: boolean, onClose: () => void, onCancelar: (motivo) => void, theme: ...`\n\n5. **`AddItemModal.tsx`** (~100 lines) - Modal with item selector + quantity\n   - Props: `visible: boolean, onClose: () => void, itemsDisponibles: Item[], onAgregar: (item, cantidad) => void, theme: ...`\n\n6. **`TotalCard.tsx`** (~20 lines) - Shows total\n   - Props: `total: number, theme: ...`\n\n7. **`MotivoFallaCard.tsx`** (~20 lines) - Shows motivo de no entrega\n   - Props: `motivo: string, theme: ...`\n\nThen reduce `PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx` to ~200 lines:\n- Imports all subcomponents\n- State declarations\n- Handlers (handleConfirmar, handleCancelar, etc.)\n- JSX with subcomponents instead of inline code\n- Modals use the new components\n\nLet me create the task now. I'll provide very detailed instructions.\n\nActually, this is going to be a very long task description. Let me make it comprehensive but structured.

### Mensaje:
## Goal\nComplete offline support, fix item mutations end-to-end, migrate fully to real backend (remove all mock fallbacks), polish dark mode UI, and verify all flows against the real backend.\n\n## Constraints & Preferences\n- Avoid native dependencies where possible (SecureStore fallback to AsyncStorage for web).\n- Cross-environment compatibility: `gap` replaced with explicit margin, AsyncStorage over localStorage.\n- No mock data in production code paths ÔÇö only real backend endpoints.\n- Agent must not push commits ÔÇö only file changes + run tests.\n\n## Progress\n### Done\n- **401 ÔåÆ logout flow verified**: `api.ts` response interceptor catches 401 ÔåÆ `clearToken()` ÔåÆ `logout()` ÔåÆ `router.replace('/login')`. Auth gate bidirectional: `login.tsx` redirects to `/` if authenticated, `(tabs)/_layout.tsx` redirects to `/login` if not.\n- **Empty mocks directories removed**: `features/pedidos/mocks/` and `features/repartos/mocks/` no longer exist.\n- **Final end-to-end verification**: Backend `GET /health` ÔåÆ `{\"status\":\"ok\"}`, `POST /auth/login` ÔåÆ 200 with JWT, `GET /auth/me` ÔåÆ 200 with user, `GET /clientes` ÔåÆ 6 clientes, `GET /pedidos?estado=PENDIENTE` ÔåÆ 6 pedidos with items. All working.\n- **`EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL` verified**: Created `mobile/.env` with `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:3000/api/v1` and other defaults. Two fallback usages (`api.ts` and `_layout.tsx`) match the correct backend URL.\n- **Hardcoded mock URLs**: Zero `from '...mock...'` imports in production code (`app/`, `features/`, `services/`, `stores/`, `components/`, `constants/`, `hooks/`, `lib/`). All mock references are in test files only.\n- **Back navigation fixed**: `<Header onBack={...}>` now uses `onBack` prop in `PedidoDetalleScreen`. Each route (`pedidos/[id]`, `repartos/[id]`, `mapa/[id]`) passes `router.replace('/pedidos')` / `/repartos` / `/mapa` so back always goes to the parent list, never to the previous tab.\n- **PedidoDetalleScreen refactored**: 982 lines ÔåÆ 195 lines (-80%). Created 8 subcomponents in `features/pedidos/components/`.\n- **Submit flow tests added**: 5 new tests for `PedidoDetalleScreen` (edit mode, stepper, delete item, add modal, guardarCambios), 4 new tests for `PedidoAltaScreen` (validation, toggle items, submit, error toast). All pass.\n- **console.log removed** from line 246 of PedidoDetalleScreen.\n- **­ƒùæ´©Å emoji replaced** with text \"Eliminar\" in edit mode.\n\n### In Progress\n- *(none)*\n\n### Blocked\n- *(none)*\n\n## Key Decisions\n- `router.back()` replaced with `router.replace('/pedidos')` (and `/repartos`, `/mapa`) for header back button ÔÇö ensures predictable navigation regardless of stack history. Post-action callbacks (confirmar/cancelar) keep `router.back()`.\n- `onBack?: () => void` prop added to `PedidoDetalleScreen` interface. Fallback to `() => router.back()` when prop is undefined.\n- Return types in `pedidoService.ts` are intentionally inconsistent to match backend: endpoints wrapped in `{ data }` use `response.data.data`, raw endpoints use `response.data`. This is correct ÔÇö inconsistency is in the backend, not the mobile client.\n- 8 subcomponents extracted from `PedidoDetalleScreen` to keep each file under 200 lines. Editing logic kept in the screen (not extracted to a hook) to avoid complex parameter passing.\n- Self-contained `getEstadoColor`, `getEstadoLabel`, `formatPrecio`, `formatFecha` functions kept inside each subcomponent instead of shared ÔÇö avoids circular imports.\n\n## Next Steps\n1. Explore what's left in other feature sections (Repartos, Mapa, Perfil, Inicio) ÔÇö or move to a new milestone.\n\n## Critical Context\n- Backend Docker containers all running: `supplycycle_backend_dev` (port 3000), `supplycycle_db_dev` (port 5432), `supplycycle_pgadmin_dev` (port 5050).\n- Health: `GET /health` ÔåÆ `{\"status\":\"ok\"}`. All endpoints verified with real JWT auth.\n- Backend `.env`: JWT_SECRET=`supplycycle-dev-secret-key-2026`, JWT_EXPIRES_IN=`24h`, DB URL `postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/supplycycle`.\n- Seed credentials: `repartidor@supplycycle.com` / `Repartidor123`.\n- Tests: 131 pass, 8 pre-existing failures (`@expo/vector-icons` JSX parsing). No regressions after all changes.\n- `.gitignore` only ignores `.env*.local` ÔÇö `mobile/.env` would be staged on `git add .`. Recommend adding `.env` to `.gitignore`.\n\n## Relevant Files\n- `features/pedidos/screens/PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx`: Refactored from 982ÔåÆ195 lines, uses 8 subcomponents.\n- `features/pedidos/components/EstadoBadge.tsx`: Estado badge with colored pill + \"Pedido #{orden}\".\n- `features/pedidos/components/InfoCliente.tsx`: Client info card with tappable map/phone.\n- `features/pedidos/components/ItemsList.tsx`: Item list with edit mode, stepper, delete, add.\n- `features/pedidos/components/CancelModal.tsx`: Bottom-sheet modal for motivo selection.\n- `features/pedidos/components/AddItemModal.tsx`: Two-step modal (select item ÔåÆ set quantity).\n- `features/pedidos/components/TotalCard.tsx`: Total formatted display.\n- `features/pedidos/components/MotivoFallaCard.tsx`: Motivo de no entrega display.\n- `features/pedidos/components/ConfirmarAccionButtons.tsx`: Two action buttons (confirmar/cancelar).\n- `app/(tabs)/pedidos/[id].tsx`: Updated with `onBack={() => router.replace('/pedidos')}`.\n- `app/(tabs)/repartos/[id].tsx`: Updated with `onBack={() => router.replace('/repartos')}`.\n- `app/(tabs)/mapa/[id].tsx`: Updated with `onBack={() => router.replace('/mapa')}`.\n- `mobile/.env`: Created with `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:3000/api/v1` and defaults.\n- `features/pedidos/screens/__tests__/PedidoDetalleScreen.test.tsx`: Added 5 edit mode + submit tests.\n- `features/pedidos/screens/__tests__/PedidoAltaScreen.test.tsx`: Added 4 validation + submit tests.

Auth gate bidirectional: `login.tsx` redirects to `/` if authenticated, `(tabs)/_layout.tsx` redirects to `/login` if not.\n- **Empty mocks directories removed**: `features/pedidos/mocks/` and `features/repartos/mocks/` no longer exist.\n- **Final end-to-end verification**: Backend `GET /health` ÔåÆ `{\"status\":\"ok\"}`, `POST /auth/login` ÔåÆ 200 with JWT, `GET /auth/me` ÔåÆ 200 with user, `GET /clientes` ÔåÆ 6 clientes, `GET /pedidos?estado=PENDIENTE` ÔåÆ 6 pedidos with items. All mock references are in test files only.\n- **Back navigation fixed**: `<Header onBack={...}>` now uses `onBack` prop in `PedidoDetalleScreen`. Each route (`pedidos/[id]`, `repartos/[id]`, `mapa/[id]`) passes `router.replace('/pedidos')` / `/repartos` / `/mapa` so back always goes to the parent list, never to the previous tab.\n- **PedidoDetalleScreen refactored**: 982 lines ÔåÆ 195 lines (-80%). Created 8 subcomponents in `features/pedidos/components/`.\n- **Submit flow tests added**: 5 new tests for `PedidoDetalleScreen` (edit mode, stepper, delete item, add modal, guardarCambios), 4 new tests for `PedidoAltaScreen` (validation, toggle items, submit, error toast). All endpoints verified with real JWT auth.\n- Backend `.env`: JWT_SECRET=`supplycycle-dev-secret-key-2026`, JWT_EXPIRES_IN=`24h`, DB URL `postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/supplycycle`.\n- Seed credentials: `repartidor@supplycycle.com` / `Repartidor123`.\n- Tests: 131 pass, 8 pre-existing failures (`@expo/vector-icons` JSX parsing). No regressions after all changes.\n- `.gitignore` only ignores `.env*.local` ÔÇö `mobile/.env` would be staged on `git add .`. Recommend adding `.env` to `.gitignore`.\n\n## Relevant Files\n- `features/pedidos/screens/PedidoDetalleScreen.tsx`: Refactored from 982ÔåÆ195 lines, uses 8 subcomponents.\n- `features/pedidos/components/EstadoBadge.tsx`: Estado badge with colored pill + \"Pedido #{orden}\".\n- `features/pedidos/components/InfoCliente.tsx`: Client info card with tappable map/phone.\n- `features/pedidos/components/ItemsList.tsx`: Item list with edit mode, stepper, delete, add.\n- `features/pedidos/components/CancelModal.tsx`: Bottom-sheet modal for motivo selection.\n- `features/pedidos/components/AddItemModal.tsx`: Two-step modal (select item ÔåÆ set quantity).\n- `features/pedidos/components/TotalCard.tsx`: Total formatted display.\n- `features/pedidos/components/MotivoFallaCard.tsx`: Motivo de no entrega display.\n- `features/pedidos/components/ConfirmarAccionButtons.tsx`: Two action buttons (confirmar/cancelar).\n- `app/(tabs)/pedidos/[id].tsx`: Updated with `onBack={() => router.replace('/pedidos')}`.\n- `app/(tabs)/repartos/[id].tsx`: Updated with `onBack={() => router.replace('/repartos')}`.\n- `app/(tabs)/mapa/[id].tsx`: Updated with `onBack={() => router.replace('/mapa')}`.\n- `mobile/.env`: Created with `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:3000/api/v1` and defaults.\n- `features/pedidos/screens/__tests__/PedidoDetalleScreen.test.tsx`: Added 5 edit mode + submit tests.\n- `features/pedidos/screens/__tests__/PedidoAltaScreen.test.tsx`: Added 4 validation + submit tests.

### Mensaje:
Here's a comprehensive assessment of the current state of the codebase and what's left to do:\n\n---\n\n## Current State Assessment\n\n### Ô£à Completed\n- **Auth flow**: Login, JWT storage, auth gate, 401 ÔåÆ logout, mock credentials\n- **Pedidos feature**: Full CRUD with items, subcomponents, refactored detalle screen (195 lines)\n- **Repartos feature**: Hook, service, list/inicio screens, filters, progress bar\n- **Tests**: 131 passing across all features (8 suites fail due to `@expo/vector-icons` JSX parsing ÔÇö pre-existing infra issue)\n- **Backend integration**: All production code uses real API (zero mock references)\n- **Navigation**: Back buttons go to parent list, not previous tab\n\n### ÔÜá´©Å Gaps & Remaining Issues\n\n| # | Issue | File(s) | Priority |\n|---|-------|---------|----------|\n| 1 | **ConnectivityBanner hardcoded `isConnected={true}`** | `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx` | High |\n| 2 | **`app/_layout.tsx` uses `fetch` instead of Axios** for auth bootstrap | `app/_layout.tsx` | High |\n| 3 | **Hardcoded colors breaking dark mode** (4 locations) | `PerfilScreen.tsx`, `PedidosListScreen.tsx`, `PedidoAltaScreen.tsx`, `MapaScreen.tsx` | Medium |\n| 4 | **PerfilScreen `setTimeout` for logout redirect** ÔÇö race condition | `PerfilScreen.tsx` | Medium |\n| 5 | **MapaScreen has placeholder, no real map** | `MapaScreen.tsx` | Low (post-MVP) |\n| 6 | **`@expo/vector-icons` JSX parsing in tests** (8 suites fail) | Test infra | Low |\n\n### ­ƒù║´©Å Feature Completeness\n\n| Feature | Screens | Hooks | Services | Components | Tests |\n|---------|---------|-------|----------|------------|-------|\n| **Auth** | Ô£à Login | Ô£à useLogin | Ô£à authStorage, authService | ÔÇö | Ô£à |\n| **Pedidos** | Ô£à List, Detail, Alta | Ô£à usePedidos | Ô£à pedidoService | Ô£à 8 subcomponents | Ô£à |\n| **Repartos** | Ô£à Inicio, List | Ô£à useReparto | Ô£à repartoService | ÔÇö | Ô£à |\n| **Mapa** | Ô£à (placeholder) | ÔØî (uses pedidos hook) | ÔØî | ÔÇö | Ô£à |\n| **Perfil** | Ô£à (basic) | ÔØî (uses authStore) | ÔØî | ÔÇö | Ô£à |\n\n---\n\n## Proposed Next Steps\n\nI'd recommend tackling them in priority order:\n\n1. **Wire up ConnectivityBanner ÔåÆ `useNetworkStatus`** ÔÇö replace `isConnected={true}` with actual NetInfo\n2. **Replace `fetch` with Axios in `app/_layout.tsx`** ÔÇö for consistency with `api.ts`\n3. **Fix all hardcoded colors** ÔÇö use theme colors for dark mode compatibility\n4. **Fix PerfilScreen logout race condition** ÔÇö remove `setTimeout`\n5. **Mop up**: remaining UI polish, verify all flows\n\nShall I proceed with these improvements, or would you like to prioritize differently?

'#FFFFFF' : theme.text`\n   - `PedidoAltaScreen.tsx` line 549: `color: '#FFFFFF'`\n   - `MapaScreen.tsx` line 244: `color: '#FFFFFF'`\n   - `PerfilScreen.tsx` line 121: `color: '#FFFFFF'`\n   - `PedidosListScreen.tsx` line 223: `color: '#FFFFFF'`\n\n2. **PerfilScreen `setTimeout` for logout redirect** ÔÇö race condition\n\nLet me also check if there are any other hardcoded colors I might have missed, and look at the specific files more carefully to understand the full picture.\n\nLet me read the relevant files to understand the issues better.

### Mensaje:
Good, I found another hardcoded color in ItemsList.tsx (line 201: `color: '#94A3B8'`). This is the `emptyText` style for the items list. In the theme, `muted` is `'#94A3B8'` for light mode and `'#64748B'` for dark mode. So this hardcoded color matches only the light mode, breaking dark mode.\n\nNow I have a complete picture. Let me compile the comprehensive plan.\n\n### All hardcoded colors found:\n\n1. **PerfilScreen.tsx** (2 locations):\n   - Line 121: `color: '#FFFFFF'` ÔÇö avatar text (should be `theme.headerText` or just reference theme)\n   - Line 138: `backgroundColor: '#E2E8F0'` ÔÇö divider (should be `theme.border` which is `#E2E8F0` in light, `#334155` in dark)\n\n2. **PedidosListScreen.tsx** (2 locations):\n   - Line 117: `color: '#FFFFFF'` ÔÇö active filter button text (should be `theme.headerText` or theme-compatible)\n   - Line 223: `color: '#FFFFFF'` ÔÇö add button text (should be `theme.headerText`)\n\n3. **PedidoAltaScreen.tsx** (2 locations):\n   - Line 549: `color: '#FFFFFF'` ÔÇö add item button text (should be `theme.headerText`)\n   - Line 566: `borderTopColor: '#e0e0e0'` ÔÇö total container top border (should be `theme.border`)\n\n4. **MapaScreen.tsx** (2 locations):\n   - Line 190: `borderColor: '#0a7ea4'` ÔÇö selected marker border (hardcoded tint; should use `theme.tint`)\n   - Line 244: `color: '#FFFFFF'` ÔÇö ver detalle button text (should be `theme.headerText`)\n\n5. **ItemsList.tsx** (1 location):\n   - Line 201: `color: '#94A3B8'` ÔÇö empty state text (should use `theme.muted`)\n\n### PerfilScreen setTimeout issue:\n- Lines 27-30: Uses `setTimeout(() => router.replace('/login'), 0)` after clearing auth state. This was needed because React hadn't processed the state change yet. But with Zustand, state changes are synchronous and the `(tabs)/_layout.tsx` redirect check (`if (!isAuthenticated) return <Redirect href=\"/login\" />`) should work immediately. The `setTimeout` is an unnecessary workaround.\n\nWait, actually there's a subtle issue here. When `logout()` is called in the PerfilScreen (which is inside `(tabs)/_layout.tsx`), the `isAuthenticated` becomes false, which triggers the `<Redirect href=\"/login\" />` in the tabs layout. So the `router.replace('/login')` in the PerfilScreen is redundant ÔÇö the auth gate already handles it. The `setTimeout` was added to prevent a potential race condition but it's unnecessary.\n\nActually, looking more carefully - the `setTimeout` approach is actually fine for this use case. The real question is whether removing it creates issues. Let me think...\n\nWhen `logout()` is called:\n1. Zustand state updates synchronously: `isAuthenticated` becomes `false`\n2. React re-renders\n3. `(tabs)/_layout.tsx` sees `isAuthenticated === false` ÔåÆ returns `<Redirect href=\"/login\" />`\n4. The PerfilScreen gets unmounted, so `setTimeout` callback never runs\n\nSo the `setTimeout` is harmless but unnecessary. The redirect through the auth gate already works. However, there's a subtle issue: the `<Redirect>` in Expo Router might not handle the redirect the same way as `router.replace('/login')`. The `router.replace('/login')` is the more reliable approach.\n\nActually, the safest approach is to keep the `router.replace('/login')` but remove the `setTimeout` wrapper, since:\n- Zustand state changes are synchronous\n- The redirect in the tabs layout is a safety net, not the primary mechanism\n- Using `router.replace` directly is cleaner and more predictable\n\nLet me now build the complete plan.

### Mensaje:
---\n\n## Plan: Correcciones de Prioridad Media\n\nBasado en el an├ílisis, encontr├® **9 hardcoded hex colors** + **1 setTimeout race condition** repartidos en 5 archivos. Aqu├¡ est├í el plan detallado:\n\n---\n\n### ­ƒöº 1. Hardcoded Colors ÔåÆ Reemplazar con colores del theme\n\nCada uno de estos usa un valor literal `#XXXXXX` que funciona solo en **light mode**. En **dark mode** se ven incorrectos. La soluci├│n: usar la variable `theme` correspondiente de `Colors` (ya importada en todos los archivos).\n\n| # | Archivo | L├¡nea | Actual | Reemplazar con | Raz├│n |\n|---|---------|-------|--------|----------------|-------|\n| A | `PerfilScreen.tsx` | 121 | `color: '#FFFFFF'` | `color: theme.background` | Avatar text sobre `buttonPrimary` (blue). En dark mode `buttonPrimary` es `#2563eb`, fondo oscuro ÔåÆ el blanco est├í bien, pero debe ser din├ímico. Mejor usamos `color: theme.headerText` (blanco en light, casi blanco en dark) |\n| B | `PerfilScreen.tsx` | 138 | `backgroundColor: '#E2E8F0'` | `backgroundColor: theme.border` | `theme.border` = `#E2E8F0` en light, `#334155` en dark |\n| C | `PedidosListScreen.tsx` | 117 | `color: '#FFFFFF'` | `color: theme.headerText` | Texto sobre filtro activo (color din├ímico) |\n| D | `PedidosListScreen.tsx` | 223 | `color: '#FFFFFF'` | `color: theme.headerText` | Bot├│n \"+\" flotante |\n| E | `PedidoAltaScreen.tsx` | 549 | `color: '#FFFFFF'` | `color: theme.headerText` | Texto bot├│n agregar item |\n| F | `PedidoAltaScreen.tsx` | 566 | `borderTopColor: '#e0e0e0'` | `borderTopColor: theme.border` | L├¡nea divisoria total |\n| G | `MapaScreen.tsx` | 190 | `borderColor: '#0a7ea4'` | `borderColor: theme.tint` | Borde marker seleccionado |\n| H | `MapaScreen.tsx` | 244 | `color: '#FFFFFF'` | `color: theme.headerText` | Texto bot├│n \"Ver detalle\" |\n| I | `ItemsList.tsx` | 201 | `color: '#94A3B8'` | `color: theme.muted` | Texto lista vac├¡a |\n\n**┬┐Por qu├® `theme.headerText` y no `'#FFFFFF'`?**  \n`headerText` en light es `#FFFFFF`, en dark es `#F8FAFC` ÔÇö mantiene contraste sobre fondos primarios/coloreados. Es la variable sem├íntica correcta para texto sobre botones primarios.\n\n**Problema especial:** Los estilos en `StyleSheet.create()` son est├íticos y no pueden acceder a `theme`. Las l├¡neas (C, D, E, G, H, I) ya usan estilo inline via `style={[styles.xxx, { color: theme.xxx }]}` ÔåÆ simplemente cambiar el valor fijo por `theme.xxx` en el objeto inline. Las l├¡neas (A, B, F) est├ín en el objeto `StyleSheet.create()` est├ítico ÔÇö necesitan moverse a estilo inline.\n\n---\n\n### ­ƒöº 2. PerfilScreen ÔÇö Eliminar `setTimeout` en `handleLogout`\n\n**Problema:**  \n```ts\nsetTimeout(() => {\n  router.replace('/login');\n}, 0);\n```\nEl `setTimeout` es un workaround que asume que React no ha procesado el cambio de estado de Zustand. Pero Zustand es **s├¡ncrono** ÔÇö cuando se llama `logout()`, el estado cambia inmediatamente. El `<Redirect href=\"/login\" />` en `(tabs)/_layout.tsx` ya redirige autom├íticamente al detectar `isAuthenticated === false`.\n\n**Soluci├│n:**  \nEliminar el `setTimeout` y dejar solo `router.replace('/login')`. El auth gate en el layout es el mecanismo principal de redirecci├│n; `router.replace` es el mecanismo expl├¡cito por si el layout no alcanza a re-renderizar.\n\n```ts\nconst handleLogout = async () => {\n  logout();                                // 1. Limpia store (s├¡ncrono)\n  try { await clearToken(); } catch (e) {  // 2. Limpia SecureStore\n    console.warn('Error al limpiar token:', e);\n  }\n  router.replace('/login');                // 3. Redirige (sin setTimeout)\n};\n```\n\n---\n\n### ­ƒôï Resumen de cambios por archivo\n\n| Archivo | Cambios |\n|---------|---------|\n| `features/perfil/screens/PerfilScreen.tsx` | L121: `#FFFFFF` ÔåÆ `theme.headerText` (inline). ### Mensaje:
Todas las correcciones de prioridad media est├ín implementadas. Resumen final:\n\n### Ô£à Cambios realizados (10 en 5 archivos)\n\n| Archivo | Cambios |\n|---------|---------|\n| **`PerfilScreen.tsx`** | Divider `#E2E8F0` ÔåÆ `theme.border` ┬À Avatar text `#FFFFFF` ÔåÆ `theme.headerText` ┬À Eliminado `setTimeout` del logout |\n| **`PedidosListScreen.tsx`** | Filtro activo `#FFFFFF` ÔåÆ `theme.headerText` ┬À Bot├│n \"+\" `#FFFFFF` ÔåÆ `theme.headerText` |\n| **`PedidoAltaScreen.tsx`** | Btn agregar `#FFFFFF` ÔåÆ `theme.headerText` ┬À Total border `#e0e0e0` ÔåÆ `theme.border` |\n| **`MapaScreen.tsx`** | Marker selected `#0a7ea4` ÔåÆ `theme.tint` ┬À Btn detalle `#FFFFFF` ÔåÆ `theme.headerText` |\n| **`ItemsList.tsx`** | Empty text `#94A3B8` ÔåÆ `theme.muted` |\n\n### ­ƒö¼ Tests\n- 131 tests siguen pasando\n- 0 regresiones\n- Las 8 suites \"failed\" son el error pre-existente de `@expo/vector-icons` (no tiene que ver con estos cambios)

### Mensaje:
que significa cuando dice arriba \"hay cambios pendientes de sincronizar\"?

### Mensaje:
The user is asking about the \"Hay cambios pendientes de sincronizar\" message that appears in the ConnectivityBanner. Let me explain what this means in the context of the offline support system.\n\nThe ConnectivityBanner is used in `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx` and displays this message when:\n1. `isConnected === false` ÔåÆ \"Sin conexi├│n ÔÇö los datos pueden no estar actualizados\"\n2. `isConnected === true && hasPendingSync === true` ÔåÆ \"Hay cambios pendientes de sincronizar\"\n\nThe `hasPendingSync` comes from the `offlineStore` - it checks if `queue.length > 0`.\n\nSo the message means there are offline mutations (confirmar entrega, cancelar pedido, etc.) that were made while offline and are queued in AsyncStorage waiting to be synced to the backend when connectivity is restored.\n\nBut this is read-only mode, so I can't make edits. The user is just asking for an explanation.

### Mensaje:
Ese mensaje pertenece al **sistema offline** definido en `mobile/rules/offline-patterns.md` y `mobile/stores/offlineStore.ts`. Significa:\n\n### Flujo completo\n\n1. **El repartidor est├í offline** (sin conexi├│n al backend)\n2. **Realiza una acci├│n** como confirmar entrega, cancelar pedido, agregar/quitar items\n3. Esa mutaci├│n **se encola localmente** en `offlineStore.queue[]` en lugar de enviarse al backend\n4. El `ConnectivityBanner` detecta que la cola no est├í vac├¡a (`queue.length > 0`) y muestra:  \n   **\"Hay cambios pendientes de sincronizar\"**\n5. Cuando **la conexi├│n se restaura**, el hook `useOfflineSync` (en `app/_layout.tsx`) procesa la cola FIFO: env├¡a cada mutaci├│n al backend y la remueve de la cola\n6. Una vez vac├¡a la cola, el banner desaparece\n\n### ┬┐Por qu├® aparece ahora?\n\nEn `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx` l├¡nea 16:\n```ts\nconst hasPendingSync = useOfflineStore((state) => state.queue.length > 0);\n```\n\nY el `ConnectivityBanner` lo muestra cuando `isConnected === true && hasPendingSync === true`.\n\nActualmente `isConnected` est├í **hardcodeado en `true`** (l├¡nea 24 del layout), as├¡ que el banner solo se activar├¡a si algo agreg├│ mutaciones a la cola offline ÔÇö pero el c├│digo de mutaciones (confirmar/cancelar en `PedidoDetalleScreen`) no est├í usando la cola offline todav├¡a. **Es un feature que est├í dise├▒ado pero no completamente integrado** con las mutaciones reales.\n\n### ┬┐Quieres que lo integremos?\n\nSi quer├®s que las mutaciones (confirmar entrega, cancelar, agregar/quitar items) usen la cola offline cuando no hay conexi├│n, ese ser├¡a el siguiente paso l├│gico de prioridad alta junto con conectar `useNetworkStatus` real al `ConnectivityBanner` (actualmente hardcodeado `isConnected={true}`).

### Mensaje:
quiero que nunca aparezca, que lo cambios se hagas

### Mensaje:
The user wants the offline queue to never be used - they want mutations to always happen directly (no queue, no \"pending sync\" banner). Let me plan what needs to change:\n\n1. **Remove the offline queue logic** from `offlineStore.ts` - or keep the store but make it inert\n2. **Remove `useOfflineSync`** from `app/_layout.tsx` or make it a no-op\n3. **Remove `ConnectivityBanner`** from `(tabs)/_layout.tsx` or hide it permanently\n4. **Remove `hasPendingSync`** logic from `(tabs)/_layout.tsx`\n\nWait, but the user might also want the connectivity banner to simply never show \"hay cambios pendientes de sincronizar\". They might still want the \"Sin conexi├│n ÔÇö los datos pueden no estar actualizados\" banner. Let me re-read the user's message.\n\n\"quiero que nunca aparezca, que lo cambios se hagas\" - \"I want it to never appear, that the changes are made [directly]\"\n\nSo they want:\n1. No offline queue - mutations happen directly always\n2. No \"pending sync\" banner\n\nBut should we keep the connectivity detection (isConnected) for showing the \"Sin conexi├│n\" warning? The user said \"nunca aparezca\" which seems to mean the entire offline system should go away.\n\nLet me think about what changes are needed:\n\n1. **`offlineStore.ts`** - Can be removed entirely. The `useOfflineStore` is used in:\n   - `(tabs)/_layout.tsx` - for `hasPendingSync`\n   - `useOfflineSync.ts` - for processing the queue\n   \n2. **`useOfflineSync.ts`** - Can be removed. Used in `app/_layout.tsx`.\n\n3. **`(tabs)/_layout.tsx`** - Remove `useOfflineStore`, remove `hasPendingSync`, remove `ConnectivityBanner`. Or keep ConnectivityBanner but only for connection status, not sync pending.\n\n4. **`app/_layout.tsx`** - Remove `useOfflineSync` import and call.\n\nWait, actually the ConnectivityBanner has two states:\n- `!isConnected` ÔåÆ \"Sin conexi├│n ÔÇö los datos pueden no estar actualizados\"\n- `isConnected && hasPendingSync` ÔåÆ \"Hay cambios pendientes de sincronizar\"\n\nThe user wants the second one to never appear. Options:\nA. Remove ConnectivityBanner entirely\nB. Keep ConnectivityBanner but always pass `hasPendingSync={false}`\nC. I'm in plan mode, so I need to present a plan.\n\nLet me also check what references exist to these files.

### Mensaje:
Now I understand the full picture. The offline queue system is integrated into:\n\n1. **`usePedidos.ts`** ÔÇö Every mutation (confirmar, cancelar, agregar item, quitar item, actualizar cantidad) wraps the API call in a try/catch. If the API call fails (offline), it **encola la mutaci├│n** y devuelve una **respuesta optimista** local. The UI cree que funcion├│, pero en realidad est├í en la cola.\n\n2. **`useOfflineSync.ts`** ÔÇö Procesa la cola peri├│dicamente cuando hay conexi├│n.\n\n3. **`offlineStore.ts`** ÔÇö Almacena las mutaciones pendientes.\n\n4. **`(tabs)/_layout.tsx`** ÔÇö Muestra el banner cuando hay cola.\n\nThe user wants:\n- \"que nunca aparezca\" ÔåÆ que el banner nunca se muestre\n- \"que los cambios se hagan\" ÔåÆ que las mutaciones vayan directo al backend\n\nSo the plan is:\n1. **Remove the try/catch offline fallback** from all mutations in `usePedidos.ts` ÔÇö let the error propagate naturally\n2. **Remove `useOfflineSync`** from `app/_layout.tsx`\n3. **Remove `ConnectivityBanner`** from `(tabs)/_layout.tsx` (or simplify it)\n4. **Remove `offlineStore.ts`** and `useOfflineSync.ts` (the whole offline system)\n5. **Remove/update tests** that test offline queuing behavior\n6. **Clean up `stores/index.ts`**\n\nLet me also check if ConnectivityBanner serves any other purpose. Looking at it:\n```ts\nif (isConnected && !hasPendingSync) return null;\n// Shows either:\n// \"Sin conexi├│n ÔÇö los datos pueden no estar actualizados\" (when !isConnected)\n// \"Hay cambios pendientes de sincronizar\" (when isConnected && hasPendingSync)\n```\n\nSince we're removing hasPendingSync entirely, the ConnectivityBanner would only show \"Sin conexi├│n\". The user said \"nunca aparezca\" - should we also remove the \"Sin conexi├│n\" banner? Let me think...\n\nThe user said \"quiero que nunca aparezca\" referring to the \"hay cambios pendientes de sincronizar\" message. But if we remove the entire offline system, there's no reason to keep ConnectivityBanner. However, the connectivity banner could still be useful for informing the user they're offline. But the user's instruction seems to be pretty clear - they don't want the offline system at all.\n\nActually, re-reading: \"quiero que nunca aparezca, que lo cambios se hagas\" - \"I want it to never appear, that the changes happen [directly]\". This means: remove the offline queue so changes always go directly to the backend. The ConnectivityBanner with \"Sin conexi├│n\" could still be useful to show when the user is offline. But the \"pending sync\" part needs to go away entirely.\n\nLet me present a clear plan.

### Mensaje:
Perfecto. Ya entend├¡ el alcance completo. Esto es lo que encontr├®:\n\n### Estado actual del sistema offline\n\nEl `offlineStore` est├í integrado en **5 mutaciones** de `usePedidos.ts`. Cada una envuelve la llamada API en un `try/catch`:\n\n```ts\n// usePedidos.ts (patr├│n que se repite 5 veces)\nmutationFn: async (pedidoId) => {\n  try {\n    return await confirmarEntregaRequest(pedidoId);\n  } catch {\n    addToQueue({ type: 'CONFIRMAR_ENTREGA', payload: { pedidoId } });\n    return { /* respuesta optimista */ };\n  }\n},\n```\n\n**Si la API falla**, en lugar de lanzar error, mete la operaci├│n en la cola offline y finge ├®xito. Eso genera el \"pendiente de sincronizar\".\n\n### Plan de limpieza\n\n| # | Archivo | Cambio |\n|---|---------|--------|\n| 1 | `features/pedidos/hooks/usePedidos.ts` | En las 5 mutaciones (`useConfirmarEntrega`, `useCancelarPedido`, `useAgregarItem`, `useQuitarItem`, `useActualizarCantidadItem`): **eliminar el `try/catch` + `addToQueue`**, dejar que el error se propague normalmente |\n| 2 | `hooks/useOfflineSync.ts` | Eliminar archivo completo (96 l├¡neas) |\n| 3 | `stores/offlineStore.ts` | Eliminar archivo completo (48 l├¡neas) |\n| 4 | `app/_layout.tsx` | Eliminar `import useOfflineSync` y `useOfflineSync()` |\n| 5 | `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx` | Eliminar `import { useOfflineStore }` y `hasPendingSync`. Simplificar `ConnectivityBanner` a solo `isConnected` |\n| 6 | `stores/index.ts` | Eliminar `export { useOfflineStore }` |\n| 7 | Tests (`usePedidos.test.ts`, `offlineStore.test.ts`) | Eliminar los 6 tests que prueban el encolado offline (no el archivo completo, solo los `it()` relevantes) |\n\nEl `ConnectivityBanner` se queda pero simplificado ÔÇö solo mostrar├í \"Sin conexi├│n ÔÇö los datos pueden no estar actualizados\" cuando realmente no haya conexi├│n. El mensaje \"Hay cambios pendientes de sincronizar\" desaparece para siempre.\n\n### Resumen de l├¡neas afectadas\n- **Archivos eliminados**: 2 (`offlineStore.ts`, `useOfflineSync.ts`)\n- **Archivos modificados**: 6 (usePedidos.ts, _layout.tsx x2, (tabs)/_layout.tsx, stores/index.ts, usePedidos.test.ts)\n- **Tests eliminados**: 6 it() blocks (los que prueban encolado offline)\n- **Tests que siguen**: los que prueban l├│gica normal de mutaciones seguir├ín pasando\n\n┬┐Procedo con la implementaci├│n?

Ah, ahora entiendo. El usuario quiere mantener el sistema offline (cola de sincronizaci├│n) pero **sin que aparezca el banner** \"Hay cambios pendientes de sincronizar\". Es decir:\n\n1. Ô£à Las mutaciones offline siguen funcionando (encolar cuando no hay conexi├│n)\n2. Ô£à El \"Sin conexi├│n ÔÇö los datos pueden no estar actualizados\" se queda\n3. ÔØî Eliminar solo el mensaje \"Hay cambios pendientes de sincronizar\"\n\nEntonces el cambio es mucho m├ís simple: solo modificar el `ConnectivityBanner` para que nunca muestre el mensaje de \"pendiente de sincronizar\". El resto del sistema offline sigue igual.\n\nOpciones:\nA. Quitar la prop `hasPendingSync` del ConnectivityBanner\nB. En `(tabs)/_layout.tsx`, pasar siempre `hasPendingSync={false}` \nC. En el ConnectivityBanner, eliminar la condici├│n `hasPendingSync` y que solo muestre el banner cuando `!isConnected`\n\nLa opci├│n m├ís limpia es C: simplificar el ConnectivityBanner para que solo muestre el estado de conexi├│n.

### Mensaje:
Ahora entiendo. Quer├®s mantener el sistema offline (cola de sincronizaci├│n funcionando) pero **eliminar solo el cartelito** \"Hay cambios pendientes de sincronizar\". El resto intacto.\n\n### Plan corregido ÔÇö 1 solo archivo\n\n| Cambio | Archivo | Detalle |\n|--------|---------|---------|\n| Simplificar `ConnectivityBanner` | `components/ui/connectivity-banner.tsx` | Eliminar la prop `hasPendingSync` y la l├│gica que muestra el mensaje. Solo mostrar banner cuando `!isConnected` con \"Sin conexi├│n ÔÇö los datos pueden no estar actualizados\" |\n\n**Antes:**\n```tsx\ninterface ConnectivityBannerProps {\n  isConnected: boolean;\n  hasPendingSync?: boolean;\n}\n\nexport default function ConnectivityBanner({ isConnected, hasPendingSync }: ConnectivityBannerProps) {\n  if (isConnected && !hasPendingSync) return null;\n  return (\n    <View style={[styles.banner, { backgroundColor: isConnected ? theme.warning : theme.error }]}>\n      <Text>\n        {!isConnected\n          ? 'Sin conexi├│n ÔÇö los datos pueden no estar actualizados'\n          : 'Hay cambios pendientes de sincronizar'}\n      </Text>\n    </View>\n  );\n}\n```\n\n**Despu├®s:**\n```tsx\ninterface ConnectivityBannerProps {\n  isConnected: boolean;\n}\n\nexport default function ConnectivityBanner({ isConnected }: ConnectivityBannerProps) {\n  if (isConnected) return null;\n  return (\n    <View style={[styles.banner, { backgroundColor: theme.error }]}>\n      <Text>Sin conexi├│n ÔÇö los datos pueden no estar actualizados</Text>\n    </View>\n  );\n}\n```\n\nY en `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx`:\n- Eliminar `import { useOfflineStore }` y `hasPendingSync`\n- Simplificar `<ConnectivityBanner isConnected={true} hasPendingSync={hasPendingSync}>` ÔåÆ `<ConnectivityBanner isConnected={true} />`\n\n**Impacto en tests:**\n- `connectivity-banner.test.tsx` ÔÇö actualizar para reflejar que solo hay un estado (offline)\n- El resto del sistema offline (`offlineStore`, `useOfflineSync`, `addToQueue` en `usePedidos.ts`) **no se toca**\n\n┬┐As├¡ est├í bien? ┬┐Procedo?

### Mensaje:
The .gitignore only ignores `.env*.local` but not `.env`. The `mobile/.env` file would be staged if someone runs `git add .`. I should recommend adding `.env` to the gitignore, but per the rules I shouldn't make config changes without asking. The user didn't ask me to commit this file, so it's fine - I'll just note it.\n\nLet me also check if the `session-ses_19f0.md` is gitignored.

### Mensaje:

Recomiendo agregar `.env` al `.gitignore`.
